definition_en: any device through which a user enters information into a computer
example_sentence: The principal input device among microcomputers is the keyboard.

definition_en: an input device used primarily with computer games
example_sentence: A joystick is an input device used primarily with computer games.

definition_en: one thousand; a unit measuring a computer's memory capacity
example_sentence: A computer whose memory has a capacity of 64K can store 64,000 bits of information.

definition_en: a portable computer small enough to be held comfortably on a user's lap
example_sentence: A laptop computer is a portable computer small enough to be held comfortably on a user's lap.

definition_en: a printer, containing an internal laser, that prints text in the same way a photocopier makes copies
example_sentence: A laser printer prints text in the same way a photocopier makes copies.

definition_en: a printer that produces copy whose quality is as good as or better than that produced by a typewriter
example_sentence: A letter quality printer produces copy whose quality is as good as or better than a typewriter.

definition_en: what computers think in; a programming language for professionals only
example_sentence: Machine language is what computers think in.

definition_en: a computer's capacity for storing information
example_sentence: Memory is a computer's capacity for storing information.

definition_en: a personal computer
example_sentence: A microcomputer is essentially a personal computer.

definition_en: the central brain of the computer
example_sentence: The microprocessor acts as the central brain of the computer.

definition_en: a device that enables one computer to communicate with another using ordinary telephone lines
example_sentence: A modem enables one computer to communicate with another using telephone lines.

definition_en: a computer screen or cathode ray tube
example_sentence: A monitor displays visual output from the computer.

definition_en: a hand-held device for moving a computer cursor and entering simple information
example_sentence: The mouse is a hand-held device for moving a computer cursor.

definition_en: a printer that comes close to achieving typewriter quality
example_sentence: A near letter-quality printer comes close to achieving typewriter quality.

definition_en: a relatively simple programming language
example_sentence: Pascal is a relatively simple programming language.

definition_en: an accessory, such as a printer or modem, that is attached to a computer
example_sentence: A peripheral is an accessory that is attached to a computer.

definition_en: a computer small enough to be carried from one place to another
example_sentence: A portable computer is small enough to be carried from one place to another.

definition_en: the set of instructions that causes a computer to do something
example_sentence: A program is the set of instructions that causes a computer to do something.

definition_en: an organized system of commands that enables a computer user to create programs
example_sentence: A programming language enables a computer user to create programs.

definition_en: RAM; the part of the computer's memory that the user can add to and delete from
example_sentence: Random access memory is the part of the computer's memory that the user can add to and delete from.

definition_en: ROM; a permanent part of a computer's memory, which the user can neither add to nor delete from
example_sentence: Read-only memory is a permanent part of a computer's memory.

definition_en: computer programs
example_sentence: Software refers to computer programs.

definition_en: to leave a place, thing, or person, usually forever
example_sentence: The scientists had to abandon the experiment due to safety protocols.
academic_context: 역사 — 영토 포기, 과학 — 실험/가설 폐기

definition_en: to take in liquid, gas, or another substance from the surface or space around
example_sentence: Plants absorb carbon dioxide from the atmosphere during photosynthesis.
academic_context: 과학 — 에너지/빛/액체 흡수, 사회 — 문화 동화

definition_en: relating to education and scholarship
example_sentence: The student received an award for her outstanding academic achievements.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 학교 생활, 학업 성취도 평가

definition_en: the means or opportunity to approach or enter a place
example_sentence: Students need a password to access the online library database.
academic_context: 정보/문헌 검색, 지리 — 지형적 접근성

definition_en: to provide lodging or sufficient space for; to fit in with the wishes or needs of
example_sentence: The new laboratory is large enough to accommodate thirty students.
academic_context: 사회/경제 — 인구 수용, 변화에 대한 적응

definition_en: to go somewhere with someone as a companion or escort
example_sentence: A sudden drop in temperature accompanied the severe storm.
academic_context: 과학 — 수반되는 현상 설명, 역사 — 동맹/동행

definition_en: to achieve or complete successfully
example_sentence: You can accomplish great things with hard work and dedication.
academic_context: 역사 — 역사적 인물의 업적, 일반 — 프로젝트 완료

definition_en: correct in all details; exact
example_sentence: It is essential to take accurate measurements during the chemistry lab.
academic_context: 과학/수학 — 측정 및 데이터의 정확성

definition_en: to successfully bring about or reach a desired objective, level, or result
example_sentence: The country managed to achieve economic stability after the crisis.
academic_context: 역사/사회 — 국가나 단체의 목표 달성

definition_en: to buy or obtain for oneself; to learn or develop a skill
example_sentence: Children acquire language skills rapidly in their early years.
academic_context: 언어학 — 언어 습득, 경제 — 기업 인수(acquisition)

definition_en: to make suitable for a new use or purpose; modify
example_sentence: Animals must adapt to their changing environment to survive.
academic_context: 생물학 — 진화와 적응, 문학 — 원작의 각색

definition_en: satisfactory or acceptable in quality or quantity
example_sentence: Ensure you have adequate lighting when examining the microscopic slides.
academic_context: 과학/보건 — 필수 조건 충족 여부 (예: 충분한 영양)

definition_en: to alter or move something slightly in order to achieve the desired fit, appearance, or result
example_sentence: You may need to adjust the telescope's focus to see the moon clearly.
academic_context: 과학 — 실험 기구 조작, 경제 — 물가 조정

definition_en: to manage and be responsible for the running of a business, organization, etc.
example_sentence: The principal will administer the state-wide exams next week.
academic_context: 정치/사회 — 정부 행정, 보건 — 약물 투여

definition_en: to choose to take up, follow, or use; to legally take another's child and bring it up as one's own
example_sentence: The committee decided to adopt the new environmental policy.
academic_context: 사회/정치 — 법안이나 정책의 채택

definition_en: to move forward in a purposeful way; development or improvement
example_sentence: Technological advances have completely changed the way we communicate.
academic_context: 역사 — 군대의 진격, 과학 — 기술 발전

definition_en: a person who publicly supports or recommends a particular cause or policy
example_sentence: Environmentalists advocate for the transition to renewable energy sources.
academic_context: 정치/사회 — 권리 옹호, 토론 — 특정 입장의 지지

definition_en: to have an effect on; make a difference to
example_sentence: Climate change will significantly affect global weather patterns.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 원인과 결과 분석 (Cause and Effect)

definition_en: to change or cause to change in character or composition
example_sentence: Failing to water the plant will permanently alter its growth cycle.
academic_context: 과학 — 상태 변화, 문학 — 줄거리나 성격의 변형

definition_en: one of two or more available possibilities
example_sentence: Solar power is a clean alternative to fossil fuels.
academic_context: 환경/사회 — 대체 에너지, 논증 에세이 — 대안 제시

definition_en: to make minor changes to a text, piece of legislation, etc., in order to make it fairer or more accurate
example_sentence: The constitution was amended to grant women the right to vote.
academic_context: 사회/정치 — 법률 및 헌법 수정 (Amendment)

definition_en: to examine methodically and in detail the constitution or structure of something
example_sentence: Students must analyze the poem to uncover its underlying themes.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 에세이, 실험 데이터 분석, 문학 비평

definition_en: to regard as probable; expect or predict
example_sentence: Economists anticipate a rise in inflation by the end of the year.
academic_context: 경제 — 시장 예측, 과학 — 실험 결과 예상

definition_en: clearly visible or understood; obvious
example_sentence: It became apparent that the initial hypothesis was incorrect.
academic_context: 논리/추론 — 명백한 증거 제시 및 결론 도출

definition_en: to add as a supplement, accessory, or appendix
example_sentence: The researcher will append a list of references to the end of the report.
academic_context: 논문/보고서 — 부록(appendix) 첨부

definition_en: a way of dealing with a situation or problem; to come near to
example_sentence: The scientist took a novel approach to solving the complex equation.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 문제 해결 방식, 방법론(methodology)

definition_en: close to the actual, but not completely accurate or exact
example_sentence: The approximate distance from the Earth to the Sun is 93 million miles.
academic_context: 수학/과학 — 근사치 계산(estimation), 오차 범위

definition_en: a particular part or feature of something
example_sentence: We must consider every aspect of the issue before making a decision.
academic_context: 논증 에세이 — 다각적 관점 분석

definition_en: to gather together in one place for a common purpose; to put together the parts of
example_sentence: The students assemble in the auditorium every Monday for announcements.
academic_context: 역사 — 국회/의회(assembly), 과학 — 실험 장비 조립

definition_en: to evaluate or estimate the nature, ability, or quality of
example_sentence: The final exam is designed to assess your understanding of the course material.
academic_context: 교육 — 수행 평가(assessment), 경제 — 세금/가치 사정

definition_en: to allocate a job or duty
example_sentence: The teacher will assign a new reading project next week.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 과제(assignment) 부여, 역할 분담

definition_en: to help someone typically by doing a share of the work
example_sentence: A microscope can assist researchers in viewing cellular structures.
academic_context: 연구 — 연구 보조, 사회복지 — 원조 및 지원

definition_en: to suppose to be the case, without proof; to take on a role
example_sentence: In geometry, we often assume a line is straight unless stated otherwise.
academic_context: 수학/논리학 — 전제와 가정(assumption), 역사 — 권력 장악

definition_en: to tell someone something positively or confidently to dispel any doubts
example_sentence: The teacher assured the students that the test would only cover Chapter 3.
academic_context: 논증 — 신뢰성 부여

definition_en: to fasten, join, or connect something
example_sentence: Please attach your research paper to the email.
academic_context: 생물학 — 바이러스의 세포 부착, 문서 작성 — 첨부

definition_en: to succeed in achieving something that one has worked for
example_sentence: Water boils when it attains a temperature of 100 degrees Celsius.
academic_context: 과학 — 특정 수치 도달, 교육 — 학업 성취

definition_en: a settled way of thinking or feeling about someone or something
example_sentence: A positive attitude can greatly influence a student's academic performance.
academic_context: 심리학 — 태도 형성, 문학 — 화자의 어조/태도

definition_en: to regard something as being caused by someone or something; a quality or feature
example_sentence: Historians attribute the fall of the empire to economic instability.
academic_context: 역사 — 원인 분석, 문학 — 캐릭터의 속성(특징)

definition_en: a writer of a book, article, or report
example_sentence: The author used vivid imagery to describe the setting of the novel.
academic_context: 문학 — 작가 분석, 인용문 출처 표기

definition_en: the power or right to give orders, make decisions, and enforce obedience
example_sentence: The Constitution gives Congress the authority to pass new laws.
academic_context: 정치/사회 — 권력 구조, 학술 논문 — 해당 분야의 권위자

definition_en: to convert a process or facility to be operated by largely automatic equipment
example_sentence: Factories automate assembly lines to increase production speed.
academic_context: 기술/경제 — 산업 혁명 및 공정 자동화

definition_en: able to be used or obtained; at someone's disposal
example_sentence: All course materials will be available on the school's website.
academic_context: 자료 검색 — 문헌/자료 확보 가능성

definition_en: having knowledge or perception of a situation or fact
example_sentence: Students must be aware of the safety procedures before starting the lab.
academic_context: 심리학/보건 — 인지 및 인식(awareness)

definition_en: in the interests of a person, group, or principle
example_sentence: The class president accepted the award on behalf of all the students.
academic_context: 연설문/공식 문서 — 대표성 표기

definition_en: an advantage or profit gained from something
example_sentence: One major benefit of regular exercise is improved cardiovascular health.
academic_context: 논술 — 찬반(Pros and Cons) 및 장점 분석

definition_en: prejudice in favor of or against one thing, person, or group compared with another
example_sentence: A good journalist must try to report the news without political bias.
academic_context: 사회학/통계 — 데이터 편향, 역사 — 사료의 객관성 비판

definition_en: a connection that fastens things together; an agreement with legal force
example_sentence: In chemistry, atoms share electrons to form a covalent bond.
academic_context: 화학 — 화학 결합, 경제 — 채권, 사회 — 유대감

definition_en: of short duration; concise
example_sentence: The teacher gave a brief overview of the chapter before giving the quiz.
academic_context: 작문 — 간결한 요약 작성

definition_en: the mass or magnitude of something large; the majority
example_sentence: The bulk of the essay should focus on providing evidence for your thesis.
academic_context: 통계/데이터 — 데이터의 대다수(the bulk of)

definition_en: having the ability, fitness, or quality necessary to do or achieve a specified thing
example_sentence: This computer is capable of processing massive amounts of data.
academic_context: 기술/과학 — 기계나 생명체의 잠재 능력

definition_en: the maximum amount that something can contain; the ability to do something
example_sentence: The stadium was filled to capacity during the championship game.
academic_context: 과학 — 열용량/부피, 경제 — 생산 능력

definition_en: a class or division of people or things having particular shared characteristics
example_sentence: Whales fall into the category of mammals, not fish.
academic_context: 생물학 — 분류학, 통계 — 범주형 데이터 분리

definition_en: to bring or come to an end
example_sentence: The company decided to cease production of the outdated model.
academic_context: 역사 — 휴전(ceasefire) 및 조약

definition_en: a call to take part in a contest or competition; a task that tests someone's abilities
example_sentence: Einstein's theory of relativity challenged the fundamental concepts of physics.
academic_context: 논증 — 기존 가설에 대한 반박/이의 제기

definition_en: a band of frequencies; a method of communication; to direct towards a particular end
example_sentence: The river carved a deep channel through the valley over millions of years.
academic_context: 지리 — 수로/해협, 생물 — 세포막 통로(ion channel)

definition_en: a main division of a book, typically with a number or title
example_sentence: The final chapter of the textbook summarizes all the main concepts.
academic_context: 문학 — 소설의 장, 역사 — 역사의 한 시기

definition_en: a sheet of information in the form of a table, graph, or diagram
example_sentence: The biology report included a chart showing the population growth of bacteria.
academic_context: 수학/과학 — 시각적 데이터 표현 및 분석

definition_en: relating to chemistry, or the interactions of substances
example_sentence: A chemical reaction occurs when baking soda is mixed with vinegar.
academic_context: 화학 — 화학 반응, 화합물

definition_en: a fact or condition connected with or relevant to an event or action
example_sentence: Under no circumstance should you mix these two volatile liquids.
academic_context: 역사 — 역사적 사건의 배경/정황, 법학 — 정황 증거

definition_en: to quote (a passage, book, or author) as evidence for or justification of an argument
example_sentence: You must appropriately cite your sources to avoid plagiarism in your essay.
academic_context: 작문/연구 — 논문 및 출처 인용 형식(MLA, APA)

definition_en: to make a statement or situation less confused and more comprehensible
example_sentence: Could you clarify what you mean by 'sustainable development'?
academic_context: 토론/에세이 — 개념 정의 및 논지 명확화

definition_en: judged over a period of time to be of the highest quality and outstanding of its kind
example_sentence: Shakespeare's 'Hamlet' is a classic example of Renaissance tragedy.
academic_context: 문학/예술 — 고전 작품 분석

definition_en: a unit of grammatical organization; a particular and separate article in a treaty, bill, or contract
example_sentence: An independent clause contains a subject and a verb and can stand alone as a sentence.
academic_context: 문법/언어학 — 문장 구조, 사회 — 법안의 세부 조항

definition_en: a system of words, letters, figures, or other symbols substituted for other words or letters
example_sentence: The school enforces a strict dress code for all students.
academic_context: 컴퓨터 과학 — 프로그래밍 언어, 사회 — 행동 강령/규범

definition_en: to occur at or during the same time
example_sentence: The publication of the new book was timed to coincide with the author's birthday.
academic_context: 역사 — 사건의 동시 발생

definition_en: to suddenly fall down or give way
example_sentence: The bridge may collapse if the structural damage is not repaired immediately.
academic_context: 역사/경제 — 제국의 멸망, 주식 시장의 붕괴

definition_en: a person with whom one works, especially in a profession or business
example_sentence: The scientist published the research paper along with two of her colleagues.
academic_context: 직업/사회 — 전문적인 직장 동료

definition_en: to begin or start
example_sentence: The graduation ceremony will commence promptly at 10 AM.
academic_context: 공식 문서 — 행사나 학위 수여식(commencement) 시작

definition_en: an instruction, command, or duty given to a person or group of people
example_sentence: The government set up a commission to investigate the cause of the accident.
academic_context: 사회/정치 — 특별 위원회, 예술 — 작품 의뢰

definition_en: to carry out or perpetrate a mistake, crime, or flaw; to pledge or bind
example_sentence: Students must commit to studying at least two hours a day to pass the AP exam.
academic_context: 윤리/법학 — 범죄 발생, 일반 — 목표에 대한 헌신

definition_en: a raw material or primary agricultural product that can be bought and sold
example_sentence: Oil is a vital commodity that heavily influences the global economy.
academic_context: 경제/지리 — 1차 산물 및 무역 품목

definition_en: to share or exchange information, news, or ideas
example_sentence: Dolphins use a complex system of sounds to communicate with each other.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 정보와 의사 전달, 생물학 — 동물들의 신호 전달

definition_en: a group of people living in the same place or having a particular characteristic in common
example_sentence: The local library serves as a central hub for the entire community.
academic_context: 생물학 — 군집(생태계), 사회 — 공동체 의식

definition_en: to produce something, especially a list, report, or book, by assembling information collected from other sources
example_sentence: The researchers will compile the survey data into a comprehensive report.
academic_context: 연구/보고서 — 자료 수집 및 취합

definition_en: consisting of many different and connected parts; not simple
example_sentence: The human brain is an incredibly complex organ that controls our body.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 시스템이나 구조의 복잡성

definition_en: a part or element of a larger whole, especially a part of a machine or vehicle
example_sentence: The mitochondria are a vital component of a living cell.
academic_context: 과학 — 기계/세포/물질의 핵심 구성 요소

definition_en: a thing that is composed of two or more separate elements
example_sentence: Water is a chemical compound made up of hydrogen and oxygen.
academic_context: 화학 — 화합물, 경제 — 복리(compound interest)

definition_en: to calculate or reckon a figure or amount
example_sentence: We need a calculator to quickly compute the volume of the cylinder.
academic_context: 수학/컴퓨터 과학 — 수치적 계산 연산

definition_en: to form or devise a plan or idea in the mind; to become pregnant
example_sentence: It is difficult to conceive how immense the universe truly is.
academic_context: 철학/문학 — 개념이나 추상적 아이디어의 구상

definition_en: to focus all one's attention on a particular object or activity
example_sentence: Students must concentrate intensely during the timed portion of the test.
academic_context: 화학 — 용액의 농도(concentration), 심리학 — 주의 집중

definition_en: an abstract idea; a general notion
example_sentence: Gravity is a fundamental concept taught in introductory physics.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 핵심 원리나 추상적인 관념 설명

definition_en: to bring something to an end; to arrive at a judgment or opinion by reasoning
example_sentence: Based on the data, scientists conclude that the Earth's temperature is rising.
academic_context: 에세이/연구 — 데이터 및 논거에 기반한 최종 결론

definition_en: the manner in which a person behaves; to organize and carry out
example_sentence: The university will conduct a survey to measure student satisfaction.
academic_context: 과학 — 실험 수행(conduct an experiment), 열/전기의 전도

definition_en: to grant or bestow a title, degree, benefit, or right; to have discussions
example_sentence: The principal will confer the diplomas to the graduating class.
academic_context: 교육 — 학위 수여, 비즈니스/외교 — 회담 개최

definition_en: to keep or restrict someone or something within certain limits
example_sentence: Please confine your answers to the space provided on the exam sheet.
academic_context: 생물/의학 — 전염병의 제한, 논증 — 논의 범위 설정

definition_en: to establish the truth or correctness of something previously believed or suspected to be the case
example_sentence: Further tests are needed to confirm the initial diagnosis.
academic_context: 과학/논리 — 가설 입증, 데이터 확인

definition_en: a serious disagreement or argument; a prolonged armed struggle
example_sentence: The novel centers on the emotional conflict between the two main characters.
academic_context: 문학 — 서사적 갈등 구조, 역사 — 전쟁과 국가 간 분쟁

definition_en: to comply with rules, standards, or laws
example_sentence: The new building design must conform to local safety regulations.
academic_context: 사회/심리 — 집단 규범 순응, 법/제도 준수

definition_en: permission for something to happen or agreement to do something
example_sentence: The experiment cannot proceed without the written consent of the participants.
academic_context: 윤리/보건 — 피실험자 및 환자의 사전 동의 (Informed consent)

definition_en: following as a result or effect
example_sentence: The severe drought and consequent crop failure led to a famine.
academic_context: 인과관계 에세이 — 결과적 상황 묘사

definition_en: notably large in size, amount, or extent
example_sentence: The project requires a considerable amount of time and resources.
academic_context: 통계/연구 — 유의미한 양이나 규모 표현

definition_en: to be composed or made up of
example_sentence: A standard deck of playing cards consists of 52 cards.
academic_context: 화학/물리 — 물질의 구성 요소 설명

definition_en: occurring continuously over a period of time; a situation that does not change
example_sentence: The speed of light in a vacuum is a physical constant.
academic_context: 수학/물리 — 변하지 않는 값(상수), 통제 변인

definition_en: to be a part of a whole; to make up
example_sentence: Nitrogen and oxygen constitute the majority of the Earth's atmosphere.
academic_context: 사회/법학 — 헌법(constitution), 국가 구성 요소

definition_en: to severely restrict the scope, extent, or activity of
example_sentence: Budget cuts will severely constrain the school's ability to offer extracurricular programs.
academic_context: 경제/물리 — 제약 조건(constraints) 분석

definition_en: to build or erect; an idea or theory containing various conceptual elements
example_sentence: The author constructs a compelling argument using historical evidence.
academic_context: 문학/작문 — 논리 구축, 기하학 — 도형 작도

definition_en: to seek information or advice from someone with expertise
example_sentence: You should consult a dictionary if you are unsure of a word's meaning.
academic_context: 연구 — 문헌 참고, 전문가 자문

definition_en: to use up a resource; to eat, drink, or ingest
example_sentence: Modern appliances consume much less electricity than older models.
academic_context: 경제 — 소비 및 소비자(consumer), 생물 — 에너지 소모

definition_en: living or occurring at the same time; belonging to the present
example_sentence: Shakespeare and Marlowe were contemporary playwrights in Elizabethan England.
academic_context: 역사 — 동시대 인물/사건, 예술 — 현대 미술

definition_en: the circumstances that form the setting for an event, statement, or idea
example_sentence: To understand the poem, you must look at the historical context in which it was written.
academic_context: 문학/역사 — 역사적 배경 및 문맥적 의미 파악

definition_en: a written or spoken agreement; to decrease in size, number, or range
example_sentence: Metals tend to expand when heated and contract when cooled.
academic_context: 물리 — 열수축, 경제/법 — 계약 조건

definition_en: to deny the truth of a statement by asserting the opposite
example_sentence: The new evidence seems to contradict the witness's earlier testimony.
academic_context: 논리학/법학 — 논리적 모순, 반대 증거 제시

definition_en: opposite in nature, direction, or meaning
example_sentence: Contrary to popular belief, bats are not blind.
academic_context: 에세이 — 통념에 대한 반박 (Contrary to ~)

definition_en: the state of being strikingly different from something else; to compare in order to show differences
example_sentence: The essay will compare and contrast the two political systems.
academic_context: 문학/작문 — 비교와 대조 (Compare and contrast)

definition_en: to give something in order to help achieve or provide something
example_sentence: Vehicle emissions contribute significantly to air pollution in urban areas.
academic_context: 과학/사회 — 원인 제공 요소 파악

definition_en: disagreement, typically when prolonged, public, and heated
example_sentence: The new grading policy sparked a lot of controversy among parents and teachers.
academic_context: 사회/정치 — 사회적 찬반 논쟁 주제

definition_en: to come or bring together for a meeting or activity; assemble
example_sentence: The committee will convene next week to discuss the proposed changes.
academic_context: 정치/역사 — 의회나 위원회 소집

definition_en: to tend to meet at a point; to come together from different directions
example_sentence: The two rivers converge at the center of the valley.
academic_context: 수학/과학 — 극한의 수렴, 지각판의 수렴 경계

definition_en: to engage in conversation; the opposite or reverse of a fact or statement
example_sentence: In logic, the converse of a true statement is not necessarily true.
academic_context: 논리학/수학 — 명제의 역(converse)

definition_en: to cause to change in form, character, or function
example_sentence: Solar panels are used to convert sunlight into electrical energy.
academic_context: 과학 — 단위 변환, 에너지 전환, 종교사 — 개종

definition_en: to cause someone to believe firmly in the truth of something
example_sentence: The lawyer tried to convince the jury of his client's innocence.
academic_context: 토론/에세이 — 독자 설득형 글쓰기 (Persuasive writing)

definition_en: to work jointly toward the same end
example_sentence: The two nations agreed to cooperate on environmental protection projects.
academic_context: 사회/역사 — 국가 간 조약 및 협력

definition_en: to organize the different elements of a complex body or activity; numerical values to locate a point
example_sentence: You must use the x and y coordinates to graph the line equation.
academic_context: 수학/지리 — 좌표계 (Coordinate system)

definition_en: the tough central part of various fruits; the central or most important part of something
example_sentence: The Earth's core is primarily made of molten iron and nickel.
academic_context: 지구과학 — 지구의 핵, 문학/사회 — 핵심 가치

definition_en: relating to a large company or group
example_sentence: The corporate tax rate was lowered to stimulate economic growth.
academic_context: 경제/비즈니스 — 기업 구조 및 경제 정책

definition_en: to have a close similarity; match or agree almost exactly; to communicate by writing letters
example_sentence: The results of the second trial correspond closely with the first one.
academic_context: 과학/수학 — 데이터 값의 일치, 상응하는 각(Corresponding angles)

definition_en: the ability of a customer to obtain goods before payment; acknowledgment for an achievement
example_sentence: Students must complete 120 credits to graduate from the university.
academic_context: 교육 — 이수 학점, 학술 — 출처 인용 및 공로 인정

definition_en: a principle or standard by which something may be judged or decided (plural of criterion)
example_sentence: The teacher handed out a rubric outlining the grading criteria for the project.
academic_context: 평가 — 평가 기준표(Rubric), 과학 — 분류 기준

definition_en: decisive or critical, especially in the success or failure of something
example_sentence: Water is a crucial element for the survival of all living organisms.
academic_context: 논증 에세이 — 핵심 논거 강조

definition_en: to prepare and use land for crops; to try to acquire or develop a quality or skill
example_sentence: Reading regularly helps to cultivate a strong vocabulary.
academic_context: 농업/역사 — 농경의 시작, 교육 — 소양 및 능력 개발

definition_en: a system of money in general use in a particular country; fact of being generally accepted
example_sentence: The euro is the official currency for many countries in the European Union.
academic_context: 경제 — 환율 및 화폐 시스템

definition_en: a formal discussion on a particular topic in a public meeting or legislative assembly
example_sentence: The candidates engaged in a heated debate over healthcare reform.
academic_context: 정치/사회 — 입법 논의, 토론 대회

definition_en: to become smaller, fewer, or less; to politely refuse
example_sentence: The population of the endangered species continues to decline due to habitat loss.
academic_context: 역사 — 문명의 쇠퇴, 생태학 — 개체수 감소

definition_en: to state or describe exactly the nature, scope, or meaning of
example_sentence: The first paragraph of your essay should clearly define the problem.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 핵심 용어의 개념 정의

definition_en: clearly stated or decided; not vague or doubtful
example_sentence: We need a definite answer by tomorrow so we can finalize the schedule.
academic_context: 수학 — 정적분(Definite integral), 명확한 증거

definition_en: to clearly show the existence or truth of something by giving proof or evidence
example_sentence: The lab experiment will demonstrate how chemical bonds are formed.
academic_context: 과학 — 시연 및 입증, 사회 — 정치적 시위

definition_en: to be a sign of; to indicate
example_sentence: In this formula, 'V' is used to denote volume.
academic_context: 수학/과학 — 기호가 상징하는 의미 설명

definition_en: to state that one refuses to admit the truth or existence of
example_sentence: Galileo was forced to deny his scientific claims about the solar system.
academic_context: 역사/법 — 사실의 부인, 논증 — 반대 의견 서술

definition_en: to make someone feel utterly dispirited or dejected; to reduce the level of economic activity
example_sentence: High interest rates can depress the housing market by making mortgages expensive.
academic_context: 경제 — 경제 대공황(The Great Depression), 경기 침체

definition_en: to obtain something from a specified source
example_sentence: Many English words derive from ancient Latin and Greek roots.
academic_context: 언어학 — 어원 파생, 수학 — 도함수(derivative) 도출

definition_en: a plan or drawing produced to show the look and function of a building, garment, or object before it is made
example_sentence: Engineers must design bridges that can withstand extreme weather conditions.
academic_context: 공학/예술 — 실험 및 프로젝트 설계 (Experimental design)

definition_en: without being affected by; in spite of
example_sentence: Despite the heavy rain, the football game continued as scheduled.
academic_context: 논증 — 반전 접속 및 대조적 사실 제시

definition_en: to discover or identify the presence or existence of
example_sentence: The smoke alarm is designed to detect even the smallest trace of a fire.
academic_context: 과학 — 화학 물질 및 파동의 감지

definition_en: to depart from an established course or accepted standard
example_sentence: If the results deviate significantly from the expected values, the experiment must be repeated.
academic_context: 통계 — 표준 편차(Standard deviation), 사회 — 규범 일탈

definition_en: a thing made or adapted for a particular purpose, especially a piece of mechanical or electronic equipment
example_sentence: A mnemonic is a useful memory device for learning new vocabulary.
academic_context: 문학 — 문학적 장치(Literary device), 기술 — 전자기기

definition_en: to give all or a large part of one's time or resources to a person, activity, or cause
example_sentence: Marie Curie devoted her entire life to the study of radioactivity.
academic_context: 역사 — 역사적 인물의 헌신적 업적 설명

definition_en: to state or order authoritatively; to say or read aloud words to be typed or written down
example_sentence: The laws of physics dictate that the ball must eventually fall back to the ground.
academic_context: 역사 — 독재 체제(dictatorship), 과학 — 원리의 지배

definition_en: a measurable extent of some kind, such as length, breadth, depth, or height
example_sentence: We live in a physical universe with three spatial dimensions.
academic_context: 물리/수학 — 3차원 도형, 논증 — 문제의 다각적 관점

definition_en: to make or become less
example_sentence: The sound of the train began to diminish as it moved further away.
academic_context: 경제 — 수확 체감의 법칙(Law of diminishing returns)

definition_en: to take over the place, position, or role of someone or something
example_sentence: The construction of the new dam will displace thousands of local residents.
academic_context: 물리 — 변위(displacement), 부력 계산, 사회 — 난민 발생

definition_en: to make a prominent exhibition of something in a place where it can be easily seen
example_sentence: Male peacocks display their colorful feathers to attract a mate.
academic_context: 생물 — 동물의 과시 행동, 데이터 시각화 — 도표 전시

definition_en: to get rid of by throwing away or giving or selling to someone else
example_sentence: Hazardous chemicals must be disposed of safely according to the lab rules.
academic_context: 환경 — 폐기물 처리(Waste disposal)

definition_en: too large or too small in comparison with something else

academic_context: 통계/사회 — 비례하지 않는 자원 분배나 통계적 불균형

definition_en: a disagreement, argument, or debate
example_sentence: The two countries have been in a border dispute for decades.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 영토 분쟁, 학술적 이견 제기

definition_en: recognizably different in nature from something else of a similar type
example_sentence: There is a distinct difference between the symptoms of a cold and the flu.
academic_context: 생물 — 종의 뚜렷한 특징, 논술 — 명확한 차이점 서술

definition_en: to pull or twist out of shape; to give a misleading or false account or impression of
example_sentence: Media bias can often distort the truth of political events.
academic_context: 사회/역사 — 역사적 사실 왜곡, 물리 — 음향/시각의 왜곡

definition_en: to give shares of something; to supply goods
example_sentence: The teacher will distribute the exam papers facedown on your desks.
academic_context: 경제 — 소득 분배, 통계 — 데이터의 분포(Distribution)

definition_en: showing a great deal of variety; very different

academic_context: 생태학 — 생물 다양성(Biodiversity), 사회 — 문화적 다양성

definition_en: a piece of written, printed, or electronic matter that provides information or evidence; to record something
example_sentence: The historian spent years trying to document the daily lives of ancient peasants.
academic_context: 역사 — 1차 사료(Primary document), 실험 과정 기록

definition_en: an area of territory owned or controlled by a ruler or government; a specified sphere of activity or knowledge
example_sentence: Quantum physics is a highly specialized domain of science.
academic_context: 수학 — 함수의 정의역(Domain), 생물 — 분류학의 역(Domain)

definition_en: to have a commanding influence on; exercise control over

academic_context: 생물 — 우성 유전자(Dominant trait), 역사 — 패권 지배

definition_en: a preliminary version of a piece of writing; to select a person for a certain purpose (such as military service)
example_sentence: You should always write a rough draft before submitting your final essay.
academic_context: 작문 — 에세이 초안 작성, 역사 — 징병제

definition_en: a play for theater, radio, or television; an exciting, emotional, or unexpected series of events
example_sentence: Shakespeare's works are considered the pinnacle of English Renaissance drama.
academic_context: 문학 — 희곡 분석, 서사 구조

definition_en: the time during which something continues
example_sentence: Students must remain seated for the entire duration of the exam.
academic_context: 물리/과학 — 시간 간격 및 기간 측정

definition_en: characterized by constant change, activity, or progress
example_sentence: The Earth's climate is a dynamic system influenced by numerous factors.
academic_context: 물리 — 동역학(Dynamics), 문학 — 입체적 인물(Dynamic character)

definition_en: the wealth and resources of a country or region, especially in terms of the production and consumption of goods and services
example_sentence: The industrial revolution completely transformed the global economy.
academic_context: 사회/역사 — 거시경제학, 국가 경제 체제 분석

definition_en: to prepare written material for publication by correcting, condensing, or otherwise modifying it
example_sentence: Make sure to carefully edit your essay for grammar and spelling errors.
academic_context: 작문/언어 — 에세이 교정 및 편집 과정

definition_en: a part or aspect of something abstract; a substance that cannot be broken down into simpler substances
example_sentence: Gold is a chemical element with the symbol Au on the periodic table.
academic_context: 화학 — 화학 원소, 문학 — 이야기의 구성 요소

definition_en: to completely remove or get rid of something
example_sentence: The body uses the kidneys to eliminate waste products from the blood.
academic_context: 수학 — 소거법(Elimination method), 생물 — 노폐물 배출

definition_en: to move out of or away from something and come into view; to become apparent
example_sentence: New patterns begin to emerge when we graph the survey data.
academic_context: 역사 — 신흥 국가의 등장, 과학 — 새로운 현상 발현

definition_en: special importance, value, or prominence given to something
example_sentence: The history curriculum places a strong emphasis on the Civil Rights Movement.
academic_context: 작문 — 논점의 강조, 언어학 — 강세

definition_en: to give someone or something the authority or means to do something
example_sentence: The microscope will enable you to see cells that are invisible to the naked eye.
academic_context: 과학 — 도구를 통한 관찰 가능성 부여

definition_en: to unexpectedly experience or be faced with something difficult or hostile
example_sentence: Researchers often encounter unexpected problems during field studies.
academic_context: 연구 — 예상치 못한 변수나 문제 직면

definition_en: to compel observance of or compliance with a law, rule, or obligation
example_sentence: The police are responsible for enforcing the speed limit on the highway.
academic_context: 사회/법학 — 법 집행 (Law enforcement)

definition_en: to intensify, increase, or further improve the quality, value, or extent of
example_sentence: Adding visual aids like charts can greatly enhance your presentation.
academic_context: 작문/발표 — 전달력 및 품질 향상

definition_en: very large in size, quantity, or extent
example_sentence: The blue whale requires an enormous amount of food every day to survive.
academic_context: 지리/과학 — 천체나 자연물의 거대한 규모 표현

definition_en: to make certain that something shall occur or be the case
example_sentence: Please double-check your calculations to ensure the accuracy of your results.
academic_context: 연구/실험 — 통제 변인 유지 및 정확성 확보

definition_en: a thing with distinct and independent existence
example_sentence: The two companies merged to form a single, powerful corporate entity.
academic_context: 정치/경제 — 국가나 기업 등의 독립된 주체

definition_en: the surroundings or conditions in which a person, animal, or plant lives or operates
example_sentence: Polar bears have thick fur to survive in the harsh Arctic environment.
academic_context: 생태학/사회 — 자연 환경 및 사회적 배경

definition_en: to consider one thing to be the same as or equivalent to another
example_sentence: You cannot simply equate high test scores with high intelligence.
academic_context: 수학 — 방정식(Equation), 논리 — 개념의 등치 오류 비판

definition_en: to supply with the necessary items for a particular purpose
example_sentence: The new biology lab is equipped with state-of-the-art microscopes.
academic_context: 과학 — 실험 장비 구비 상태

definition_en: equal in value, amount, function, meaning, etc.
example_sentence: One inch is roughly equivalent to 2.54 centimeters.
academic_context: 수학/물리 — 단위 변환 및 등가 교환

definition_en: to gradually wear away (soil, rock, or land) by wind, water, or other natural agents
example_sentence: The constant crashing of the waves will slowly erode the coastline.
academic_context: 지구과학 — 풍화 작용과 토양 침식(Soil erosion)

definition_en: a mistake; the state or condition of being wrong in conduct or judgment
example_sentence: A minor mathematical error in the first step ruined the entire calculation.
academic_context: 수학/과학 — 측정 오차 범위(Margin of error)

definition_en: to set up on a firm or permanent basis; to show to be true or certain by determining the facts
example_sentence: The experiment helped to establish a clear link between smoking and lung cancer.
academic_context: 역사 — 제도/기관 설립, 과학 — 인과관계 규명

definition_en: an extensive area of land in the country, usually with a large house; all the money and property owned by a particular person
example_sentence: The wealthy industrialist left his entire estate to a charitable foundation.
academic_context: 역사 — 장원(Manor estate), 신분제(The Three Estates)

definition_en: to roughly calculate or judge the value, number, quantity, or extent of
example_sentence: Scientists estimate that the universe is about 13.8 billion years old.
academic_context: 수학/과학 — 근사치 계산(Estimation)

definition_en: to form an idea of the amount, number, or value of; assess
example_sentence: You must evaluate the reliability of your sources before citing them in a research paper.
academic_context: 비판적 사고 — 문헌 및 증거의 신뢰성 평가

definition_en: occurring at the end of or as a result of a series of events
example_sentence: The heavy taxation led to the eventual rebellion of the colonies.
academic_context: 역사 — 지속적 원인으로 인한 최종 사건 도출

definition_en: plain or obvious; clearly seen or understood
example_sentence: It became evident that the plant needed more sunlight to survive.
academic_context: 논증 — 증거(evidence)를 바탕으로 한 명백한 사실 적시

definition_en: to develop gradually, especially from a simple to a more complex form
example_sentence: Over millions of years, some dinosaurs evolved into modern birds.
academic_context: 생물학 — 진화론(Theory of Evolution), 사회 — 제도의 발전

definition_en: to be greater in number or size than; to go beyond what is allowed
example_sentence: The concentration of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere continues to exceed previous records.
academic_context: 수학/통계 — 수치 초과 및 임계점 돌파

definition_en: to deny someone access to or bar someone from a place, group, or privilege
example_sentence: The study had to exclude participants who did not meet the health criteria.
academic_context: 과학/통계 — 데이터 및 변인 통제, 이상치 배제

definition_en: to publicly display a work of art or item of interest; to manifest a quality
example_sentence: Patients with this virus often exhibit symptoms of extreme fatigue.
academic_context: 생물/의학 — 증상 발현, 역사 — 유물 전시

definition_en: to become or make larger or more extensive
example_sentence: Water is the only liquid that expands when it freezes into a solid.
academic_context: 물리 — 열팽창, 경제 — 시장 및 제국의 확장

definition_en: a person who has a comprehensive and authoritative knowledge of or skill in a particular area
example_sentence: The court called upon a forensic expert to analyze the DNA evidence.
academic_context: 연구 논문 — 권위자의 의견 인용

definition_en: to make full use of and derive benefit from a resource; to use a situation or person in an unfair or selfish way
example_sentence: During the Industrial Revolution, many factory owners exploited child labor.
academic_context: 역사/사회 — 제국주의의 식민지 착취, 자원 개발

definition_en: to send goods or services to another country for sale
example_sentence: Countries often export agricultural products that grow well in their climate.
academic_context: 경제/지리 — 국제 무역, 데이터 — 자료 내보내기

definition_en: to make something visible by uncovering it; to reveal the true, objectionable nature of someone or something
example_sentence: The investigative journalist wrote an article to expose the political scandal.
academic_context: 과학 — 방사선/화학물질 노출, 사회 — 비리 폭로

definition_en: belonging to or forming the outer surface or structure of something
example_sentence: The cell membrane protects the internal structures from the external environment.
academic_context: 생물 — 외부 환경, 심리 — 외적 동기(External motivation)

definition_en: to remove or take out, especially by effort or force
example_sentence: Scientists use a centrifuge to extract DNA from the plant cells.
academic_context: 화학/생물 — 성분 추출, 문학/역사 — 텍스트 발췌본

definition_en: to make an action or process easy or easier
example_sentence: Enzymes are biological catalysts that facilitate chemical reactions in the body.
academic_context: 생물/화학 — 반응 촉진, 토론 — 진행자의 역할

definition_en: a circumstance, fact, or influence that contributes to a result or outcome
example_sentence: Climate is a major factor in determining what types of crops can grow in a region.
academic_context: 수학 — 인수 분해(Factorization), 과학/사회 — 원인 분석

definition_en: a distinctive attribute or aspect of something
example_sentence: One distinguishing feature of mammals is that they produce milk for their young.
academic_context: 지리 — 지리적 특성(Geographical feature), 생물 — 형태적 특징

definition_en: having or relating to a system of government in which several states form a unity but remain independent in internal affairs
example_sentence: The Constitution establishes the division of power between state and federal governments.
academic_context: 정치/역사 — 미국의 연방주의 체제(Federalism)

definition_en: a payment made to a professional person or to a professional or public body in exchange for advice or services
example_sentence: Many universities charge an application fee to process student admissions.
academic_context: 경제/사회 — 서비스 이용료 및 학비

definition_en: the management of large amounts of money, especially by governments or large companies
example_sentence: The government struggled to finance the massive infrastructure project.
academic_context: 경제/경영 — 자본주의 체제 및 재정 정책

definition_en: having limits or bounds
example_sentence: Fossil fuels like coal and oil are finite resources that will eventually run out.
academic_context: 수학 — 유한 집합, 환경/경제 — 한정된 자원

definition_en: capable of bending easily without breaking; able to be easily modified to respond to altered circumstances
example_sentence: The cartilage in our joints is a tough but flexible connective tissue.
academic_context: 생물/물리 — 물질의 성질, 사회 — 정책/규칙의 융통성

definition_en: to rise and fall irregularly in number or amount
example_sentence: Stock market prices tend to fluctuate based on economic news and investor sentiment.
academic_context: 통계/경제 — 데이터나 물가의 불규칙한 변동

definition_en: the center of interest or activity; to adapt to the prevailing level of light and become able to see clearly
example_sentence: A magnifying glass can focus the sun's rays to create enough heat to start a fire.
academic_context: 물리 — 광학적 초점, 작문 — 주제의 집중성

definition_en: the way in which something is arranged or set out
example_sentence: Your essay must strictly follow the MLA formatting guidelines.
academic_context: 작문/컴퓨터 — 문서 및 데이터의 배열 형식

definition_en: a mathematical relationship or rule expressed in symbols
example_sentence: The formula for calculating the area of a circle is pi times the radius squared.
academic_context: 수학/화학 — 대수 방정식 및 화합물 표기식

definition_en: planned for or about to happen in the near future; ready or made available when wanted or needed
example_sentence: The politician refused to comment on the forthcoming election.
academic_context: 논문/보고서 — 향후 발표될 연구 및 출판물

definition_en: the lowest load-bearing part of a building; an underlying basis or principle for something
example_sentence: An understanding of algebra provides the foundation for more advanced calculus.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 논리/학문의 기초 원리 (Foundational principles)

definition_en: an essential supporting structure of a building, vehicle, or object; a basic structure underlying a system, concept, or text
example_sentence: The Magna Carta provided the legal framework for constitutional law in England.
academic_context: 이론적 배경 — 개념적 틀(Theoretical framework), 체제

definition_en: an activity or purpose natural to or intended for a person or thing; a relationship or expression involving one or more variables
example_sentence: The primary function of the heart is to pump oxygenated blood throughout the body.
academic_context: 수학 — 함수(Function), 생물/기계 — 역할 및 작동 원리

definition_en: a sum of money saved or made available for a particular purpose; to provide with money
example_sentence: The government established a special fund to support renewable energy research.
academic_context: 연구/사회 — 프로젝트 자금 조달 및 지원금

definition_en: forming a necessary base or core; of central importance
example_sentence: Freedom of speech is a fundamental right guaranteed by the First Amendment.
academic_context: 물리/윤리 — 가장 기초적이고 중요한 원리나 권리

definition_en: in addition; besides (used to introduce a fresh consideration in an argument)
example_sentence: The hypothesis is scientifically unsound; furthermore, it lacks empirical evidence.
academic_context: 논증 에세이 — 추가적인 논거나 근거 제시 (접속 부사)

definition_en: either of the two sexes (male and female), especially when considered with reference to social and cultural differences rather than biological ones
example_sentence: Sociology examines how societal expectations shape traditional gender roles.
academic_context: 사회/인문 — 성 역할 및 불평등, 인구 통계

definition_en: to cause (something, especially an emotion or situation) to arise or come about; to produce energy
example_sentence: Wind turbines generate electricity without producing harmful greenhouse gas emissions.
academic_context: 과학 — 전력 및 에너지 생산, 수학 — 데이터/패턴 생성

definition_en: all of the people born and living at about the same time, regarded collectively; the production of energy
example_sentence: Traits are passed down from one generation to the next through DNA.
academic_context: 생물 — 유전의 세대, 사회 — 인구 통계학적 세대 구분

definition_en: the earth; a spherical representation of the earth
example_sentence: Climate change is an environmental crisis that affects the entire globe.
academic_context: 지리/사회 — 전 지구적(Global) 이슈 및 현상

definition_en: the object of a person's ambition or effort; an aim or desired result
example_sentence: The ultimate goal of the research project is to discover a cure for the disease.
academic_context: 연구/기획 — 프로젝트의 주요 목적(Objective)

definition_en: to agree to give or allow something requested; a sum of money given by an organization for a particular purpose
example_sentence: The university received a federal grant to upgrade its science laboratories.
academic_context: 역사 — 권리 부여, 교육/연구 — 학자금 및 연구 보조금

definition_en: a formal promise or assurance that certain conditions will be fulfilled; to provide such an assurance
example_sentence: The Bill of Rights was designed to guarantee individual freedoms for all citizens.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 권리 보장, 경제 — 품질 보증

definition_en: a general rule, principle, or piece of advice
example_sentence: Please refer to the safety guidelines before operating any of the heavy machinery.
academic_context: 규정/실험 — 절차 및 안전 규칙 명시

definition_en: the natural home or environment of an animal, plant, or other organism
example_sentence: Deforestation is rapidly destroying the natural habitat of the orangutan.
academic_context: 생태학 — 서식지 파괴(Habitat destruction) 및 보존

definition_en: as a consequence; for this reason
example_sentence: The chemical is highly volatile; hence, it must be stored in a cool, dark place.
academic_context: 논증 에세이 — 결론이나 인과 관계 도출 (따라서)

definition_en: to draw special attention to; an outstanding part of an event or period of time
example_sentence: The professor used a diagram to highlight the differences between plant and animal cells.
academic_context: 발표/작문 — 주요 요점이나 차이점 부각

definition_en: a supposition or proposed explanation made on the basis of limited evidence as a starting point for further investigation
example_sentence: Before conducting the experiment, the students must formulate a testable hypothesis.
academic_context: 과학적 방법론(Scientific method) — 가설 설정 및 검증

definition_en: similar in every detail; exactly alike
example_sentence: Identical twins share the exact same genetic code.
academic_context: 유전학 — 일란성 쌍둥이, 기하학 — 합동/동일 조건

definition_en: to establish or indicate who or what (someone or something) is
example_sentence: A botanist can easily identify different tree species by examining their leaves.
academic_context: 생물 — 종 식별, 문학 — 주제 파악, 심리 — 정체성

definition_en: lacking knowledge or awareness in general; uneducated or unsophisticated
example_sentence: Many colonizers were willfully ignorant of the complex cultures of the indigenous peoples.
academic_context: 역사/사회 — 특정 정보에 대한 결여 상태

definition_en: to explain or make (something) clear by using examples, charts, pictures, etc.
example_sentence: The graph will help illustrate how the global temperature has changed over the last century.
academic_context: 논술/발표 — 예시나 시각 자료를 통한 부연 설명

definition_en: a representation of the external form of a person or thing in art; a general impression that a person, organization, or product presents to the public
example_sentence: The poet uses vivid imagery to paint a clear image of the battlefield in the reader's mind.
academic_context: 문학 — 심상(Imagery), 사회 — 대중적 평판

definition_en: the action of one object coming forcibly into contact with another; a marked effect or influence
example_sentence: The invention of the printing press had a massive impact on the spread of knowledge in Europe.
academic_context: 물리 — 충격량, 역사/사회 — 심대한 영향 및 파급 효과

definition_en: to show (someone) to be involved in a crime; to convey (a meaning or intention) indirectly through what one says
example_sentence: The newly discovered documents implicate several high-ranking officials in the scandal.
academic_context: 법/역사 — 범죄/사건에의 연루, 논리 — 암시된 결과(Implication)

definition_en: implied though not plainly expressed
example_sentence: The author made an implicit criticism of the government's economic policies in her novel.
academic_context: 문학/작문 — 숨겨진 의도나 의미 분석

definition_en: to strongly suggest the truth or existence of (something not expressly stated)
example_sentence: The correlation between the two variables seems to imply a causal relationship.
academic_context: 논리학/데이터 해석 — 명시되지 않은 결론 유추

definition_en: to force (something unwelcome or unfamiliar) to be accepted or put in place
example_sentence: The British Parliament attempted to impose heavy taxes on the American colonies.
academic_context: 정치/역사 — 세금 부과 및 권력 강제

definition_en: a thing that motivates or encourages one to do something
example_sentence: Tax breaks are often used as an incentive for companies to reduce their carbon emissions.
academic_context: 경제/심리 — 행동을 유발하는 경제적/심리적 동기

definition_en: the occurrence, rate, or frequency of a disease, crime, or something else undesirable
example_sentence: Areas with poor sanitation typically have a higher incidence of waterborne diseases.
academic_context: 통계/보건 — 질병이나 사건의 발병 빈도

definition_en: to feel willing or favorably disposed toward (an action, belief, or attitude); a slope
example_sentence: The lack of solid evidence makes me incline towards rejecting the hypothesis.
academic_context: 물리 — 빗면(Inclined plane), 심리 — 성향 및 경향성

definition_en: money received, especially on a regular basis, for work or through investments
example_sentence: A progressive tax system applies higher rates to individuals with higher levels of income.
academic_context: 경제 — 소득세, 개인 및 국가 수입

definition_en: to take in or contain (something) as part of a whole; include
example_sentence: Try to incorporate at least three quotes from the primary text into your essay.
academic_context: 작문/기획 — 다른 요소나 인용구를 글에 통합

definition_en: an alphabetical list of records or pointers; an indicator, sign, or measure of something
example_sentence: The Consumer Price Index is a widely used measure of inflation.
academic_context: 수학 — 지수, 경제 — 경제 지표, 문헌 — 색인

definition_en: to point out; show
example_sentence: The chemical indicator will change color to indicate the presence of an acid.
academic_context: 과학 — 시약 반응, 통계 — 데이터가 가리키는 방향 설명

definition_en: single; separate; a single human being as distinct from a group, class, or family
example_sentence: In a democratic society, the rights of the individual must be protected.
academic_context: 사회/정치 — 개인주의와 집단주의 비교, 생물 — 개체

definition_en: to succeed in persuading or influencing (someone) to do something; to bring about or give rise to
example_sentence: The doctor administered a medication to induce labor.
academic_context: 논리학 — 귀납법(Inductive reasoning), 물리 — 전자기 유도

definition_en: certain to happen; unavoidable
example_sentence: Many historians argue that the collapse of the overextended empire was inevitable.
academic_context: 역사 — 필연적 사건 분석, 논술 — 피할 수 없는 결과

definition_en: the basic physical and organizational structures and facilities needed for the operation of a society or enterprise
example_sentence: The country needs to invest heavily in its transportation infrastructure to support economic growth.
academic_context: 경제/지리 — 도시 개발, 도로망, 전력망 등 기초 시설

definition_en: to hinder, restrain, or prevent (an action or process)
example_sentence: Cold temperatures severely inhibit the growth of most bacteria.
academic_context: 생물/화학 — 효소 반응 억제, 화학적 억제제(Inhibitor)

definition_en: existing or occurring at the beginning
example_sentence: The initial results of the trial look promising, but further testing is required.
academic_context: 물리 — 초기 속도/상태, 일반 — 시작 단계

definition_en: to cause (a process or action) to begin
example_sentence: The government plans to initiate a new public health campaign next month.
academic_context: 정치 — 정책 입안/주도(Initiative), 과학 — 연쇄 반응 개시

definition_en: to make changes in something established, especially by introducing new methods, ideas, or products
example_sentence: To remain competitive in the tech industry, companies must constantly innovate.
academic_context: 기술/경제 — 기술 혁신, 산업 발전 방향

definition_en: what is put in, taken in, or operated on by any process or system
example_sentence: The computer program requires the user to input their password to proceed.
academic_context: 컴퓨터 과학/경제 — 투입량과 산출량(Input and output)

definition_en: to place, fit, or thrust (something) into another thing, especially with care
example_sentence: Carefully insert the slide into the microscope to view the specimen.
academic_context: 과학 — 실험 기구 조작, 작문 — 인용문 삽입

definition_en: the capacity to gain an accurate and deep intuitive understanding of a person or thing
example_sentence: Studying ancient artifacts gives archaeologists profound insight into past civilizations.
academic_context: 심리/역사 — 현상에 대한 깊이 있는 분석 결과

definition_en: to look at (someone or something) closely, typically to assess their condition or to discover any shortcomings
example_sentence: A health official will inspect the restaurant kitchen to ensure cleanliness.
academic_context: 과학 — 표본 관찰, 일반 — 규정 준수 여부 심사

definition_en: an example or single occurrence of something
example_sentence: The American Revolution is a classic instance of colonies rebelling against a monarchy.
academic_context: 에세이 — 사례 제시 (For instance = For example)

definition_en: a society or organization having a particular object or common factor, especially a scientific, educational, or social one; to introduce or establish
example_sentence: The research institute was founded to study the effects of climate change on marine life.
academic_context: 사회/정치 — 제도적 관행(Institution), 학술 연구 기관

definition_en: to direct or command someone to do something, especially as an official order; to teach
example_sentence: The teacher will instruct the students on how to safely handle the chemicals.
academic_context: 교육 — 지시 사항 명시(Instructions), 교사(Instructor)

definition_en: the quality of being honest and having strong moral principles; the state of being whole and undivided
example_sentence: Academic integrity strictly prohibits any form of cheating or plagiarism.
academic_context: 윤리/학교 — 학문적 진실성 (Academic integrity), 구조적 안정성

definition_en: the ability to acquire and apply knowledge and skills; the collection of information of military or political value
example_sentence: The Turing Test was proposed as a way to measure a machine's artificial intelligence.
academic_context: 심리 — 지능 평가, 역사 — 정보 기관

definition_en: of extreme force, degree, or strength
example_sentence: The chemical reaction produced a bright flash of light and intense heat.
academic_context: 물리/기상 — 자극이나 자연 현상의 강도

definition_en: to act in such a way as to have an effect on another; to communicate with or react to
example_sentence: Ecosystems function through the way plants and animals interact with their environment.
academic_context: 과학/사회 — 변인 간의 상호 작용 분석

definition_en: coming between two things in time, place, order, character, etc.
example_sentence: The larva is an intermediate stage of development between the egg and the adult insect.
academic_context: 생물 — 발달 단계의 중간체, 일반 — 중급 수준

definition_en: of or situated on the inside
example_sentence: Mammals have an internal skeleton made of bone.
academic_context: 생물/의학 — 체내 기관, 정치 — 국내 문제(Internal affairs)

definition_en: to explain the meaning of (information, words, or actions)
example_sentence: Students must analyze the data and interpret what the results mean for the hypothesis.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 시문학, 역사적 사료, 실험 데이터 해석

definition_en: an intervening time or space; a pause or break in activity
example_sentence: Measurements of the plant's growth were taken at weekly intervals.
academic_context: 수학/물리 — 시간/공간의 간격 측정

definition_en: to come between so as to prevent or alter a result or course of events
example_sentence: The United Nations may intervene to try and establish a ceasefire between the warring nations.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 타국의 분쟁이나 경제 문제에 대한 정부/단체의 개입

definition_en: to expend money with the expectation of achieving a profit or material result
example_sentence: The government needs to invest more money into public education.
academic_context: 경제 — 자본 투자, 경영 논리

definition_en: to carry out a systematic or formal inquiry to discover and examine the facts of (an incident, allegation, etc.) so as to establish the truth
example_sentence: Scientists will investigate the long-term effects of the new drug.
academic_context: 연구/과학 — 현상이나 가설을 탐구하고 조사하는 과정

definition_en: to cite or appeal to (someone or something) as an authority for an action or in support of an argument
example_sentence: The lawyer decided to invoke the Fifth Amendment to protect his client.
academic_context: 법/정치 — 법률 조항이나 권위자의 말 인용

definition_en: to have or include (something) as a necessary or integral part or result
example_sentence: Earning a medical degree will involve many years of rigorous study and practice.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 과정 및 현상에 필수적으로 요구되는 사항 서술

definition_en: to cause (a person or place) to be or remain alone or apart from others
example_sentence: To prevent the disease from spreading, doctors must isolate the infected patients.
academic_context: 생물/의학 — 바이러스 격리, 지리/역사 — 지정학적 고립주의

definition_en: an important topic or problem for debate or discussion; to supply or distribute
example_sentence: Climate change is a critical global issue that requires immediate attention.
academic_context: 논술/토론 — 핵심 논제 및 사회적 문제(Issue)

definition_en: an individual article or unit, especially one that is part of a list, collection, or set
example_sentence: Every item on the periodic table represents a unique chemical element.
academic_context: 통계/시험 — 설문지 문항, 목록의 구성 단위

definition_en: to show or prove to be right or reasonable
example_sentence: You must use quotes from the text to justify your interpretation of the poem.
academic_context: 논증 에세이 — 주장에 대한 타당한 논리 및 증거 제시

definition_en: a small piece of paper, fabric, plastic, or similar material attached to an object and giving information about it; to assign to a category
example_sentence: Students must properly label all parts of the diagram before submitting the biology quiz.
academic_context: 과학 — 해부도/도표 기입, 사회 — 사회적 낙인(Labeling theory)

definition_en: work, especially hard physical work
example_sentence: The construction of the pyramids required immense physical labor.
academic_context: 역사/경제 — 노동 계급, 노동 시장

definition_en: a sheet, quantity, or thickness of material, typically one of several, covering a surface or body
example_sentence: The Earth's crust is the outermost solid layer of the planet.
academic_context: 지구과학 — 지층(strata), 대기권 구조

definition_en: an educational talk to an audience, especially to students in a university or college
example_sentence: The professor gave a fascinating lecture on the causes of World War I.
academic_context: 교육/대학 — 수업 형태 (Lecture vs. Seminar)

definition_en: of, based on, or concerned with the law
example_sentence: Citizens have a legal obligation to pay their taxes.
academic_context: 사회/정치 — 법적 권리와 의무

definition_en: to make or enact laws
example_sentence: Congress has the power to legislate on matters concerning national security.
academic_context: 정치 — 입법부(Legislative branch), 법안 통과

definition_en: conforming to the law or to rules; able to be defended with logic or justification
example_sentence: The rebels claimed that the current government was not legitimate.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 권력의 정통성(Legitimacy), 논리적 타당성

definition_en: in the same way; also
example_sentence: Water is essential for plants; likewise, it is crucial for human survival.
academic_context: 작문 — 비교/대조 에세이의 접속 부사 (Similarly)

definition_en: a relationship between two things or situations, especially where one affects the other
example_sentence: Studies have shown a clear link between a poor diet and heart disease.
academic_context: 과학/사회 — 변인 간의 연관성 파악

definition_en: reasoning conducted or assessed according to strict principles of validity
example_sentence: You must use logic and evidence to build a convincing argument.
academic_context: 철학/수학 — 논증, 명제, 비판적 사고

definition_en: to cause or enable (a condition or state of affairs) to continue; to state strongly
example_sentence: The historian maintains that the revolution was driven primarily by economic factors.
academic_context: 논증 — 화자의 강력한 주장, 과학 — 항상성 유지

definition_en: important, serious, or significant
example_sentence: Climate change poses a major threat to global biodiversity.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 핵심 요소/원인 강조

definition_en: to handle or control in a skillful manner; to alter or edit (data) to achieve a desired result
example_sentence: In an experiment, the researcher manipulates the independent variable.
academic_context: 과학 — 조작 변인(Manipulated variable), 미디어 왜곡

definition_en: relating to or done with the hands; a book of instructions
example_sentence: Before the Industrial Revolution, most goods were produced through manual labor.
academic_context: 기술/역사 — 수작업 및 기계화 이전의 생산 방식

definition_en: the edge or border of something; an amount by which a thing is won or falls short
example_sentence: The candidate won the election by a very narrow margin.
academic_context: 통계 — 오차 범위(Margin of error), 문서 서식

definition_en: to make as large or great as possible
example_sentence: Companies often seek to minimize costs and maximize profits.
academic_context: 경제/수학 — 최대값 구하기, 이윤 극대화

definition_en: a system of parts working together in a machine; a natural or established process
example_sentence: Natural selection is the primary mechanism of evolution.
academic_context: 과학 — 생물학적/화학적 작동 원리

definition_en: the main means of mass communication (broadcasting, publishing, and the internet) regarded collectively
example_sentence: The media plays a crucial role in shaping public opinion during elections.
academic_context: 사회/정치 — 여론 형성 및 정보 전달 매체

definition_en: relating to the science or practice of medicine
example_sentence: Advances in medical technology have significantly increased human life expectancy.
academic_context: 과학/보건 — 보건 및 의료 기술

definition_en: relating to the mind
example_sentence: Solving complex math puzzles is a good form of mental exercise.
academic_context: 심리학 — 인지 과정, 정신 건강

definition_en: a particular form of procedure for accomplishing or approaching something
example_sentence: The scientific method involves observation, hypothesis, experimentation, and conclusion.
academic_context: 과학/연구 — 연구 방법론(Methodology)

definition_en: to move from one region or habitat to another, especially regularly according to the seasons
example_sentence: Many species of birds migrate to warmer climates during the winter months.
academic_context: 생물 — 동물의 철새 이동, 지리/역사 — 인구 이주

definition_en: relating to or characteristic of soldiers or armed forces
example_sentence: The empire expanded its borders through aggressive military conquest.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 국방력, 전쟁, 군사 전략

definition_en: of a minimum amount, quantity, or degree; negligible
example_sentence: The damage to the building after the earthquake was minimal.
academic_context: 통계/평가 — 영향력이나 수치의 미미함

definition_en: to reduce (something, especially something unwanted or unpleasant) to the smallest possible amount or degree
example_sentence: Engineers designed the car to minimize air resistance and improve fuel efficiency.
academic_context: 공학/경제 — 비용 및 위험의 최소화

definition_en: the least or smallest amount or quantity possible, attainable, or required
example_sentence: The thermometer recorded a minimum temperature of minus ten degrees overnight.
academic_context: 수학/과학 — 최소값, 하한선

definition_en: a government department headed by a minister of state
example_sentence: The Ministry of Education announced new changes to the national curriculum.
academic_context: 정치/사회 — 행정부 부처 명칭

definition_en: lesser in importance, seriousness, or significance
example_sentence: The essay contained a few minor grammatical errors, but the argument was strong.
academic_context: 평가/분석 — 덜 중요한 세부 사항

definition_en: to make partial or minor changes to (something), typically so as to improve it or to make it less extreme
example_sentence: Scientists genetically modify crops to make them more resistant to pests.
academic_context: 생물/문법 — 유전자 조작, 수식어의 역할

definition_en: a reason for doing something, especially one that is hidden or not obvious
example_sentence: The detective tried to determine the suspect's motive for committing the crime.
academic_context: 문학/심리 — 캐릭터 및 역사적 인물의 행동 동기

definition_en: held in common by two or more parties; experienced or done by each of two or more parties toward the other
example_sentence: The agreement was based on mutual respect and understanding between the two countries.
academic_context: 생물 — 상리 공생(Mutualism), 사회 — 상호 이익

definition_en: to make ineffective or invalid; to deny the existence of
example_sentence: The discovery of new evidence could negate the previous conclusions of the study.
academic_context: 논리학/수학 — 명제의 부정, 결과 무효화

definition_en: an arrangement of intersecting horizontal and vertical lines; a group or system of interconnected people or things
example_sentence: The human brain contains a complex network of billions of neurons.
academic_context: 과학/기술 — 신경망, 통신망 시스템

definition_en: not helping or supporting either side in a conflict, disagreement, etc.; impartial
example_sentence: Switzerland is famous for remaining politically neutral during major global conflicts.
academic_context: 역사 — 중립국, 화학 — 중성(pH 7)

definition_en: in spite of that; notwithstanding; all the same
example_sentence: The test was incredibly difficult; nevertheless, most students managed to pass.
academic_context: 논증 에세이 — 반대/양보의 접속 부사

definition_en: something that is usual, typical, or standard
example_sentence: Working from home has become the norm for many employees since the pandemic.
academic_context: 사회학 — 사회적 규범(Social norms)

definition_en: conforming to a standard; usual, typical, or expected
example_sentence: A body temperature of 98.6°F is considered normal for humans.
academic_context: 통계 — 정규 분포(Normal distribution), 과학 — 기준 상태

definition_en: a conception of or belief about something
example_sentence: The author challenges the traditional notion of gender roles in her novel.
academic_context: 철학/문학 — 추상적 관념이나 대중적 믿음 지칭

definition_en: (of a person or their judgment) not influenced by personal feelings or opinions; a thing aimed at or sought; a goal
example_sentence: A journalist must remain objective and report the facts without bias.
academic_context: 연구/작문 — 객관적 시각 유지, 논문의 목적

definition_en: to get, acquire, or secure (something)
example_sentence: Plants obtain energy from the sun through the process of photosynthesis.
academic_context: 과학/연구 — 데이터/결과 습득, 화학 물질 추출

definition_en: easily perceived or understood; clear, self-evident, or apparent
example_sentence: It is obvious from the data that the new marketing campaign was a success.
academic_context: 논증 — 명확한 근거를 바탕으로 한 결론 도출

definition_en: to reside or have one's place of business in (a building); to take control of a place
example_sentence: During the war, enemy forces managed to occupy the capital city.
academic_context: 역사 — 영토 점령, 과학 — 부피(공간) 차지

definition_en: to happen; take place
example_sentence: Earthquakes occur most frequently along the boundaries of tectonic plates.
academic_context: 과학/역사 — 사건이나 자연 현상의 발생

definition_en: different to what is usual or expected; strange; (of numbers) not divisible by two
example_sentence: The sequence consists of only odd numbers: 1, 3, 5, 7, and 9.
academic_context: 수학 — 홀수(Odd number), 통계 — 확률/배당률(Odds)

definition_en: to counteract (something) by having an opposing force or effect
example_sentence: The company planted trees to offset its carbon dioxide emissions.
academic_context: 경제/환경 — 손실 보전, 효과 상쇄

definition_en: continuing; still in progress
example_sentence: Finding a cure for the common cold is an ongoing challenge for medical researchers.
academic_context: 연구/사회 — 현재 진행 중인 연구 및 이슈

definition_en: a thing that is or may be chosen
example_sentence: Students have the option to take either French or Spanish to fulfill the language requirement.
academic_context: 평가/제도 — 객관식(Multiple-choice options), 선택 과목

definition_en: to align or position (something) relative to the points of a compass or other specified positions
example_sentence: Sunflowers naturally orient themselves to face the sun as it moves across the sky.
academic_context: 지리/생물 — 공간적 방향성, 교육 — 오리엔테이션

definition_en: the way a thing turns out; a consequence
example_sentence: The treaty was a successful outcome of months of diplomatic negotiations.
academic_context: 역사/실험 — 사건의 최종 결과 및 결론

definition_en: the amount of something produced by a person, machine, or industry
example_sentence: The factory increased its daily output of cars to meet the rising demand.
academic_context: 컴퓨터/경제 — 입력 대비 산출량, 생산성 지표

definition_en: taken as a whole; in all
example_sentence: Despite a few minor setbacks, the overall success of the project was remarkable.
academic_context: 요약/결론 — 전반적인 흐름과 결과 총평

definition_en: to extend over so as to cover partly; to coincide partly in time or subject
example_sentence: There is a significant overlap in the themes explored by the two poets.
academic_context: 문학/생물 — 교집합, 서식지나 주제의 공통 부분

definition_en: a small group of people brought together to discuss, investigate, or decide on a particular matter; a flat board
example_sentence: A panel of experts was assembled to evaluate the environmental impact of the dam.
academic_context: 사회/토론 — 전문가 집단 토의(Panel discussion)

definition_en: a distinct section of a piece of writing, usually dealing with a single theme and indicated by a new line
example_sentence: Each paragraph in your essay should begin with a strong topic sentence.
academic_context: 작문 — 에세이 구조(Introduction, Body paragraph, Conclusion)

definition_en: occurring or existing at the same time or in a similar way; (of lines) side by side and having the same distance continuously between them
example_sentence: The author draws a parallel between the fall of Rome and modern political events.
academic_context: 수학 — 평행선, 문학 — 평행 구조 및 유사성 비교

definition_en: a numerical or other measurable factor forming one of a set that defines a system or sets the conditions of its operation
example_sentence: The researchers set strict parameters for who could participate in the clinical trial.
academic_context: 수학/컴퓨터 — 매개변수, 실험/연구의 기준선 및 범위

definition_en: to take part in an action or endeavor
example_sentence: All students are encouraged to participate in extracurricular activities.
academic_context: 연구/학교 — 피실험자(Participant), 토론 참여

definition_en: a person who takes part in an undertaking with another or others, especially in a business or company with shared risks and profits
example_sentence: The university partnered with a local tech company to fund the new robotics lab.
academic_context: 경제/사회 — 협력 관계 (Partnership)

definition_en: accepting or allowing what happens or what others do, without active response or resistance
example_sentence: In grammar, the passive voice focuses on the object receiving the action rather than the subject performing it.
academic_context: 문법 — 수동태(Passive voice), 심리 — 소극적 성향

definition_en: to become aware or conscious of (something); come to realize or understand
example_sentence: Bats use echolocation to perceive their surroundings in complete darkness.
academic_context: 심리/철학 — 감각 인지(Perception), 시각의 차이

definition_en: by a specified amount in or for every hundred
example_sentence: Over seventy percent of the Earth's surface is covered by oceans.
academic_context: 통계/수학 — 백분율 데이터를 통한 비율 설명

definition_en: a length or portion of time; a punctuation mark at the end of a sentence
example_sentence: The Renaissance was a period of great cultural and artistic revival in Europe.
academic_context: 역사 — 특정 시대 구분, 화학 — 주기율표(Periodic table)

definition_en: to continue firmly or obstinately in an opinion or a course of action in spite of difficulty, opposition, or failure
example_sentence: If the symptoms persist for more than three days, you should consult a doctor.
academic_context: 과학 — 잔류성(지속성) 물질, 심리 — 끈기

definition_en: a particular attitude toward or way of regarding something; a point of view
example_sentence: The novel is interesting because it is told from the perspective of the villain.
academic_context: 문학 — 서술자 시점(Point of view), 미술 — 원근법

definition_en: a distinct period or stage in a series of events or a process of change or development
example_sentence: The moon goes through several different phases as it orbits the Earth.
academic_context: 과학 — 달의 위상, 물질의 상태 변화(Phase change)

definition_en: a fact or situation that is observed to exist or happen, especially one whose cause or explanation is in question
example_sentence: The Northern Lights are a beautiful natural phenomenon caused by solar wind.
academic_context: 과학/사회 — 탐구 및 분석의 대상이 되는 관찰된 현상

definition_en: relating to the body as opposed to the mind; relating to things perceived through the senses as opposed to the mind
example_sentence: Melting ice into water is an example of a physical change, not a chemical one.
academic_context: 과학 — 물리적 변화, 보건 — 신체 활동

definition_en: with the addition of
example_sentence: Knowledge of a second language is a definite plus when applying for jobs.
academic_context: 수학 — 덧셈 연산, 일반 — 추가적인 이점

definition_en: a course or principle of action adopted or proposed by a government, party, business, or individual
example_sentence: The school implemented a strict zero-tolerance policy against bullying.
academic_context: 정치/사회 — 정부 규제 및 기관 운영 방침

definition_en: a part of a whole; an amount, section, or piece of something
