example_sentence: A large portion of the budget was allocated to military spending.
academic_context: 통계/수학 — 전체 중 차지하는 비율이나 부분

definition_en: to present or constitute (a problem, danger, or difficulty); to sit or stand in a particular position
example_sentence: The increasing amount of space debris poses a serious threat to satellites.
academic_context: 논증 — 문제나 질문 제기 (pose a question/threat)

definition_en: consisting in or characterized by the presence rather than the absence of distinguishing features; showing optimism
example_sentence: Protons are subatomic particles that carry a positive electrical charge.
academic_context: 과학 — 양전하, 수학 — 양수, 심리 — 긍정적 강화

definition_en: having or showing the capacity to become or develop into something in the future
example_sentence: A boulder resting at the top of a hill has a high amount of potential energy.
academic_context: 물리 — 위치 에너지(Potential energy), 잠재력

definition_en: a person actively engaged in an art, discipline, or profession, especially medicine
example_sentence: As a medical practitioner, she was required to keep her patients' records confidential.
academic_context: 의학/법학 — 현업 종사자

definition_en: to come before (something) in time, order, or position
example_sentence: The Bronze Age preceded the Iron Age in the history of human technological development.
academic_context: 역사 — 시대의 선후 관계, 논리 — 전제 조건

definition_en: marked by exactness and accuracy of expression or detail
example_sentence: The chemist needed a precise measurement of the liquid to ensure the reaction worked.
academic_context: 과학/수학 — 수치의 정밀도(Precision)와 정확도(Accuracy)

definition_en: to say or estimate that (a specified thing) will happen in the future or will be a consequence of something
example_sentence: Meteorologists use complex computer models to predict the path of a hurricane.
academic_context: 과학 — 가설 설정 후 결과 예측, 기상학

definition_en: present as the strongest or main element; having or exerting control or power
example_sentence: English is the predominant language used in international business and aviation.
academic_context: 통계/역사 — 가장 높은 비율이나 주도권

definition_en: denoting an action or event preceding or done in preparation for something fuller or more important
example_sentence: The researchers published their preliminary findings before the study was officially completed.
academic_context: 연구 — 예비 조사, 논문 초안 전 단계

definition_en: to suppose that something is the case on the basis of probability
example_sentence: In the legal system, a defendant is presumed innocent until proven guilty.
academic_context: 법/논리 — 합리적 근거에 기반한 전제/추정

definition_en: existing or occurring before in time or order
example_sentence: The current economic crisis is much worse than the previous one.
academic_context: 문헌 조사 — 선행 연구(Previous studies) 참조

definition_en: of chief importance; principal; earliest in time or order of development
example_sentence: The primary cause of the Civil War was the dispute over the institution of slavery.
academic_context: 역사 — 1차 사료(Primary source), 과학 — 주요 원인

definition_en: of first importance; main; of the best possible quality
example_sentence: A prime number is a number greater than 1 that has only two factors: 1 and itself.
academic_context: 수학 — 소수(Prime number), 중요성의 강조

definition_en: first in order of importance; main; the person with the highest authority or most important position in an organization
example_sentence: The principal reason for the company's failure was a lack of innovation.
academic_context: 경제 — 투자 원금, 학교 — 교장

definition_en: a fundamental truth or proposition that serves as the foundation for a system of belief or behavior or for a chain of reasoning
example_sentence: Archimedes' principle explains why heavy ships are able to float on water.
academic_context: 물리/윤리 — 과학적 원리 및 도덕적 규범

definition_en: existing or coming before in time, order, or importance
example_sentence: Please ensure you have read the assigned chapter prior to attending the lecture.
academic_context: 논문/에세이 — 시간적 선행 조건 서술

definition_en: the fact or condition of being regarded or treated as more important
example_sentence: The government's top priority should be improving the nation's healthcare system.
academic_context: 정치/기획 — 문제 해결의 시급성 및 순서

definition_en: to begin or continue a course of action
example_sentence: Once you have completed the first section, you may proceed to the next page of the test.
academic_context: 절차/실험 — 다음 단계로의 이행

definition_en: a series of actions or steps taken in order to achieve a particular end
example_sentence: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert sunlight into food.
academic_context: 과학 — 화학적/생물학적 작용 과정

definition_en: relating to or connected with a profession; a person engaged in a specified activity as one's main paid occupation
example_sentence: You must use professional and academic language when writing a cover letter.
academic_context: 작문 — 형식적이고 정제된 어조 요구

definition_en: to formally forbid (something) by law, rule, or other authority
example_sentence: School rules strictly prohibit the use of cell phones during exams.
academic_context: 역사 — 금주법(Prohibition era), 규정 설명

definition_en: an individual or collaborative enterprise that is carefully planned to achieve a particular aim; to estimate or forecast
example_sentence: The science fair project requires students to test a hypothesis and present their findings.
academic_context: 수행 평가 — 장기 과제, 경제 — 미래 수치 예상(project)

definition_en: to further the progress of (something, especially a cause, venture, or aim); support or actively encourage
example_sentence: The health department launched a campaign to promote healthy eating habits in schools.
academic_context: 사회/보건 — 인식 제고 및 권장 사항

definition_en: a part, share, or number considered in comparative relation to a whole
example_sentence: A large proportion of the Earth's freshwater is frozen in glaciers and ice caps.
academic_context: 수학/통계 — 비례 관계, 백분율 및 분수 개념

definition_en: the possibility or likelihood of some future event occurring
example_sentence: The prospect of discovering life on other planets excites many astronomers.
academic_context: 경제/지리 — 미래 가치, 광물 탐사(prospecting)

definition_en: the official procedure or system of rules governing affairs of state or diplomatic occasions; a procedure for carrying out a scientific experiment
example_sentence: The lab strictly follows a safety protocol to prevent contamination.
academic_context: 과학/기술 — 실험 준칙 및 통신 규약

definition_en: the scientific study of the human mind and its functions, especially those affecting behavior in a given context
example_sentence: Psychology helps us understand how environmental factors influence human behavior.
academic_context: 인문/사회과학 — 인간 행동 및 정신 과정 분석

definition_en: the preparation and issuing of a book, journal, or piece of music for public sale
example_sentence: The scientist's research gained international recognition after its publication in a major journal.
academic_context: 연구/문학 — 학술지 게재, 문헌 출처 표시

definition_en: to prepare and issue (a book, journal, piece of music, or other work) for public sale or readership
example_sentence: Many authors hope to publish their novels with a traditional publishing house.
academic_context: 연구/문학 — 연구 논문이나 작품의 대외적 발표

definition_en: to acquire (something) by paying for it; buy
example_sentence: The school used its funding to purchase new computers for the library.
academic_context: 경제/역사 — 영토 매입(The Louisiana Purchase), 소비 경제

definition_en: to follow (someone or something) in order to catch or attack them; to seek to attain or accomplish (a goal)
example_sentence: After high school, she plans to pursue a degree in environmental science.
academic_context: 진로/목표 — 학위나 연구 주제의 지속적 탐구

definition_en: to repeat or copy out (a group of words from a text or speech), typically with an indication that one is not the original author or speaker
example_sentence: You should quote evidence directly from the text to support your literary analysis.
academic_context: 작문/문학 — 직접 인용(Direct quote) 및 근거 제시

definition_en: the emission of energy as electromagnetic waves or as moving subatomic particles
example_sentence: The ozone layer protects the Earth from harmful ultraviolet radiation from the sun.
academic_context: 과학/물리 — 복사 에너지, 방사성 물질

definition_en: relating to or affecting the fundamental nature of something; far-reaching or thorough
example_sentence: The invention of the internet brought about a radical change in how we communicate.
academic_context: 역사 — 급진파 정치(Radicalism), 수학 — 근호(Radical sign)

definition_en: made, done, happening, or chosen without method or conscious decision
example_sentence: To ensure fairness, the participants for the study were chosen through a random selection process.
academic_context: 통계/과학 — 무작위 표본 추출(Random sample), 변인 통제

definition_en: the area of variation between upper and lower limits on a particular scale
example_sentence: The students' scores on the math test fell within a wide range.
academic_context: 수학/통계 — 치역, 데이터의 범위(Range), 생태학 — 서식 범위

definition_en: the quantitative relation between two amounts showing the number of times one value contains or is contained within the other
example_sentence: The school aims to maintain a student-to-teacher ratio of 20 to 1.
academic_context: 수학/경제 — 두 수량 간의 비례 관계(Ratio)

definition_en: based on or in accordance with reason or logic
example_sentence: We must look at the evidence and make a rational decision rather than an emotional one.
academic_context: 수학 — 유리수(Rational number), 철학/논술 — 합리적 추론

definition_en: to respond or behave in a particular way in response to something
example_sentence: Baking soda will react with vinegar to produce carbon dioxide gas.
academic_context: 화학 — 화학 반응(Chemical reaction), 심리 — 자극에 대한 반응

definition_en: a person who rises in opposition or armed resistance against an established government or ruler
example_sentence: The colonists decided to rebel against the unfair taxation imposed by the king.
academic_context: 역사 — 반란, 혁명, 독립 전쟁

definition_en: to bring (a fact, event, or situation) back into one's mind, especially so as to recount it to others
example_sentence: The witness could not recall the exact time the incident occurred.
academic_context: 심리학 — 기억의 회상, 문학 — 회상 기법(Flashback)

definition_en: a period of temporary economic decline during which trade and industrial activity are reduced
example_sentence: The stock market crash of 1929 led to a severe global economic recession.
academic_context: 경제 — 경제 위기, 생물 — 열성 유전자(Recessive gene)

definition_en: to identify (someone or something) from having encountered them before; to acknowledge the existence or truth of
example_sentence: It is important to recognize the contributions of indigenous peoples to the region's history.
academic_context: 역사/사회 — 공로 인정, 논증 — 반론의 부분적 수용(인정)

definition_en: to put forward (someone or something) with approval as being suitable for a particular purpose or role
example_sentence: The CDC recommends getting a flu vaccine every year before the winter season.
academic_context: 보건/사회 — 공식 기관의 권고 사항, 에세이 결론

definition_en: to return to a normal state of health, mind, or strength; to find or regain possession of
example_sentence: The ecosystem took decades to fully recover from the devastating forest fire.
academic_context: 생태/경제 — 경제 지표 회복, 자연의 자정/회복 능력

definition_en: to make smaller or less in amount, degree, or size
example_sentence: We must actively reduce our carbon footprint to combat global warming.
academic_context: 수학 — 약분(Reduce a fraction), 과학 — 환원 반응(Reduction)

definition_en: to mention or allude to; to pass a matter to a higher authority for a decision
example_sentence: When writing a research paper, you must refer to your sources in the bibliography.
academic_context: 연구/작문 — 문헌 참조(Reference), 대명사가 지칭하는 대상

definition_en: to remove impurities or unwanted elements from (a substance); to improve (something) by making small changes
example_sentence: The author spent months revising and refining the first draft of her novel.
academic_context: 화학 — 석유 정제, 작문 — 글의 질적 향상

definition_en: to throw back (heat, light, or sound) without absorbing it; to think deeply or carefully about
example_sentence: Mirrors reflect light, allowing us to see an image of ourselves.
academic_context: 물리 — 빛/소리의 반사, 문학/작문 — 성찰 에세이(Reflective essay)

definition_en: to make changes in (something, typically a social, political, or economic institution or practice) in order to improve it
example_sentence: The Protestant Reformation in the 16th century led to massive religious reform in Europe.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 사회 제도 개선, 종교 개혁

definition_en: a condition of being safe or sheltered from pursuit, danger, or trouble
example_sentence: Thousands of citizens sought refuge in neighboring countries during the civil war.
academic_context: 사회/역사 — 난민(Refugee) 문제, 생태학 — 서식지 보존 지역

definition_en: a government, especially an authoritarian one; a system or planned way of doing things
example_sentence: The military regime heavily censored the press to control public information.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 지배 체제, 보건 — 식이/운동 요법

definition_en: an area or division, especially part of a country or the world having definable characteristics but not always fixed boundaries
example_sentence: The Pacific Northwest is a region known for its dense forests and rainy climate.
academic_context: 지리 — 지역적 특성(기후, 경제, 문화)

definition_en: to enter or record on an official list or directory; to detect and show on a measuring instrument
example_sentence: The earthquake was powerful enough to register a 6.5 on the Richter scale.
academic_context: 과학/지구과학 — 지진계 측정 기록, 사회 — 선거인 등록

definition_en: to control or maintain the rate or speed of (a machine or process) so that it operates properly; to control by rules
example_sentence: The body perspires to help regulate its internal temperature.
academic_context: 생물 — 항상성 조절, 경제 — 정부의 시장 규제

definition_en: to strengthen or support (an object or substance), especially with additional material; to strengthen an existing feeling, idea, or habit
example_sentence: Providing clear examples will reinforce the main argument of your essay.
academic_context: 작문 — 논거 보강, 심리 — 강화 이론(Reinforcement)

definition_en: to dismiss as inadequate, inappropriate, or not to one's taste
example_sentence: The scientific community will reject a hypothesis if it is not supported by experimental data.
academic_context: 연구 — 귀무 가설 기각(Reject the null hypothesis), 면역 거부

definition_en: to make or show a connection between; to feel sympathy with or identify with
example_sentence: The biology teacher explained how the structure of an organ relates directly to its function.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 원인과 결과, 두 변인 간의 상관관계

definition_en: to allow or enable to escape from confinement; set free; to allow (something) to move, act, or flow freely
example_sentence: During respiration, cells release energy stored in glucose molecules.
academic_context: 생물/화학 — 에너지 및 호르몬 방출, 사회 — 성명서 발표

definition_en: closely connected or appropriate to what is being done or considered
example_sentence: Make sure all the sources you cite in your paper are relevant to your topic.
academic_context: 에세이/토론 — 논제와의 타당성 및 연관성(Relevance)

definition_en: consistently good in quality or performance; able to be trusted
example_sentence: A Wikipedia article is generally not considered a reliable source for academic research.
academic_context: 문헌 평가 — 정보 출처의 신뢰도(Reliability)

definition_en: to depend on with full trust or confidence
example_sentence: Desert plants rely heavily on adaptations to conserve water.
academic_context: 생태/사회 — 생존 또는 기능 유지를 위한 의존도

definition_en: to continue to exist, especially after other similar or related people or things have ceased to exist; to stay in the same place or condition
example_sentence: Despite new scientific discoveries, some mysteries of the universe remain unsolved.
academic_context: 수학 — 나머지(Remainder), 역사 — 유물/유해(Remains)

definition_en: to say something as a comment; mention
example_sentence: The historian remarked that the general's strategy was brilliant but highly risky.
academic_context: 문학/역사 — 비평 및 인물 발언 인용

definition_en: a medicine or treatment for a disease or injury; a means of counteracting or eliminating something undesirable
example_sentence: The government is trying to find a remedy for the high unemployment rate.
academic_context: 사회/경제 — 사회 문제에 대한 해결 방안(Solution)

definition_en: situated far from the main centers of population; distant
example_sentence: The tribe lived in a remote valley, completely isolated from modern civilization.
academic_context: 지리/역사 — 지리적 고립, 기술 — 원격(Remote sensing)

definition_en: to take (something) away or off from the position occupied
example_sentence: The surgeon successfully removed the tumor during the operation.
academic_context: 과학/수학 — 변인 및 오류 제거 과정

definition_en: to cause to be or become; make; to provide or give (a service, help, etc.)
example_sentence: The sudden earthquake rendered the entire city's power grid useless.
academic_context: 법/문학 — 판결을 내리다, 번역/표현하다(Rendering)

definition_en: to take the place of; to put something new in the place of
example_sentence: The factory plans to replace human workers with automated robotic systems.
academic_context: 경제/기술 — 인력이나 기술의 대체, 화학 — 치환 반응

definition_en: to be entitled or appointed to act or speak for (someone); to be a symbol or embodiment of
example_sentence: In algebra, letters like 'x' and 'y' are often used to represent unknown variables.
academic_context: 수학 — 변수, 정치 — 대표 민주주의(Representative democracy)

definition_en: to produce a copy or representation of; to produce offspring
example_sentence: Bacteria reproduce very quickly through a process called binary fission.
academic_context: 생물 — 생식/번식, 과학 — 실험 결과의 재현성(Reproducibility)

definition_en: to need for a particular purpose; to cause to be necessary
example_sentence: Photosynthesis requires sunlight, carbon dioxide, and water to occur.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 전제 조건 및 필수 요소 명시

definition_en: the systematic investigation into and study of materials and sources in order to establish facts and reach new conclusions
example_sentence: The scientist published her research on climate change in a peer-reviewed journal.
academic_context: 과학/인문 — 학술 논문 및 조사 활동

definition_en: to have qualities or features, especially those of appearance, in common with (someone or something)
example_sentence: The molecular structure of this synthetic compound closely resembles that of a natural hormone.
academic_context: 생물/문학 — 형태적 유사성(상동/상사 기관 등)

definition_en: to have one's permanent home in a particular place
example_sentence: Many diverse species of fish reside within the coral reef ecosystem.
academic_context: 지리/생태 — 인구 분포, 동식물의 서식

definition_en: to settle or find a solution to (a problem, dispute, or contentious matter)
example_sentence: The treaty helped to finally resolve the long-standing border dispute between the nations.
academic_context: 문학 — 서사적 갈등 해소(Resolution), 역사 — 분쟁 해결

definition_en: a stock or supply of money, materials, staff, and other assets that can be drawn on by a person or organization
example_sentence: Water is a vital natural resource that we must strive to conserve.
academic_context: 경제/환경 — 천연 자원, 연구 문헌(sources)

definition_en: to say something in reply; to do something as a reaction to someone or something
example_sentence: The immune system will respond to the virus by producing antibodies.
academic_context: 생물 — 자극과 반응, 논술 — 반론에 대한 대응

definition_en: to bring back (a previous right, practice, custom, or situation); to return to a former condition
example_sentence: Conservationists are working to restore the wetlands to their natural state.
academic_context: 역사 — 왕정 복고(Restoration), 생태계 복원

definition_en: to prevent (someone or something) from doing something; to keep under control or within limits
example_sentence: The government raised interest rates to restrain inflation.
academic_context: 경제/물리 — 작용의 억제, 물리적 구속력

definition_en: to put a limit on; keep under control
example_sentence: The new law will strictly restrict the use of chemical pesticides in agriculture.
academic_context: 사회/법 — 규제 조항, 생물 — 제한 효소(Restriction enzyme)

definition_en: to continue to have (something); keep possession of
example_sentence: A good thesis statement should help the reader retain the main point of your essay.
academic_context: 과학 — 수분/열의 보유, 심리 — 기억 유지(Memory retention)

definition_en: to make (previously unknown or secret information) known to others; to cause to be seen
example_sentence: The microscopic analysis will reveal the true structure of the cellular membrane.
academic_context: 연구/실험 — 새로운 사실과 데이터의 발견

definition_en: income, especially when of a company or organization and of a substantial nature
example_sentence: The government relies on tax revenue to fund public schools and infrastructure.
academic_context: 경제/정치 — 국가 예산, 기업의 총수익

definition_en: to move backward; to make (something) the opposite of what it was
example_sentence: Some scientists are researching ways to reverse the effects of global warming.
academic_context: 수학/과학 — 역함수, 가역 반응(Reversible reaction)

definition_en: to re-examine and make alterations to (written or printed matter); to reconsider and alter (an opinion or judgment)
example_sentence: After receiving feedback from the teacher, the student must revise his essay.
academic_context: 작문/연구 — 글의 수정 및 교열(Revision) 단계

definition_en: a forcible overthrow of a government or social order in favor of a new system; a dramatic and wide-reaching change
example_sentence: The Industrial Revolution completely changed the nature of global manufacturing.
academic_context: 역사 — 정치적/산업적 혁명, 과학 — 천체의 공전

definition_en: unable to bend or be forced out of shape; not flexible; not able to be changed or adapted
example_sentence: Plant cells have a rigid cell wall that provides structural support.
academic_context: 생물 — 세포벽의 성질, 사회 — 엄격한 법규

definition_en: the function assumed or part played by a person or thing in a particular situation
example_sentence: The mitochondria play a crucial role in producing energy for the cell.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 구조 내에서의 기능적 역할 명시

definition_en: a way or course taken in getting from a starting point to a destination
example_sentence: The Silk Road was an ancient trade route connecting the East and the West.
academic_context: 역사/지리 — 무역 및 탐험 경로, 생물 — 물질의 이동 경로

definition_en: in, relating to, or characteristic of the countryside rather than the town
example_sentence: Many people migrated from rural areas to urban centers during the 19th century.
academic_context: 지리/역사 — 도시(urban)와 농촌(rural) 인구의 비교

definition_en: the relative size or extent of something; an instrument for weighing; a series of marks at regular intervals
example_sentence: The map is drawn to scale, where one inch equals fifty miles.
academic_context: 지리/수학 — 축척, 통계 — 평가 척도, 생물 — 파충류 비늘

definition_en: a postulated sequence or development of events; a written outline of a movie, novel, or stage work
example_sentence: In a worst-case scenario, the dam could break and flood the entire valley.
academic_context: 과학/토론 — 가상 상황 설정(Hypothetical scenario)

definition_en: a plan for carrying out a process or procedure, giving lists of intended events and times
example_sentence: The course syllabus provides a detailed schedule of all assignments and exams.
academic_context: 학교 생활 — 강의 계획서, 시간표

definition_en: a large-scale systematic plan or arrangement for attaining some particular object or putting a particular idea into effect
example_sentence: The city introduced a new recycling scheme to reduce landfill waste.
academic_context: 문학 — 운율 체계(Rhyme scheme), 사회 — 정부 기획

definition_en: the extent of the area or subject matter that something deals with or to which it is relevant
example_sentence: The topic is too broad; you need to narrow the scope of your research paper.
academic_context: 연구/에세이 — 논의의 범위와 한계 설정

definition_en: any of the more or less distinct parts into which something is or may be divided or from which it is made up
example_sentence: The final section of the textbook contains a glossary of key terms.
academic_context: 기하학 — 단면(Cross-section), 교재 파트 구분

definition_en: an area or portion that is distinct from others; a distinct part of an economy or society
example_sentence: The technology sector has experienced immense growth over the last two decades.
academic_context: 수학 — 부채꼴(Sector), 경제 — 공공/민간 부문

definition_en: fixed or fastened so as not to give way, become loose, or be lost; to succeed in obtaining (something)
example_sentence: The activists managed to secure enough signatures to put the issue on the ballot.
academic_context: 정치/경제 — 권리 획득, 데이터 보안(Security)

definition_en: to attempt to find (something); to ask for (something) from someone
example_sentence: Many refugees seek asylum in countries that offer political freedom.
academic_context: 연구 — 정보 탐색, 사회 — 권리 주장 및 모색

definition_en: to carefully choose as being the best or most suitable
example_sentence: Natural selection is a process where organisms better adapted to their environment tend to survive.
academic_context: 생물 — 자연 선택(Natural selection), 일반 — 취사 선택

definition_en: a particular order in which related events, movements, or things follow each other
example_sentence: The DNA sequence determines the genetic makeup of an organism.
academic_context: 수학 — 수열(Sequence), 생물 — 유전자 배열 순서

definition_en: a number of things, events, or people of a similar kind or related nature coming one after another
example_sentence: A series circuit provides only one path for the electricity to flow.
academic_context: 물리 — 직렬 회로(Series circuit), 역사 — 연속된 사건

definition_en: to move or cause to move from one place to another, especially over a small distance; a slight change in position, direction, or tendency
example_sentence: The tectonic plates slowly shift, which can eventually cause an earthquake.
academic_context: 지구과학 — 단층 이동, 문학 — 시점이나 어조의 전환

definition_en: sufficiently great or important to be worthy of attention; noteworthy
example_sentence: The discovery of penicillin was a highly significant event in medical history.
academic_context: 통계 — 통계적으로 유의미한(Statistically significant), 논증

definition_en: looking or being almost, but not exactly, the same
example_sentence: In geometry, similar triangles have the same shape but not necessarily the same size.
academic_context: 기하학 — 닮음(Similar), 논술 — 유사점 비교

definition_en: to imitate the appearance or character of
example_sentence: Scientists use computers to simulate the formation of a galaxy.
academic_context: 과학/컴퓨터 — 모의 상황 및 모델링(Simulation)

definition_en: an area of ground on which a town, building, or monument is constructed; a website
example_sentence: The archeological site revealed evidence of a civilization dating back 5,000 years.
academic_context: 역사 — 발굴 현장, 세포 — 결합 부위(Binding site)

definition_en: one and only; belonging or restricted to one person or group of people
example_sentence: The queen possessed sole authority over the kingdom's military decisions.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 단독 권력, 논증 — 유일한 원인

definition_en: to a moderate extent or by a moderate amount
example_sentence: The results of the second trial were somewhat different from our initial expectations.
academic_context: 에세이/분석 — 극단적 주장을 피하기 위한 완화 표현

definition_en: a place, person, or thing from which something comes or can be obtained
example_sentence: Historians rely heavily on primary sources, such as diaries and official letters.
academic_context: 연구/역사 — 1차/2차 사료 표기

definition_en: clearly defined or identified; relating uniquely to a particular subject
example_sentence: You must provide specific examples from the text to support your thesis.
academic_context: 작문 — 추상적 개념에 대한 구체적(Specific) 근거

definition_en: to identify clearly and definitely; to state a fact or requirement clearly and precisely
example_sentence: The essay prompt specifies that the paper must be exactly five pages long.
academic_context: 수학/과학 — 명확한 조건 부여, 규칙 규정

definition_en: a round solid figure, or its surface, with every point on its surface equidistant from its center; an area of activity
example_sentence: The Earth is not a perfect sphere; it bulges slightly at the equator.
academic_context: 기하학 — 구형, 사회 — 정치적 세력권(Sphere of influence)

definition_en: not likely to change or fail; firmly established
example_sentence: Noble gases are chemically stable and rarely react with other elements.
academic_context: 화학/물리 — 물질의 화학적/물리적 안정 상태

definition_en: a fact or piece of data from a study of a large quantity of numerical data
example_sentence: The author cited a striking statistic showing the rapid decline of the bee population.
academic_context: 수학/연구 — 통계학 및 수치 데이터 증거

definition_en: the relative social, professional, or other standing of someone or something
example_sentence: The revolution aimed to completely overthrow the existing social status quo.
academic_context: 사회/역사 — 사회적 신분, 현재 상태(Status quo)

definition_en: uncomplicated and easy to do or understand
example_sentence: The process of solving a linear equation is relatively straightforward.
academic_context: 논리/수학 — 명쾌하고 단순한 원리나 해결책

definition_en: a plan of action or policy designed to achieve a major or overall aim
example_sentence: The general employed a brilliant defensive strategy to defeat the invading army.
academic_context: 역사 — 군사 전략, 일반 — 문제 해결을 위한 방법론

definition_en: pressure or tension exerted on a material object; a state of mental or emotional strain
example_sentence: Engineers must calculate the structural stress a bridge can endure before breaking.
academic_context: 물리 — 응력(압력), 언어학 — 음절의 강세

definition_en: the arrangement of and relations between the parts or elements of something complex
example_sentence: The atomic structure consists of a nucleus surrounded by orbiting electrons.
academic_context: 과학 — 분자/원자/세포의 구조, 문학 — 글의 구성

definition_en: to present (a proposal, application, or other document) to a person or body for consideration or judgment; to yield to superior force
example_sentence: You must submit your final essay through the online portal by midnight.
academic_context: 학교 생활 — 과제 제출, 역사 — 항복 및 복종

definition_en: coming after something in time; following
example_sentence: The initial experiment failed, but subsequent trials yielded positive results.
academic_context: 논증 에세이 — 시간적 선후 관계 (Subsequent to ~)

definition_en: a sum of money granted by the government or a public body to assist an industry or business so that the price of a commodity or service may remain low or competitive
example_sentence: The government offers a subsidy to farmers to encourage the production of corn.
academic_context: 경제/정치 — 정부의 재정 지원 및 경제 정책

definition_en: a person or thing acting or serving in place of another
example_sentence: In algebra, we can substitute the variable 'x' with a number to solve the equation.
academic_context: 수학 — 대입법(Substitution), 경제 — 대체재

definition_en: to achieve the desired aim or result; to take over a throne, inheritance, office, or other position from
example_sentence: When the king died, his eldest son succeeded him to the throne.
academic_context: 역사 — 왕위 계승(Succession), 생태학 — 천이 현상

definition_en: enough; adequate
example_sentence: There is not sufficient evidence to prove that the suspect committed the crime.
academic_context: 논리학/수학 — 충분조건(Sufficient condition), 근거의 양

definition_en: a particular amount of money; the total amount resulting from the addition of two or more numbers, amounts, or items
example_sentence: The sum of the interior angles of a triangle is always 180 degrees.
academic_context: 수학 — 덧셈의 결과(Sum), 벡터의 합

definition_en: a brief statement or account of the main points of something
example_sentence: The conclusion paragraph should provide a brief summary of your main arguments.
academic_context: 독해/작문 — 글의 요지 파악 및 간결한 요약 작성

definition_en: something that completes or enhances something else when added to it
example_sentence: In geometry, supplementary angles are two angles that add up to 180 degrees.
academic_context: 수학 — 보각(Supplementary angle), 건강/식단 — 영양 보충

definition_en: to look carefully and thoroughly at (someone or something); to investigate the opinions or experience of a group of people by asking them questions
example_sentence: The sociologist conducted a survey to understand the voting habits of college students.
academic_context: 통계/사회 — 설문 조사(Survey), 지리 — 지형 측량

definition_en: to continue to live or exist, especially in spite of danger or hardship
example_sentence: Only species that can adapt to changing environments will survive.
academic_context: 생물학 — 적자생존 (Survival of the fittest), 역사 — 위기 극복

definition_en: to temporarily prevent from continuing or being in force or effect; to hang
example_sentence: The school decided to suspend the student for violating the code of conduct.
academic_context: 화학 — 현탁액(Suspension), 물리 — 매달린 상태, 법 — 집행 유예

definition_en: to strengthen or support physically or mentally; to cause to continue for an extended period
example_sentence: The ecosystem cannot sustain this level of pollution for much longer.
academic_context: 환경/경제 — 지속 가능성 (Sustainability), 발전 및 유지

definition_en: a mark or character used as a conventional representation of an object, function, or process
example_sentence: In literature, water is often used as a symbol for purity or rebirth.
academic_context: 문학 — 상징주의(Symbolism), 화학/수학 — 원소 기호 및 연산자

definition_en: the quality of being made up of exactly similar parts facing each other or around an axis
example_sentence: A butterfly's wings exhibit perfect bilateral symmetry.
academic_context: 기하학 — 대칭, 생물 — 생물의 대칭적 구조

definition_en: feelings of pity and sorrow for someone else's misfortune; understanding between people
example_sentence: The author evokes the reader's sympathy for the tragic hero.
academic_context: 문학 — 독자의 감정 이입, 심리학 — 공감 능력

definition_en: a physical or mental feature which is regarded as indicating a condition of disease
example_sentence: A persistent cough is a common symptom of a respiratory infection.
academic_context: 의학/보건 — 질병의 증상, 사회 — 사회적 문제의 징후

definition_en: a group of symptoms which consistently occur together, or a condition characterized by a set of associated symptoms
example_sentence: Down syndrome is a genetic disorder caused by the presence of an extra chromosome 21.
academic_context: 보건/심리학 — 복합적인 증상의 묶음 (Syndrome)

definition_en: a set of connected things or parts forming a complex whole
example_sentence: The solar system consists of the sun and the celestial bodies bound to it by gravity.
academic_context: 과학/사회 — 순환기 계통(Nervous system), 법률 체계

definition_en: to make determined efforts to deal with (a problem or difficult task)
example_sentence: The new environmental policy aims to tackle the issue of plastic pollution.
academic_context: 사회/토론 — 복잡한 논제나 문제를 정면으로 다룸

definition_en: an action or strategy carefully planned to achieve a specific end
example_sentence: Boycotting goods was a common tactic used during the Civil Rights Movement.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 시위 전술, 군사 작전 세부 실행 방법

definition_en: a person, object, or place selected as the aim of an attack; an objective or result towards which efforts are directed
example_sentence: The marketing campaign's primary target is young adults aged 18 to 25.
academic_context: 경제/과학 — 목표치, 표적 세포(Target cell)

definition_en: a piece of work to be done or undertaken
example_sentence: The student's main task for the weekend was to outline the research paper.
academic_context: 학습/심리 — 수행 과제, 인지적 과업

definition_en: relating to a particular subject, art, or craft, or its techniques; requiring special knowledge to be understood
example_sentence: The textbook uses complex technical vocabulary that may be difficult for beginners.
academic_context: 공학/작문 — 전문 용어(Technical term), 기술적 세부 사항

definition_en: a way of carrying out a particular task, especially the execution or performance of an artistic work or a scientific procedure
example_sentence: Foreshadowing is a literary technique used to hint at future plot developments.
academic_context: 문학/예술 — 표현 기법, 과학 — 실험 기법

definition_en: the application of scientific knowledge for practical purposes, especially in industry
example_sentence: Advancements in medical technology have drastically improved surgical outcomes.
academic_context: 과학/사회 — 정보 통신 기술, 문명 발달의 도구

definition_en: lasting for only a limited period of time; not permanent
example_sentence: The pain from the injection should only be temporary.
academic_context: 물리/의학 — 일시적인 현상, 역사 — 임시 정부

definition_en: to regularly or frequently behave in a particular way or have a certain characteristic
example_sentence: Hot air tends to rise because it is less dense than cold air.
academic_context: 과학/통계 — 일반적 경향성(Tendency) 서술

definition_en: stretched tight; unable to relax because of nervousness; a set of forms taken by a verb to indicate time
example_sentence: The diplomatic relations between the two countries remained incredibly tense.
academic_context: 문법 — 과거/현재/미래 시제(Tense), 문학 — 서사적 긴장감

definition_en: the state of being stretched tight; mental or emotional strain
example_sentence: Surface tension allows some insects to walk on water without sinking.
academic_context: 물리 — 표면 장력, 문학/역사 — 갈등과 긴장 상태

definition_en: forming or situated at the end or extremity of something; (of a disease) predicted to lead to death
example_sentence: The object reached its terminal velocity as it fell through the atmosphere.
academic_context: 물리 — 종단 속도(Terminal velocity), 생물/의학 — 말기 질환

definition_en: to bring to an end
example_sentence: The company reserves the right to terminate the contract without prior notice.
academic_context: 법/비즈니스 — 계약 및 절차의 법적 종료, 과학 — 반응 종결

definition_en: a stretch of land, especially with regard to its physical features
example_sentence: The army struggled to advance through the mountainous and rugged terrain.
academic_context: 지구과학/지리 — 토양과 지형 분석

definition_en: an area of land under the jurisdiction of a ruler or state
example_sentence: Wolves fiercely defend their hunting territory from rival packs.
academic_context: 생태학 — 동물의 세력권, 역사/정치 — 국가의 주권 영토

definition_en: a book or other written or printed work, regarded in terms of its content rather than its physical form
example_sentence: You must quote directly from the text to support your literary analysis.
academic_context: 문학/작문 — 원문 및 분석 대상이 되는 글

definition_en: the subject of a talk, a piece of writing, a person's thoughts, or an exhibition; a topic
example_sentence: The destructive nature of ambition is a major theme in Shakespeare's Macbeth.
academic_context: 문학 — 작품의 핵심 주제나 교훈, 에세이 — 전반적 논제

definition_en: a supposition or a system of ideas intended to explain something, especially one based on general principles independent of the thing to be explained
example_sentence: Einstein's theory of relativity revolutionized our understanding of space and time.
academic_context: 과학 — 과학적 사실로 검증된 체계(Theory vs. Hypothesis)

definition_en: treatment intended to relieve or heal a disorder
example_sentence: Cognitive behavioral therapy is highly effective in treating anxiety disorders.
academic_context: 심리/의학 — 정신 및 신체 치료 요법

definition_en: by that means; as a result of that
example_sentence: The plants perform photosynthesis, thereby producing oxygen for the atmosphere.
academic_context: 에세이/과학 — 인과 관계 연결 접속 부사

definition_en: a statement or theory that is put forward as a premise to be maintained or proved; a long essay or dissertation
example_sentence: The first paragraph of your essay must contain a clear, arguable thesis statement.
academic_context: 작문 — 에세이의 핵심 주장(Thesis statement), 학술 논문

definition_en: a statement of an intention to inflict pain, injury, damage, or other hostile action on someone in retribution for something done or not done; a person or thing likely to cause damage or danger
example_sentence: Global warming poses a severe threat to coastal cities around the world.
academic_context: 환경/사회 — 위험 요소, 군사/안보 위협

definition_en: any of the distinct types of material of which animals or plants are made, consisting of specialized cells and their products
example_sentence: Muscle tissue is responsible for producing movement in the human body.
academic_context: 생물학 — 세포 모임(세포 -> 조직 -> 기관 -> 개체)

definition_en: the general character or attitude of a place, piece of writing, situation, etc.
example_sentence: The author uses a sarcastic tone to critique the political system.
academic_context: 문학/작문 — 화자의 태도(Attitude) 및 어조(Tone)

definition_en: a matter dealt with in a text, discourse, or conversation; a subject
example_sentence: The topic sentence of a paragraph should clearly state its main idea.
academic_context: 작문 — 소주제문(Topic sentence), 논의 대상

definition_en: to find or discover by investigation; a mark, object, or other indication of the existence or passing of something
example_sentence: Scientists use radioactive isotopes to trace the movement of carbon in ecosystems.
academic_context: 역사 — 기원 추적, 화학/생물 — 극미량 물질, 동위원소 추적

definition_en: to follow the course or trail of (someone or something), typically in order to find them or note their location
example_sentence: Researchers attach GPS collars to wolves to track their migration patterns.
academic_context: 과학/지리 — 데이터 및 위치의 지속적 추적/기록

definition_en: the action of buying and selling goods and services; to exchange
example_sentence: International trade allows countries to access goods they cannot produce domestically.
academic_context: 경제/지리 — 국제 무역 및 교환 경제

definition_en: the transmission of customs or beliefs from generation to generation, or the fact of being passed on in this way
example_sentence: Oral storytelling is an important tradition in many Native American cultures.
academic_context: 사회/역사 — 문화적 전승 및 관습

definition_en: a distinguishing quality or characteristic, typically one belonging to a person
example_sentence: Eye color is a physical trait determined by the genes inherited from your parents.
academic_context: 생물학 — 유전 형질(Genetic trait), 심리학 — 성격 특성

definition_en: an instance of buying or selling something; a business deal
example_sentence: The bank keeps a detailed record of every financial transaction made by its clients.
academic_context: 경제/경영 — 자본 및 상거래 행위

definition_en: to move from one place to another; to convey from one person, place, or situation to another
example_sentence: Conduction is the transfer of heat through direct contact between materials.
academic_context: 물리 — 에너지/열의 전달, 일반 — 학교나 계좌 이체

definition_en: to make a thorough or dramatic change in the form, appearance, or character of
example_sentence: A caterpillar will completely transform into a butterfly during metamorphosis.
academic_context: 수학/물리 — 도형/에너지의 변환, 생물 — 형태 변이

definition_en: the carrying of people, goods, or materials from one place to another
example_sentence: Public transit systems, like buses and subways, help reduce urban traffic.
academic_context: 지리/경제 — 대중교통(Mass transit), 천문 — 행성 통과

definition_en: the process or a period of changing from one state or condition to another
example_sentence: Adolescence is a critical period of physical and psychological transition.
academic_context: 작문 — 문단 연결(Transition words), 사회 — 체제 전환

definition_en: to express the sense of (words or text) in another language; to convert or be converted into another form or medium
example_sentence: Ribosomes read the mRNA sequence and translate it into a functional protein.
academic_context: 언어 — 텍스트 번역, 생물 — 단백질 번역(Translation)

definition_en: the action or process of transmitting something or the state of being transmitted
example_sentence: Washing hands frequently is the best way to prevent the transmission of viruses.
academic_context: 보건 — 질병 전염, 통신/물리 — 신호 및 에너지 전송

definition_en: to cause (something) to pass on from one place or person to another
example_sentence: Mosquitoes can transmit serious diseases like malaria to humans.
academic_context: 생물/의학 — 바이러스 매개 전염, 기술 — 전파 전송

definition_en: to take or carry (people or goods) from one place to another by means of a vehicle, aircraft, or ship
example_sentence: Red blood cells transport oxygen from the lungs to the rest of the body.
academic_context: 생물 — 세포 내 물질 수송(Active/passive transport), 경제 — 물류

definition_en: a formally concluded and ratified agreement between countries
example_sentence: The Treaty of Versailles formally ended World War I.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 국제 법률 및 평화 협정

definition_en: a general direction in which something is developing or changing
example_sentence: The scatter plot reveals a clear upward trend in global temperatures.
academic_context: 통계/과학 — 그래프 상의 경향성 및 추세선 파악

definition_en: a formal examination of evidence by a judge, typically before a jury; a test of the performance, qualities, or suitability of someone or something
example_sentence: The scientists conducted a clinical trial to ensure the new vaccine was safe.
academic_context: 법 — 배심원 재판, 과학 — 반복 실험의 횟수(Trial)

definition_en: to cause (an event or situation) to happen or exist
example_sentence: A sudden drop in atmospheric pressure can trigger a severe storm.
academic_context: 과학/역사 — 연쇄 반응 및 사건의 기폭제(원인)

definition_en: being or happening at the end of a process; final; the best or most extreme of its kind
example_sentence: The ultimate goal of the negotiations was to establish lasting peace in the region.
academic_context: 논증 — 최종 결론 및 궁극적 목적 지칭

definition_en: to discover (something previously secret or unknown); to remove a cover or covering from
example_sentence: Archaeologists recently managed to uncover the ruins of an ancient temple.
academic_context: 연구/역사 — 새로운 사실, 유물, 증거의 발견

definition_en: to experience or be subjected to (something, typically something unpleasant, painful, or arduous)
example_sentence: Caterpillars undergo a dramatic physical transformation to become butterflies.
academic_context: 과학 — 화학적/생물학적 상태 변화 경험

definition_en: to be the cause or basis of (something); to be situated under
example_sentence: A complex psychological trauma may underlie the character's erratic behavior.
academic_context: 철학/심리 — 겉으로 드러나지 않은 근본 원인(Underlying cause)

definition_en: to damage or weaken (someone or something), especially gradually or insidiously
example_sentence: The newly discovered evidence serves to undermine the prosecution's entire case.
academic_context: 논증 — 상대방의 논리나 권위, 가설의 타당성 약화

definition_en: to commit oneself to and begin (an enterprise or responsibility); take on
example_sentence: The government will undertake a massive infrastructure project to rebuild the highways.
academic_context: 연구/프로젝트 — 거대 과제나 연구의 시작

definition_en: to make or become united, uniform, or whole
example_sentence: The leader's speech helped to unify the divided nation during the crisis.
academic_context: 역사 — 국가의 통일, 물리 — 통일장 이론(Unified field theory)

definition_en: being the only one of its kind; unlike anything else
example_sentence: Each person's fingerprint contains a completely unique pattern of ridges.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 고유한 특성 강조

definition_en: an individual thing or person regarded as single and complete but which can also form an individual component of a larger or more complex whole
example_sentence: The cell is the most basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms.
academic_context: 수학/과학 — 측정 단위, 구성 요소, 교육 — 단원

definition_en: of, affecting, or done by all people or things in the world or in a particular group; applicable to all cases
example_sentence: Gravity is a universal force that affects all matter in the universe.
academic_context: 철학/문학 — 보편적 진리 및 주제, 물리 — 만유인력

definition_en: all existing matter and space considered as a whole; the cosmos
example_sentence: Many astronomers study the expansion of the universe after the Big Bang.
academic_context: 천문학 — 우주 전체 기원과 구조

definition_en: in, relating to, or characteristic of a city or town
example_sentence: The Industrial Revolution caused a massive shift from rural living to urban environments.
academic_context: 지리/역사 — 도시화(Urbanization), 인구 밀집 지역

definition_en: to try earnestly or persistently to persuade (someone) to do something; a strong desire or impulse
example_sentence: Environmentalists urge governments to adopt stricter carbon emission standards.
academic_context: 논증/연설문 — 화자의 강력한 행동 촉구

definition_en: the action of using something or the fact of being used; the way in which a word or phrase is normally and correctly used
example_sentence: The graph shows a significant increase in internet usage over the past decade.
academic_context: 문법 — 올바른 어휘 용법, 통계 — 자원 사용량

definition_en: the state of being useful, profitable, or beneficial; public services like electricity or water
example_sentence: Economists measure the utility that a consumer gains from purchasing a good.
academic_context: 경제 — 효용 가치, 사회 — 공공 수도/전기 인프라

definition_en: to make practical and effective use of
example_sentence: Plants utilize energy from the sun to convert carbon dioxide into glucose.
academic_context: 에세이/연구 — 도구, 기법, 자원의 효과적인 사용 명시

definition_en: a space entirely devoid of matter; to clean with a vacuum cleaner
example_sentence: Sound cannot travel through a vacuum because there are no particles to carry the waves.
academic_context: 물리학 — 진공 상태, 역사 — 권력의 공백(Power vacuum)

definition_en: of uncertain, indefinite, or unclear character or meaning
example_sentence: The instructions were too vague, leaving the students confused about the assignment.
academic_context: 작문 — 명확하지 않은 논지 비판

definition_en: having a sound basis in logic or fact; reasonable or cogent; legally binding
example_sentence: To form a valid conclusion, the experiment must be carefully controlled.
academic_context: 논리학/연구 — 논증의 타당성(Validity) 및 증명

definition_en: the quality of being logically or factually sound; soundness or cogency
example_sentence: Critics questioned the validity of the survey because the sample size was too small.
academic_context: 과학/논술 — 가설 검증, 실험 및 주장의 논리적 건전성

definition_en: the regard that something is held to deserve; the importance, worth, or usefulness of something; a numerical amount
example_sentence: In this algebraic equation, you must find the value of x.
academic_context: 수학/과학 — 수치(Value), 윤리 — 도덕적 가치관

definition_en: not consistent or having a fixed pattern; liable to change; an element, feature, or factor that is liable to vary or change
example_sentence: In an experiment, you should only change one independent variable at a time.
academic_context: 수학 — 변수(Variable), 과학 — 통제/조작 변인

definition_en: to differ in size, amount, degree, or nature from something else of the same general class
example_sentence: The symptoms of the disease can vary greatly from one patient to another.
academic_context: 과학/통계 — 데이터 간의 차이 및 변동성 묘사

definition_en: of very great extent or quantity; immense
example_sentence: The continent of Africa contains a vast array of different cultures and languages.
academic_context: 지리/역사 — 영토의 넓이나 자원의 방대함 표현

definition_en: a thing used for transporting people or goods; a thing used to express, embody, or fulfill something
example_sentence: Art is often used as a vehicle for expressing complex political ideas.
academic_context: 문학/예술 — 은유적 수단 및 매개체(Vehicle of expression)

definition_en: the speed of something in a given direction
example_sentence: Velocity is a vector quantity because it involves both speed and a specific direction.
academic_context: 물리 — 속도(스피드+방향의 벡터 값)

definition_en: relating to or in the form of words; spoken rather than written
example_sentence: A good speaker uses both verbal communication and nonverbal body language.
academic_context: 언어학/심리 — 의사소통 방식

definition_en: to make sure or demonstrate that (something) is true, accurate, or justified
example_sentence: Scientists must replicate the experiment to verify the accuracy of the original results.
academic_context: 연구/과학 — 데이터 및 결과의 검증

definition_en: a particular form of something differing in certain respects from an earlier form or other forms of the same type of thing
example_sentence: The historian compared the French and English versions of the treaty.
academic_context: 문헌 조사 — 문헌의 다양한 판본이나 수정본 비교

definition_en: against (especially in sports and legal use); as opposed to; in contrast to
example_sentence: The debate topic focused on the benefits of renewable energy versus fossil fuels.
academic_context: 토론/논술 — 두 개념의 뚜렷한 대립 구도 설정

definition_en: a ship or large boat; a hollow container; a tube or canal in which a body fluid is contained and conveyed or circulated
example_sentence: Blood vessels, such as arteries and veins, transport blood throughout the body.
academic_context: 생물/의학 — 혈관(Blood vessel), 식물의 물관

definition_en: traveling through (a place) en route to a destination; by way of; by means of
example_sentence: The virus can easily spread from person to person via airborne droplets.
academic_context: 과학/지리 — 이동 경로, 정보 전달의 매개/수단 지칭

definition_en: to break or fail to comply with (a rule or formal agreement); to treat (something sacred) with irreverence or disrespect
example_sentence: Searching a student's locker without probable cause may violate their privacy rights.
academic_context: 법/윤리 — 헌법/권리 침해 및 규칙 위반

definition_en: almost or nearly as described, but not completely or according to strict definition; not physically existing as such but made by software to appear to do so
example_sentence: The museum offers a virtual tour so you can view the exhibits online from home.
academic_context: 컴퓨터 과학 — 컴퓨터상 시뮬레이션, 물리 — 가상상

definition_en: behavior showing high moral standards; a quality considered morally good or desirable in a person
example_sentence: Patience is considered a crucial virtue when conducting long-term scientific research.
academic_context: 철학/윤리 — 도덕적 가치(Virtue ethics), 인물의 성격 장점

definition_en: an infective agent that typically consists of a nucleic acid molecule in a protein coat and is able to multiply only within the living cells of a host
example_sentence: Unlike bacteria, a virus requires a living host cell in order to reproduce.
academic_context: 생물/의학 — 병원체 구별 (Virus vs. Bacteria)

definition_en: able to be seen; able to be perceived or noticed easily
example_sentence: Some stars are so far away that they are not visible to the naked eye.
academic_context: 물리 — 가시광선(Visible light), 명백한 증거

definition_en: the faculty or state of being able to see; the ability to think about or plan the future with imagination or wisdom
example_sentence: The founders of the nation had a clear vision of a democratic society.
academic_context: 생물 — 시각 체계, 역사 — 리더의 이상/통찰력

definition_en: relating to seeing or sight
example_sentence: Graphs and charts are helpful visual aids during a presentation.
academic_context: 예술 — 시각 예술, 교육/발표 — 시각 자료

definition_en: absolutely necessary or important; essential; full of energy; lively
definition_ko: 필수적인, 생명 유지에 필요한, 활력이 넘치는
example_sentence: The heart is a vital organ that constantly pumps blood through the body.
academic_context: 의학 — 활력 징후(Vital signs), 논증 — 절대적 중요성 강조

definition_en: the amount of space that a substance or object occupies; a book forming part of a work or series
example_sentence: You can calculate the volume of a rectangular prism by multiplying length, width, and height.
academic_context: 수학/과학 — 부피 공식, 문헌 — 백과사전 등의 권수

definition_en: done, given, or acting of one's own free will; working, done, or maintained without payment
example_sentence: Breathing can be controlled through voluntary muscle action, but it usually occurs automatically.
academic_context: 생물 — 수의근(Voluntary muscle), 사회 — 자발적 참여

definition_en: susceptible to physical or emotional attack or harm; (of a person) in need of special care, support, or protection
example_sentence: Elderly people and infants are the most vulnerable to extreme heat waves.
academic_context: 생태학 — 취약종, 사회복지 — 취약 계층, 보안 — 취약점

definition_en: to produce or provide (a natural, agricultural, or industrial product); to give way to arguments, demands, or pressure
example_sentence: The agricultural revolution allowed farmers to significantly increase their crop yield.
academic_context: 화학 — 수득률(Yield), 역사 — 영토/권력 양도

definition_en: an area or stretch of land having a particular characteristic, purpose, or use, or subject to particular restrictions
example_sentence: The equator is located in the tropical zone, which receives direct sunlight year-round.
academic_context: 지리/기상학 — 기후대(Climate zone), 도시 계획 — 구역 설정

definition_en: to increase in speed or rate of occurrence
example_sentence: The introduction of the new catalyst will accelerate the chemical reaction.
academic_context: 물리 — 가속도(acceleration), 경제 — 성장 촉진

definition_en: to gather together or acquire an increasing number or quantity of
example_sentence: Toxins can accumulate in the food chain, affecting top predators the most.
academic_context: 과학 — 물질의 축적, 경제 — 부/자본의 축적

definition_en: the state of being correct or precise
example_sentence: Students must check the accuracy of their calculations before submitting the lab report.
academic_context: 수학/과학 — 측정의 정확도 (Accuracy vs. Precision)

definition_en: to accept or admit the existence or truth of
example_sentence: The author takes time to acknowledge the contributions of her research assistants.
academic_context: 논증 — 반론 수용, 논문 — 감사의 글(Acknowledgments)

definition_en: to make (something) active or operative
example_sentence: The enzyme is required to activate the metabolic pathway in the cell.
academic_context: 생물/화학 — 활성화 에너지(Activation energy), 세포 반응

definition_en: the fact or condition of being addicted to a particular substance, thing, or activity
example_sentence: Psychology studies the behavioral and biological roots of drug addiction.
academic_context: 보건/심리학 — 물질 의존증 및 행동 중독

definition_en: next to or adjoining something else
example_sentence: In a right triangle, the cosine of an angle is the ratio of the adjacent side to the hypotenuse.
academic_context: 기하학 — 인접각, 인접변(Adjacent side)

definition_en: in the process of developing from a child into an adult
example_sentence: The book explores the emotional struggles typical of the adolescent experience.
academic_context: 심리/사회 — 발달 심리학, 청소년기

definition_en: to officially attach or connect (a subsidiary group or a person) to an organization
example_sentence: The local hospital is affiliated with the university's medical school.
academic_context: 사회/경제 — 기관 제휴 및 협력 네트워크

definition_en: ready or likely to attack or confront; pursuing one's aims and interests forcefully
example_sentence: The empire's aggressive expansion strategy led to a series of lengthy wars.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 침략 전쟁, 생물 — 동물의 공격성

definition_en: to cause (someone) to feel isolated or estranged
example_sentence: The leader's harsh new policies threatened to alienate his core supporters.
academic_context: 사회/심리 — 사회적 소외(Alienation), 마르크스주의

definition_en: to place or arrange (things) in a straight line; to give support to (a person, organization, or cause)
example_sentence: The curriculum is designed to align with the state's educational standards.
academic_context: 천문학 — 천체의 정렬, 사회 — 규정이나 기준에 부합함

definition_en: to occur in turn repeatedly; a person who acts as a deputy or substitute
example_sentence: The region's climate is characterized by alternate periods of drought and heavy rain.
academic_context: 수학 — 엇각(Alternate interior angles), 물리 — 교류(Alternating current)

definition_en: relating to or using analysis or logical reasoning
example_sentence: To succeed in chemistry, students must develop strong analytical skills to interpret complex data.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 분석적 사고(Analytical thinking), 에세이

definition_en: not identified by name; of unknown name
example_sentence: The medieval poem was written by an anonymous author.
academic_context: 문학/역사 — 작자 미상의 문헌, 사회 — 익명 설문 조사

definition_en: the study of human societies and cultures and their development
example_sentence: Anthropology helps us understand the evolution of human social structures over thousands of years.
academic_context: 사회과학 — 인류의 진화 및 문화 연구

definition_en: the technical equipment or machinery needed for a particular activity or purpose
example_sentence: The students set up the distillation apparatus before beginning the chemistry experiment.
academic_context: 과학 — 실험 장비 일체, 정치 — 국가 기구(State apparatus)

definition_en: relevant or appropriate
example_sentence: Newton's laws of motion are applicable to objects on Earth as well as in space.
academic_context: 수학/과학 — 공식 및 이론의 현실 적용

definition_en: the art or practice of designing and constructing buildings; the complex or carefully designed structure of something
example_sentence: Gothic architecture is characterized by its pointed arches and flying buttresses.
academic_context: 역사/예술 — 건축 양식, 기술 — 시스템 구조

definition_en: a collection of historical documents or records providing information about a place, institution, or group of people
example_sentence: The historian spent months reading letters in the national archive.
academic_context: 역사/문헌 — 1차 사료 보관 및 정보 검색

definition_en: to express (an idea or feeling) fluently and coherently; having or showing the ability to speak fluently and coherently
example_sentence: A strong essay must clearly articulate its main argument in the introduction.
academic_context: 작문/발표 — 생각의 명확한 서술 및 논리적 전개

definition_en: made or produced by human beings rather than occurring naturally, especially as a copy of something natural
example_sentence: Farmers sometimes use artificial fertilizers to increase crop yields.
academic_context: 과학 — 인공위성, 인공지능(AI), 환경 — 인공 화합물

definition_en: to state a fact or belief confidently and forcefully
example_sentence: The author asserts that the fall of the empire was due to economic instability.
academic_context: 논술 — 화자의 강력한 주장 및 전제 제시

definition_en: a useful or valuable thing, person, or quality; property owned by a person or company
example_sentence: Strong critical thinking skills are a valuable asset in college.
academic_context: 경제 — 자산과 부채(Assets and liabilities), 장점

definition_en: a respiratory condition marked by spasms in the bronchi of the lungs, causing difficulty in breathing
example_sentence: Air pollution can trigger severe attacks in patients who suffer from asthma.
academic_context: 생물/보건 — 호흡기 질환 및 인체 면역계

definition_en: lack of equality or equivalence between parts or aspects of something
example_sentence: The asymmetry of the painting creates a sense of tension and movement.
academic_context: 미술 — 비대칭, 과학/수학 — 대칭 결여

definition_en: the action or state of going regularly to or being present at a place or event
example_sentence: Regular attendance is crucial for keeping up with the fast-paced curriculum.
academic_context: 학교 생활 — 출결 관리

definition_en: of undisputed origin; genuine
example_sentence: The historian confirmed that the handwritten letter was an authentic document from the Civil War.
academic_context: 역사/문헌 — 사료의 진위성 판별

definition_en: the right or condition of self-government; freedom from external control or influence
example_sentence: The colony fought a long war to gain political autonomy from the empire.
academic_context: 정치/역사 — 자치 정부, 심리 — 자율적 행동

definition_en: knowledge or perception of a situation or fact
example_sentence: The campaign was designed to raise awareness about the dangers of climate change.
academic_context: 사회/심리 — 사회적 문제 인식, 환경 보호 캠페인

definition_en: a member of a large group of unicellular microorganisms which have cell walls but lack organelles and an organized nucleus
example_sentence: Antibiotics are drugs designed to destroy or slow down the growth of harmful bacteria.
academic_context: 생물/의학 — 미생물 생태계, 질병의 원인

definition_en: a fence or other obstacle that prevents movement or access; a circumstance or obstacle that keeps people or things apart
example_sentence: The lack of reliable internet access is a major educational barrier for students in rural areas.
academic_context: 지리 — 물리적 장벽(산맥), 사회 — 불평등 구조, 언어 장벽

definition_en: involving, relating to, or emphasizing behavior
example_sentence: The psychologist studied the behavioral changes in rats when exposed to high stress.
academic_context: 심리학/생물학 — 동물 행동학, 행동주의 심리학

definition_en: relating to biology or living organisms
example_sentence: The museum has an extensive collection of biological specimens from the Amazon rainforest.
academic_context: 과학 — 생물학적 메커니즘, 진화, 생태계

definition_en: a line that marks the limits of an area; a dividing line
example_sentence: The river forms a natural boundary between the two states.
academic_context: 지리/지구과학 — 국경, 지각판의 경계(Plate boundary)

definition_en: a sudden, dramatic, and important discovery or development
example_sentence: The discovery of the DNA double helix was a major breakthrough in the field of genetics.
academic_context: 과학/역사 — 과학적 대발견, 기술적 혁신

definition_en: to produce offspring; a stock of animals or plants within a species having a distinctive appearance
example_sentence: Farmers selectively breed certain plants to create crops that are resistant to drought.
academic_context: 생물 — 선택적 교배(Selective breeding), 동물의 번식

definition_en: to determine (the amount or number of something) mathematically
example_sentence: You must use the Pythagorean theorem to calculate the length of the triangle's hypotenuse.
academic_context: 수학/물리 — 수식 계산, 결과 도출

definition_en: an organized course of action to achieve a particular goal, typically a political or social one
example_sentence: The politician launched a grassroots campaign to reform the local education system.
academic_context: 정치/역사 — 선거 운동, 군사 원정

definition_en: a person who applies for a job or is nominated for election
example_sentence: The presidential candidate gave a speech outlining his plans for economic recovery.
academic_context: 정치 — 선거 후보, 화학 — 결합 후보 물질

definition_en: the power or ability to do something
example_sentence: The new telescope has the capability to capture images of distant galaxies.
academic_context: 과학/기술 — 기계나 시스템의 성능적 역량

definition_en: the chemical element of atomic number 6, a nonmetal which has two main forms (diamond and graphite) and which also occurs in impure form in charcoal, soot, and coal
example_sentence: All known life forms on Earth are based on the element carbon.
academic_context: 과학 — 유기 화학, 탄소 순환(Carbon cycle), 이산화탄소

definition_en: to place in a particular class or group
example_sentence: Biologists categorize living organisms based on their shared genetic characteristics.
academic_context: 과학/정보 — 데이터나 생물 종의 체계적 분류

definition_en: relating to or acting as a cause
example_sentence: The study failed to establish a direct causal link between the two variables.
academic_context: 논리/연구 — 인과 관계 (Causal relationship)

definition_en: the smallest structural and functional unit of an organism, typically microscopic
example_sentence: Plant cells have a rigid cell wall, whereas animal cells do not.
academic_context: 생물 — 세포 분열, 세포 소기관

definition_en: typical of a particular person, place, or thing; a feature or quality
example_sentence: A distinguishing characteristic of mammals is their ability to produce milk.
academic_context: 문학/과학 — 인물의 성격, 종의 고유한 형태적 속성

definition_en: to move continuously or freely through a closed system or area
example_sentence: The heart pumps to circulate blood throughout the body.
academic_context: 생물/물리 — 혈액 순환(Circulatory system), 대기 순환

definition_en: a person not in the armed services or the police force
example_sentence: During the war, many innocent civilians were forced to flee their homes.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 전시 상황의 민간인 피해(Civilian casualties)

definition_en: relating to ancient Greek or Latin literature, art, or culture; traditional in form
example_sentence: The architecture of the government building was heavily inspired by classical Greek temples.
academic_context: 역사/예술 — 고전주의, 뉴턴의 고전 역학(Classical mechanics)

definition_en: to arrange (a group of people or things) in classes or categories according to shared qualities or characteristics
example_sentence: Scientists classify organisms based on their evolutionary relationships.
academic_context: 생물 — 분류학, 문헌 자료의 체계화

definition_en: relating to the observation and treatment of actual patients rather than theoretical or laboratory studies
example_sentence: Before a new drug is approved, it must undergo extensive clinical trials to ensure its safety.
academic_context: 의학/심리 — 임상 시험(Clinical trial), 환자 직접 진단

definition_en: the action of working with someone to produce or create something
example_sentence: The scientific paper was the result of a close collaboration between researchers at two universities.
academic_context: 연구/수행평가 — 조별 과제 및 학제 간 연구 연계

definition_en: done by people acting as a group
example_sentence: The team's victory was a result of their collective effort and strong communication.
academic_context: 사회/정치 — 집단주의(Collectivism), 노조의 단체 교섭

definition_en: relating to or characteristic of a colony or colonies
example_sentence: The Boston Tea Party was a major event protesting British colonial rule in America.
academic_context: 역사 — 식민주의(Colonialism), 식민지 시대

definition_en: a country or area under the full or partial political control of another country, typically a distant one, and occupied by settlers from that country; a community of animals
example_sentence: Ants are highly social insects that live together in a large organized colony.
academic_context: 역사 — 개척지, 생물 — 군집/군락 생활

definition_en: an upright pillar, typically cylindrical and made of stone or concrete, supporting an entablature, arch, or other structure; a vertical arrangement of figures or other information
example_sentence: Add up all the numbers in the right-hand column to find the total sum.
academic_context: 건축 — 고전 건축 양식의 기둥, 수학/컴퓨터 — 행렬의 열

definition_en: fighting between armed forces; to take action to reduce, destroy, or prevent (something undesirable)
example_sentence: Scientists are working to develop new vaccines to combat the spread of the virus.
academic_context: 역사 — 교전, 환경/보건 — 질병이나 오염과의 싸움

definition_en: a joining or merging of different parts or qualities in which the component elements are individually distinct
example_sentence: The safe can only be opened by entering the correct combination of numbers.
academic_context: 수학 — 순열과 조합(Combinations), 화학 — 결합

definition_en: an expression of opinions or offering of explanations about an event or situation; a descriptive spoken account
example_sentence: The novel serves as a sharp social commentary on the inequality of wealth.
academic_context: 문학/작문 — 인용구 이후에 이어지는 글쓴이의 주관적 해석/설명

definition_en: the activity of buying and selling, especially on a large scale
example_sentence: The construction of the new canal greatly facilitated international commerce.
academic_context: 경제/지리 — 교역 네트워크, 전자 상거래(E-commerce)

definition_en: a person who supports or believes in the principles of communism
example_sentence: During the Cold War, the world was divided by capitalist and communist ideologies.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 마르크스주의, 소련 및 동구권 체제

definition_en: closely and neatly packed together; dense
example_sentence: Bone tissue is incredibly compact and dense, which makes it very strong.
academic_context: 과학 — 물질의 밀도 및 압축성, 역사 — 협정(Compact)

definition_en: (of a person or thing) able to be likened to another; similar
example_sentence: The climate in this region is comparable to that of the Mediterranean.
academic_context: 논술 — 두 대상의 객관적 유사성 제시

definition_en: measured or judged by estimating the similarity or dissimilarity between one thing and another; relative
example_sentence: The students conducted a comparative study of the two different political systems.
academic_context: 사회/생물 — 비교 해부학, 비교 정치학 등 학문 분야

definition_en: to force or oblige (someone) to do something
example_sentence: The overwhelming evidence compelled the jury to find the defendant guilty.
academic_context: 법/논술 — 설득력 있는(Compelling) 근거 묘사

definition_en: to give (someone) something, typically money, in recognition of loss, suffering, or injury incurred; to make up for (something unwelcome or unpleasant) by exerting an opposite force or effect
example_sentence: If one of your senses is lost, the others often become sharper to compensate.
academic_context: 생물 — 감각 기관의 보상 작용, 법/경제 — 손해 배상

definition_en: the ability to do something successfully or efficiently
example_sentence: Students must demonstrate basic competence in mathematics before advancing to calculus.
academic_context: 교육/심리 — 학업 성취 기준 및 능력

definition_en: relating to or characterized by competition; having or displaying a strong desire to be more successful than others
example_sentence: A monopoly occurs when a company controls a market, eliminating competitive pricing.
academic_context: 경제 — 자유 경쟁 시장, 생물 — 생태계 내 자원 경쟁

definition_en: the state or quality of being intricate or complicated
example_sentence: The complexity of the human brain makes it difficult for neuroscientists to fully map it.
academic_context: 과학/문학 — 플롯의 얽힘, 시스템의 고차원적 구조

definition_en: the action or fact of complying with a wish or command
example_sentence: The laboratory was shut down for failure to maintain strict compliance with safety regulations.
academic_context: 법/규정 — 실험실 안전 수칙이나 법적 기준 준수

definition_en: to make (something) more difficult or confusing by causing it to be more complex
example_sentence: Introducing too many variables into an experiment will only complicate the results.
academic_context: 연구/의학 — 교란 변수, 합병증(Complications)

definition_en: to act in accordance with a wish or command
example_sentence: All students must strictly comply with the school's academic honesty policy.
academic_context: 규정 — 지침/명령에 대한 순응

definition_en: to write or create (a work of art, especially music or poetry); to constitute or make up
example_sentence: Water is composed of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.
academic_context: 과학 — 물질의 화학적 구성, 예술 — 창작

definition_en: very unusual or remarkable
example_sentence: Marie Curie made extraordinary contributions to the fields of physics and chemistry.
academic_context: 문학/역사 — 역사적 업적 및 특이한 현상 강조

definition_en: reaching a high or the highest degree; very great
example_sentence: Organisms that live in deep sea vents have adapted to extreme heat and pressure.
academic_context: 과학/지리 — 극한 환경, 통계 — 극단치

definition_en: cloth or other material produced by weaving or knitting fibers; the essential structure of anything
example_sentence: Widespread corruption threatened to tear apart the social fabric of the nation.
academic_context: 사회/문학 — 사회 구조의 은유적 표현 (Social fabric)

definition_en: well known from long or close association; commonly or already known
example_sentence: Before conducting the lab, students must become familiar with the safety protocols.
academic_context: 독해/안내문 — 사전 배경지식의 인지 상태

definition_en: extreme scarcity of food
example_sentence: The prolonged drought led to a devastating famine that affected millions of people.
academic_context: 역사/지리 — 자연재해 및 식량 위기(e.g., Irish Potato Famine)

definition_en: to draw irresistibly the attention and interest of (someone)
example_sentence: The complex mechanics of the human brain continue to fascinate scientists today.
academic_context: 심리/일반 — 강한 학문적 흥미 유발

definition_en: causing death; leading to failure or disaster
example_sentence: A minor miscalculation in the bridge's design proved to be a fatal flaw.
academic_context: 의학/공학 — 치사율, 시스템의 치명적 오류

definition_en: extreme tiredness resulting from mental or physical exertion or illness; weakness in materials
example_sentence: The structural failure of the airplane wing was caused by metal fatigue.
academic_context: 의학 — 신체적/정신적 소모, 공학 — 재료의 마모(Metal fatigue)

definition_en: an unattractive or unsatisfactory feature; responsibility for an accident or misfortune; an extended break in a body of rock
example_sentence: Earthquakes typically occur when stress is suddenly released along a geological fault line.
academic_context: 지구과학 — 단층(Fault line), 논증 — 논리적 오류

definition_en: possible to do easily or conveniently
example_sentence: The committee must determine if the proposed environmental project is economically feasible.
academic_context: 연구/경영 — 타당성 조사(Feasibility study)

definition_en: (of soil or land) producing or capable of producing abundant vegetation or crops; capable of breeding
example_sentence: Ancient civilizations often developed in river valleys because of the highly fertile soil.
academic_context: 역사/생물 — 비옥한 토지, 생식 능력

definition_en: literature in the form of prose, especially short stories and novels, that describes imaginary events and people
example_sentence: Unlike a biography, a work of fiction does not have to be factually accurate.
academic_context: 문학 — 문학 장르의 구분 (Fiction vs. Non-fiction)

definition_en: having or displaying an intense or ferocious aggressiveness; showing a heartfelt and powerful intensity
example_sentence: The two competing companies have been locked in a fierce rivalry for market dominance.
academic_context: 역사/생물 — 격렬한 경쟁, 육식 동물의 야생성

definition_en: a number, especially one which forms part of official statistics; a diagram or shape; a person of a particular kind
example_sentence: Figure 1 illustrates the rapid decline in the local bee population over ten years.
academic_context: 전 과목 — 도표/삽화 지칭, 수학적 수치, 역사적 인물

definition_en: a mark, fault, or other imperfection that mars a substance or object; a fundamental weakness in an argument
example_sentence: The critic pointed out a major logical flaw in the author's primary argument.
academic_context: 논증/토론 — 논리적 오류 지적, 공학 — 구조적 결함

definition_en: a substance that has no fixed shape and yields easily to external pressure; a gas or (especially) a liquid
example_sentence: In physics, both liquids and gases are classified as fluids because they flow.
academic_context: 물리/의학 — 유체 역학(Fluid dynamics), 체액

definition_en: to predict or estimate (a future event or trend)
example_sentence: Economists forecast that inflation will continue to rise throughout the next quarter.
academic_context: 기상학/경제 — 날씨/경제 동향의 데이터 기반 예측

definition_en: to make or shape (a metal object) by heating it in a fire or furnace; to create (a relationship or new conditions); to produce a copy or fake
example_sentence: The two formerly hostile nations managed to forge a strong political alliance.
academic_context: 역사 — 동맹 체결, 법학 — 문서 위조

definition_en: the action of forming or process of being formed; a structure or arrangement of something
example_sentence: Geologists study rock layers to understand the formation of the Earth's crust.
academic_context: 지구과학/화학 — 지형/화합물의 생성 과정

definition_en: a numerical quantity that is not a whole number; a small or tiny part, amount, or proportion of something
example_sentence: The chemical process extracts only a tiny fraction of the precious metal from the ore.
academic_context: 수학/화학 — 분수 계산, 미량의 비율

definition_en: the cracking or breaking of a hard object or material
example_sentence: An X-ray revealed a severe fracture in the patient's left femur.
academic_context: 의학/지질학 — 뼈의 골절, 암석의 균열

definition_en: (of an object) easily broken or damaged
example_sentence: The coral reef is a highly fragile ecosystem that is easily damaged by pollution.
academic_context: 생태/사회 — 생태계나 경제 체제의 약한 내구성

definition_en: the resistance that one surface or object encounters when moving over another
example_sentence: Air resistance is a form of fluid friction that slows down falling objects.
academic_context: 물리학/역사 — 마찰력, 국가/집단 간의 대립

definition_en: a system of millions or billions of stars, together with gas and dust, held together by gravitational attraction
example_sentence: The Milky Way is the galaxy that contains our Solar System.
academic_context: 지구과학/천문학 — 우주 구조 및 천체

definition_en: relating to genes or heredity
example_sentence: Genetic mutation is a key mechanism that drives biological evolution.
