academic_context: 과학/생물 — 유전 형질(Genetic trait), 유전자 변형

definition_en: truly what something is said to be; authentic
example_sentence: The historian verified that the manuscript was a genuine document from the 18th century.
academic_context: 역사/문헌 — 사료의 진위성(Authenticity) 판별

definition_en: the study of the physical features of the earth and its atmosphere, and of human activity as it affects and is affected by these
example_sentence: Understanding the local geography is crucial for studying the ancient civilization's trade routes.
academic_context: 사회과학/지리 — 지형, 기후, 인구 분포의 관계

definition_en: the science that deals with the earth's physical structure and substance, its history, and the processes that act on it
example_sentence: Geology helps us understand how mountain ranges were formed over millions of years.
academic_context: 지구과학 — 암석, 지각 변동, 판구조론

definition_en: the branch of mathematics concerned with the properties and relations of points, lines, surfaces, solids, and higher dimensional analogs
example_sentence: In geometry, you must use theorems and postulates to prove the properties of shapes.
academic_context: 수학 — 도형의 성질, 공간, 증명

definition_en: a movement of part of the body, especially a hand or the head, to express an idea or meaning
example_sentence: The ambassador made a gesture of goodwill to ease the political tension.
academic_context: 심리/사회 — 비언어적 의사소통(Non-verbal communication)

definition_en: to conduct the policy, actions, and affairs of a state, organization, or people
example_sentence: The Constitution outlines the fundamental rules by which the nation will be governed.
academic_context: 정치/역사 — 정부 체제, 통치 원리

definition_en: taking place or progressing slowly or by degrees
example_sentence: Evolution is typically a gradual process that occurs over millions of years.
academic_context: 과학/역사 — 점진적 변화(Gradual change) 분석

definition_en: relating to visual art, especially involving drawing, engraving, or lettering; giving a vivid picture with explicit detail
example_sentence: The textbook contains several graphic representations of the statistical data.
academic_context: 수학/컴퓨터 — 시각화된 데이터, 문학 — 생생한 묘사

definition_en: deep sorrow, especially that caused by someone's death
example_sentence: The poem explores the universal theme of grief after the loss of a loved one.
academic_context: 문학/심리 — 인간의 감정 및 애도 과정

definition_en: (of income, profit, or interest) without deduction of tax or other contributions; total
example_sentence: Gross Domestic Product (GDP) is a key indicator of a nation's economic health.
academic_context: 경제 — 총생산(Gross product), 총수익

definition_en: a place on the coast where vessels may find shelter; to keep (a thought or feeling) in one's mind secretly
example_sentence: Pearl Harbor was the site of a devastating surprise attack during World War II.
academic_context: 지리/역사 — 항구 도시의 지정학적 중요성

definition_en: the combination of simultaneously sounded musical notes to produce chords; agreement or concord
example_sentence: The philosopher envisioned a utopian society where all classes lived in perfect harmony.
academic_context: 문학/사회 — 집단 간의 화합, 예술 — 미적 조화

definition_en: the process or period of gathering in crops
example_sentence: The agricultural revolution allowed ancient societies to produce a massive surplus harvest.
academic_context: 역사/지리 — 농업의 발달, 식량 자원

definition_en: a danger or risk
example_sentence: Toxic chemicals in the laboratory pose a serious health hazard to the researchers.
academic_context: 과학/환경 — 위험 물질(Hazardous materials), 자연 재해

definition_en: a half of a sphere; a half of the earth, usually as divided into northern and southern halves by the equator
example_sentence: Summer in the Northern Hemisphere coincides with winter in the Southern Hemisphere.
academic_context: 지리/지구과학 — 북반구/남반구, 생물 — 대뇌 반구

definition_en: the passing on of physical or mental characteristics genetically from one generation to another
example_sentence: Gregor Mendel's experiments with pea plants laid the foundation for the study of heredity.
academic_context: 생물 — 유전의 법칙, 형질 유전

definition_en: property that is or may be inherited; an inheritance; valued objects and qualities passed down from previous generations
example_sentence: The ancient ruins are preserved as a crucial part of the country's cultural heritage.
academic_context: 역사/사회 — 문화유산(Cultural heritage)

definition_en: the line at which the earth's surface and the sky appear to meet; the limit of a person's mental perception or experience
example_sentence: Reading literature from different cultures can significantly expand your intellectual horizons.
academic_context: 지구과학 — 지평선/토양층, 문학 — 인식의 범위 확장

definition_en: parallel to the plane of the horizon; at right angles to the vertical
example_sentence: In a coordinate plane, the x-axis represents the horizontal line.
academic_context: 수학/물리 — x축(Horizontal axis), 수평 방향

definition_en: a regulatory substance produced in an organism and transported in tissue fluids to stimulate specific cells or tissues into action
example_sentence: Insulin is a vital hormone that regulates the amount of glucose in the blood.
academic_context: 생물/의학 — 내분비계, 체내 화학 물질 메커니즘

definition_en: a person who receives or entertains other people as guests; an animal or plant on or in which a parasite or commensal organism lives
example_sentence: A virus relies entirely on the cellular machinery of its host to replicate.
academic_context: 생물학 — 기생과 숙주(Parasite and host) 관계

definition_en: unfriendly; antagonistic; of or belonging to a military enemy
example_sentence: The treaty was signed to prevent further conflict between the two hostile nations.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 적대국 간의 대립(Hostility), 극단적 환경

definition_en: lack of knowledge or information
example_sentence: Prejudice and discrimination are often rooted in deep societal ignorance.
academic_context: 사회/철학 — 정보 및 교육의 부재, 지적 결여

definition_en: a false idea or belief; a deceptive appearance or impression
example_sentence: The optical illusion made the straight lines appear to be curved.
academic_context: 심리학/물리 — 착시(Optical illusion), 철학적 허상

definition_en: resistant to a particular infection or toxin owing to the presence of specific antibodies or sensitized white blood cells; protected or exempt
example_sentence: Vaccines stimulate the body's immune system to produce antibodies against a specific disease.
academic_context: 생물/보건 — 면역 체계(Immune system), 법 — 면책 특권

definition_en: to weaken or damage something (especially a human faculty or function)
example_sentence: Drinking alcohol will significantly impair a person's ability to drive a vehicle safely.
academic_context: 의학/보건 — 신체/인지 기능의 손상(Impairment)

definition_en: the conclusion that can be drawn from something, although it is not explicitly stated; a likely consequence
example_sentence: The author discusses the ethical implications of using artificial intelligence in healthcare.
academic_context: 논문/연구 — 연구 결과가 시사하는 바(Implications)

definition_en: a sudden strong and unreflective urge or desire to act; a driving or motivating force
example_sentence: Nerve cells transmit information throughout the body via electrical impulses.
academic_context: 생물 — 신경 자극(Nerve impulse), 물리 — 충격량

definition_en: a very young child or baby
example_sentence: The psychological study focused on the cognitive development of the infant during its first year.
academic_context: 심리/보건 — 영유아 발달, 영아 사망률(Infant mortality)

definition_en: the process of infecting or the state of being infected; an infectious disease
example_sentence: White blood cells act as the body's primary defense against bacterial infection.
academic_context: 생물/보건 — 바이러스 및 세균 감염

definition_en: a general increase in prices and fall in the purchasing value of money
example_sentence: Central banks often raise interest rates in order to control high inflation.
academic_context: 경제학 — 화폐 가치 하락 및 물가 상승, 우주 팽창(Inflation theory)

definition_en: to receive (money, property, or a title) as an heir at the death of the previous holder; to derive (a quality, characteristic, or predisposition) genetically from one's parents
example_sentence: Offspring inherit half of their genetic material from each parent.
academic_context: 생물 — 유전 형질의 전달, 역사/법 — 재산 상속

definition_en: to drive or force (a liquid, especially a drug or vaccine) into a person or animal's body with a syringe
example_sentence: The doctor will inject the vaccine directly into the patient's muscle.
academic_context: 보건/의학 — 약물 투여 방식, 경제 — 자본 주입

definition_en: to do physical harm or damage to (someone)
example_sentence: Failing to wear safety goggles in the lab can permanently injure your eyes.
academic_context: 안전 규정 — 물리적 손상 및 부상 경고

definition_en: to ask for information from someone; to investigate; look into
example_sentence: The committee was established to inquire into the causes of the economic collapse.
academic_context: 연구/과학 — 탐구 학습(Inquiry-based learning)

definition_en: an innate, typically fixed pattern of behavior in animals in response to certain stimuli
example_sentence: Birds build complex nests completely by instinct, without having to be taught.
academic_context: 생물/심리 — 동물 행동학, 선천적 행동 패턴

definition_en: a tool or implement, especially one for delicate or scientific work; a measuring device
example_sentence: A barometer is a scientific instrument used to measure atmospheric pressure.
academic_context: 과학 — 실험 도구(Scientific instrument), 문학 — 수단

definition_en: to protect (something) by interposing material that prevents the loss of heat or the intrusion of sound
example_sentence: Fat layers beneath the skin help insulate marine mammals in freezing ocean waters.
academic_context: 물리/생물 — 열/전기의 차단(Insulator), 생태적 적응

definition_en: the faculty of reasoning and understanding objectively, especially with regard to abstract or academic matters
example_sentence: The philosopher was highly respected for his sharp intellect and logical arguments.
academic_context: 철학/심리 — 이성적 사고 및 인지 능력

definition_en: to take part or intervene in an activity without invitation or necessity; to prevent a process from continuing
example_sentence: Strong winds can interfere with the radio signals sent by the weather station.
academic_context: 물리 — 파동의 간섭(Interference), 정치 — 내정 간섭

definition_en: situated within or inside; relating to the inside
example_sentence: Geologists use seismic waves to study the composition of the Earth's interior.
academic_context: 지구과학/기하학 — 내부 구조, 내각(Interior angle)

definition_en: to divide (something) by passing or lying across it; (of two or more lines) to cross or meet
example_sentence: A point where two lines intersect is called the vertex in geometry.
academic_context: 수학 — 기하학적 교차점, 선의 교차, 교집합

definition_en: closely acquainted; familiar, close; involving very close connection
example_sentence: The diary provides an intimate look into the author's daily struggles and fears.
academic_context: 문학/역사 — 개인적이고 내밀한 기록물(사료) 분석

definition_en: to enter (a country or region) so as to subjugate or occupy it; to intrude on
example_sentence: An invasive species can severely disrupt a local ecosystem when it invades a new habitat.
academic_context: 역사 — 군사적 침공, 생태학 — 외래종의 생태계 교란

definition_en: a complete list of items such as property, goods in stock, or the contents of a building
example_sentence: The laboratory manager must conduct a strict inventory of all chemical supplies each month.
academic_context: 경영/경제 — 재고자산 관리, 연구 — 물품 목록

definition_en: opposite or contrary in position, direction, order, or effect
example_sentence: In physics, there is an inverse relationship between the pressure and volume of a gas.
academic_context: 수학/과학 — 반비례 관계(Inverse proportion), 역함수

definition_en: unable to be seen; not visible to the eye
example_sentence: Microorganisms like bacteria are invisible to the naked eye and require a microscope.
academic_context: 물리/생물 — 비가시적 입자 및 파동(Invisible light)

definition_en: a newspaper or magazine that deals with a particular subject or professional activity; a daily record of events
example_sentence: The researcher published his groundbreaking findings in a prestigious medical journal.
academic_context: 연구 — 동료 평가를 거친 학술지(Peer-reviewed journal)

definition_en: the activity or profession of writing for newspapers, magazines, or news websites or preparing news to be broadcast
example_sentence: Investigative journalism plays a crucial role in exposing government corruption.
academic_context: 사회/언론학 — 대중 매체 및 언론의 사회적 기능

definition_en: of, by, or appropriate to a court or judge
example_sentence: The Supreme Court is the highest authority in the judicial branch of the government.
academic_context: 정치/법학 — 사법부(Judicial branch), 삼권 분립

definition_en: a point where two or more things are joined
example_sentence: Nerve impulses are transmitted from one neuron to another across a tiny gap at the synaptic junction.
academic_context: 생물/기술 — 시냅스 접합부, 회로의 분기점

definition_en: the official power to make legal decisions and judgments; the extent of this power
example_sentence: The local police have no jurisdiction over crimes committed on federal property.
academic_context: 법학/정치 — 권력의 한계 및 법적 권한 범위

definition_en: just behavior or treatment; the quality of being fair and reasonable; the administration of the law
example_sentence: The philosopher argued that a society must be built on the foundational principle of equal justice.
academic_context: 철학/법학 — 형사/사회적 정의, 법의 집행

definition_en: of, for, or relating to young people
example_sentence: The psychological study focused on the primary causes of juvenile delinquency.
academic_context: 사회/법 — 청소년 범죄(Juvenile delinquency), 생물 — 유생

definition_en: of, at, toward, or from the side or sides
example_sentence: The earthquake caused severe lateral movement, shaking the buildings from side to side.
academic_context: 과학/기하학 — 측면 표면적(Lateral surface area), 생물 — 측선

definition_en: the angular distance of a place north or south of the earth's equator; scope for freedom of action or thought
example_sentence: Climate zones are largely determined by a region's latitude and its distance from the equator.
academic_context: 지리/지구과학 — 위도와 경도, 기후의 결정 요소

definition_en: the way in which the parts of something are arranged or laid out
example_sentence: The visual layout of a poster presentation should clearly separate the text and the graphs.
academic_context: 작문/발표 — 문서 서식 및 프레젠테이션 시각 구도

definition_en: a traditional story sometimes popularly regarded as historical but unauthenticated; an extremely famous person; a map key
example_sentence: You must check the map's legend to understand what the different symbols represent.
academic_context: 지리/과학 — 도표와 지도의 범례(Key/Legend), 문학 — 설화

definition_en: laws, considered collectively; the process of making or enacting laws
example_sentence: The new environmental legislation imposes strict penalties on factories that pollute the rivers.
academic_context: 정치/사회 — 입법 과정, 법안

definition_en: the state of being free within society from oppressive restrictions imposed by authority
example_sentence: The constitution guarantees the fundamental civil liberties of every citizen.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 시민의 자유(Civil liberty), 계몽주의

definition_en: arranged in or extending along a straight or nearly straight line; progressing in a single series of steps
example_sentence: A linear equation is an algebraic equation that forms a straight line when graphed.
academic_context: 수학 — 선형 함수/방정식, 문학 — 선형적 서사 구조

definition_en: relating to language or linguistics
example_sentence: The anthropologist analyzed the linguistic similarities between the two isolated tribes.
academic_context: 인문학 — 언어학(Linguistics), 구조주의

definition_en: taking words in their usual or most basic sense without metaphor or allegory
example_sentence: The literal meaning of the phrase differs greatly from its commonly used idiomatic meaning.
academic_context: 독해/문학 — 직설적(Literal) 의미 vs 비유적(Figurative) 의미

definition_en: the ability to read and write; competence or knowledge in a specified area
definition_ko: 글을 읽고 쓸 줄 아는 능력, (특정 분야의) 지식
example_sentence: Improving computer literacy is essential for preparing students for the modern workforce.
academic_context: 교육/사회 — 문맹률 탈피, 정보 해독 능력(Media literacy)

definition_en: written works, especially those considered of superior or lasting artistic merit; printed material providing information
example_sentence: A literature review involves summarizing the previous academic research on a specific topic.
academic_context: 연구 — 선행 문헌 조사(Literature review), 인문학 — 문학

definition_en: the angular distance of a place east or west of the meridian at Greenwich, England
example_sentence: To pinpoint an exact location on Earth, you must know both its latitude and its longitude.
academic_context: 지리/지구과학 — 위치 파악을 위한 좌표축

definition_en: of, determined by, relating to, or resembling the moon
example_sentence: A lunar eclipse occurs when the Earth passes directly between the Sun and the Moon.
academic_context: 지구과학/천문학 — 월식, 달의 위상 변화

definition_en: the great size or extent of something; a numerical quantity or value
example_sentence: An earthquake with a magnitude of 7.0 on the Richter scale can cause severe structural damage.
academic_context: 지구과학 — 지진의 규모, 물리 — 스칼라 및 벡터의 크기

definition_en: the ideas, attitudes, or activities that are regarded as normal or conventional; the dominant trend
example_sentence: The alternative political theory eventually gained enough popularity to enter mainstream academic discourse.
academic_context: 사회/문화 — 지배적인 대중문화 및 통념

definition_en: the greater number; more than half
example_sentence: A vast majority of the scientists agreed that the experimental results were conclusive.
academic_context: 정치/통계 — 다수결, 데이터의 과반수

definition_en: a warm-blooded vertebrate animal of a class that is distinguished by the possession of hair or fur, the secretion of milk by females, and (typically) the birth of live young
example_sentence: Despite living entirely in the ocean, a whale is a mammal, not a fish.
academic_context: 생물학 — 동물 분류 체계 (계/문/강/목/과/속/종)

definition_en: required by law or rules; compulsory
example_sentence: Attendance at the lab safety orientation is mandatory for all chemistry students.
academic_context: 규정/학교 — 필수 이수 사항, 의무 조항

definition_en: of, found in, or produced by the sea
example_sentence: Coral reefs support a vast and diverse array of marine life.
academic_context: 생태/지구과학 — 해양 생물학(Marine biology)

definition_en: of or relating to marriage or the relations between husband and wife
example_sentence: The sociological study analyzed the impact of economic stress on marital stability.
academic_context: 사회/인구 통계 — 혼인 상태, 가족 사회학

definition_en: large and heavy or solid; exceptionally large
example_sentence: The asteroid impact created a massive crater in the Earth's surface.
academic_context: 지리/물리 — 질량과 규모의 압도적 크기

definition_en: relating to a mother, especially during pregnancy or shortly after childbirth
example_sentence: The study focused on the critical role of maternal care in early infant development.
academic_context: 생물/심리 — 유전(Maternal DNA), 모성 행동

definition_en: as great, high, or intense as possible or permitted
example_sentence: The function reaches its maximum value at the vertex of the parabola.
academic_context: 수학/물리 — 최대 한계점(Maximum value)

definition_en: the elected head of a city, town, or other municipality
example_sentence: The mayor implemented a new initiative to improve the city's public transportation system.
academic_context: 정치/사회 — 지방 행정부, 도시 관리

definition_en: a person who repairs and maintains machinery; the practicalities or details of something
example_sentence: To write a good essay, a student must understand the mechanics of proper grammar and punctuation.
academic_context: 물리/작문 — 고전 역학(Classical mechanics), 글의 구조적 세부 규칙

definition_en: relating to the Middle Ages
example_sentence: Feudalism was the dominant social and economic system throughout medieval Europe.
academic_context: 역사 — 중세 시대(The Middle Ages) 양식과 제도

definition_en: an agency or means of doing something; the material or form used by an artist; about halfway between two extremes
example_sentence: Sound waves require a physical medium, like air or water, in order to travel.
academic_context: 물리 — 파동의 매질(Medium), 예술/매체 수단

definition_en: a pliable sheetlike structure acting as a boundary, lining, or partition in an organism
example_sentence: The cell membrane acts as a selective barrier, controlling what enters and exits the cell.
academic_context: 생물 — 세포막(Cell membrane), 삼투압 현상

definition_en: a person or company involved in wholesale trade, especially one dealing with foreign countries or supplying merchandise to a particular trade
example_sentence: During the Renaissance, wealthy merchants heavily influenced the politics of Italian city-states.
academic_context: 역사/경제 — 상업의 발달, 중상주의(Mercantilism)

definition_en: the chemical element of atomic number 80, a heavy silvery-white metal which is liquid at ordinary temperatures
example_sentence: Mercury is unique because it is the only metal that is liquid at room temperature.
academic_context: 화학/천문학 — 화학 원소(Hg), 태양계 행성

definition_en: to combine or cause to combine to form a single entity
example_sentence: When two smaller companies merge, they can often increase their overall market share.
academic_context: 경제/지리 — 기업의 인수합병(M&A), 하천의 합류

definition_en: the quality of being particularly good or worthy, especially so as to deserve praise or reward
example_sentence: The committee evaluated the scholarship applications based solely on academic merit.
academic_context: 평가/논술 — 가치 평가 기준, 장단점(Merits and demerits)

definition_en: the chemical processes that occur within a living organism in order to maintain life
example_sentence: Cellular respiration is a crucial part of an organism's metabolism, releasing stored energy.
academic_context: 생물 — 체내 물질 합성 및 분해 과정

definition_en: a system or standard of measurement
example_sentence: In scientific experiments, data is almost exclusively recorded using the metric system.
academic_context: 과학/수학 — 표준 측정 단위계, 평가 지표

definition_en: an optical instrument used for viewing very small objects, such as mineral samples or animal or plant cells
example_sentence: The discovery of cells was made possible by the invention of the compound microscope.
academic_context: 생물/과학 — 미시 세계 관찰 도구

definition_en: seasonal movement of animals from one region to another; movement of people to a new area or country
example_sentence: The harsh winter forces the annual migration of many bird species to warmer southern climates.
academic_context: 생태/역사 — 철새 이동, 인구 집단 이동

definition_en: a solid naturally occurring inorganic substance
example_sentence: Quartz is a very common and extremely hard mineral found in the Earth's crust.
academic_context: 지구과학/영양학 — 지각을 구성하는 암석의 성분, 무기질

definition_en: the smaller number or part, especially a number that is less than half the whole number
example_sentence: Only a small minority of the population voted in the local election.
academic_context: 사회/정치 — 소수 집단, 다수결 원칙의 예외

definition_en: the ability to move or be moved freely and easily
example_sentence: Social mobility refers to the ability of individuals to move between different social classes.
academic_context: 사회 — 사회 이동(Social mobility), 물리 — 운동성

definition_en: a group of atoms bonded together, representing the smallest fundamental unit of a chemical compound
example_sentence: A single water molecule is composed of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.
academic_context: 화학/생물 — 분자 구조, 유기/무기 화합물

definition_en: the quantity of motion of a moving body, measured as a product of its mass and velocity
example_sentence: The rolling boulder gathered momentum as it traveled down the steep hill.
academic_context: 물리 — 운동량 보존의 법칙(Conservation of momentum)

definition_en: the exclusive possession or control of the supply or trade in a commodity or service
example_sentence: The government passed laws to prevent any single corporation from establishing a monopoly in the oil industry.
academic_context: 경제 — 시장 독점(Monopoly), 불공정 거래

definition_en: principles concerning the distinction between right and wrong or good and bad behavior
example_sentence: The novel questions the morality of scientists who manipulate human genetics.
academic_context: 철학/윤리 — 선악 판단, 도덕적 딜레마

definition_en: the state of being subject to death; the number of deaths in a given area or period
example_sentence: Improvements in sanitation and medicine drastically reduced the infant mortality rate.
academic_context: 보건/사회 — 영아 사망률(Infant mortality rate)

definition_en: to provide (someone) with a motive for doing something
example_sentence: A good teacher knows how to motivate students to reach their full academic potential.
academic_context: 심리 — 내적/외적 동기(Intrinsic/extrinsic motivation)

definition_en: having or involving several parts, elements, or members
example_sentence: The survey offered multiple choices for participants to select their preferred language.
academic_context: 수학 — 최소공배수(Least common multiple), 객관식(Multiple choice)

definition_en: to obtain from (a number) another that contains the first number a specified number of times; to increase greatly in number or quantity
example_sentence: Bacteria can multiply rapidly when placed in a warm and nutrient-rich environment.
academic_context: 수학 — 곱셈(Multiplication), 생물 — 세포 증식

definition_en: the changing of the structure of a gene, resulting in a variant form that may be transmitted to subsequent generations
example_sentence: A genetic mutation allowed the moths to blend in perfectly with the soot-covered trees.
academic_context: 생물학 — 유전자 변이, 자연 선택의 원인

definition_en: a traditional story concerning the early history of a people; a widely held but false belief or idea
example_sentence: It is a common myth that humans only use ten percent of their brains.
academic_context: 문학 — 신화학(Mythology), 과학 — 과학적 사실과 통념의 대조

definition_en: a spoken or written account of connected events; a story
example_sentence: The historian crafted a compelling narrative of the battle based on firsthand accounts.
academic_context: 문학/역사 — 서사 구조(Narrative structure), 이야기 전개

definition_en: to plan and direct the route or course of a ship, aircraft, or other form of transport
example_sentence: Early explorers used the stars to help them navigate across the vast oceans.
academic_context: 지리/역사 — 항해술(Navigation), 정보 검색

definition_en: the fact of being required or indispensable
example_sentence: Clean water is an absolute necessity for the survival of any human settlement.
academic_context: 논증 — 논리적 필연성, 생존의 필수 조건

definition_en: to fail to care for properly; to not pay proper attention to
example_sentence: The scientist's theory was rejected because he chose to neglect crucial counter-evidence.
academic_context: 역사/법 — 직무 유기, 변수 무시(Negligible)

definition_en: to try to reach an agreement or compromise by discussion with others
example_sentence: The two countries sent diplomats to negotiate a peaceful end to the conflict.
academic_context: 정치/역사 — 조약 체결 및 평화 교섭

definition_en: a whitish fiber or bundle of fibers that transmits impulses of sensation to the brain or spinal cord; steadiness, courage
example_sentence: Damage to the optic nerve can result in partial or complete loss of vision.
academic_context: 생물 — 신경계(Nervous system), 뇌과학

definition_en: relating to a nerve or the nervous system
example_sentence: Machine learning algorithms are often based on artificial neural networks that mimic the human brain.
academic_context: 생물/컴퓨터 — 신경망(Neural network), 뉴런

definition_en: belonging to a hereditary class with high social or political status; having fine personal qualities
example_sentence: Helium and neon are classified as noble gases because they rarely react with other elements.
academic_context: 역사 — 귀족 계급, 화학 — 비활성 기체(Noble gas)

definition_en: worthy of attention or notice; remarkable
example_sentence: A notable exception to the rule is found in deep-sea ecosystems that do not rely on sunlight.
academic_context: 논증 — 두드러지는 특징이나 예외 상황 강조

definition_en: to inform (someone) of something, typically in a formal or official manner
example_sentence: The university will notify applicants of their admission status by the end of March.
academic_context: 학교/행정 — 공식적인 정보 전달 체계

definition_en: in spite of
example_sentence: Notwithstanding the heavy rain, the students continued their field research.
academic_context: 논술 — 반전 및 대조를 나타내는 고급 접속사/전치사

definition_en: relating to the nucleus of an atom; relating to energy released in nuclear fission or fusion
example_sentence: Nuclear energy provides a massive amount of power without emitting greenhouse gases.
academic_context: 물리/역사 — 핵분열/융합(Nuclear fission/fusion), 냉전

definition_en: the central and most important part of an object, movement, or group; the dense center of an atom or cell
example_sentence: The nucleus of a eukaryotic cell contains the majority of the organism's genetic material.
academic_context: 생물/물리 — 세포핵, 원자핵

definition_en: a substance that provides nourishment essential for growth and the maintenance of life
example_sentence: Nitrogen and phosphorus are essential nutrients that plants absorb from the soil.
academic_context: 생물/보건 — 영양소 순환, 인체의 필수 영양소

definition_en: an act or course of action to which a person is morally or legally bound; a duty or commitment
example_sentence: Citizens have a moral and legal obligation to participate in the democratic process.
academic_context: 사회/법학 — 시민의 권리와 의무

definition_en: to make (someone) legally or morally bound to an action or course of action; to do as (someone) asks in order to please them
example_sentence: The terms of the treaty oblige both nations to reduce their nuclear arsenals.
academic_context: 법/역사 — 법적/도덕적 책임의 강제

definition_en: the action or process of observing something or someone carefully or in order to gain information
example_sentence: Scientific theories begin with careful observation of natural phenomena.
academic_context: 과학 — 과학적 탐구의 첫 단계(Observation)

definition_en: to notice or perceive (something) and register it as being significant; to fulfill or comply with
example_sentence: During a chemical reaction, you should observe any changes in color or temperature.
academic_context: 과학/법 — 현상의 측정, 규정의 준수

definition_en: to preoccupy or fill the mind of (someone) continually, intrusively, and to a troubling extent
definition_ko: 강박 관념을 갖게 하다, 집착하게 하다
example_sentence: Captain Ahab began to obsess over the white whale, leading to his ultimate downfall.
academic_context: 문학/심리 — 인물의 강박적 성격 분석

definition_en: a particular time or instance of an event; a special or noteworthy event, ceremony, or celebration
example_sentence: The signing of the peace treaty was a momentous occasion in world history.
academic_context: 역사 — 특정한 시간적 배경이나 중대한 사건

definition_en: a breach of a law or rule; an illegal act; annoyance or resentment brought about by a perceived insult
example_sentence: Plagiarism is considered a serious academic offense that can lead to expulsion.
academic_context: 법/학교 — 규칙 위반, 군사적 공격(Offense vs. Defense)

definition_en: causing someone to feel deeply hurt, upset, or angry; actively aggressive; attacking
example_sentence: The army launched a massive offensive campaign to retake the captured territory.
academic_context: 역사/사회 — 공격적 작전, 차별적 언행 비판

definition_en: to leave out or exclude (someone or something), either intentionally or forgetfully
example_sentence: Be careful not to omit any crucial variables when recording your experimental data.
academic_context: 작문/연구 — 데이터나 정보의 의도적/실수 누락

definition_en: someone who competes against or fights another in a contest, game, or argument; a rival or adversary
example_sentence: In a formal debate, you must listen carefully to your opponent in order to formulate a rebuttal.
academic_context: 토론/정치 — 경쟁자, 반대 입장을 지닌 세력

definition_en: to disagree with and attempt to prevent, especially by argument
example_sentence: Many environmentalists strongly oppose the construction of the new oil pipeline.
academic_context: 토론/사회 — 특정 정책이나 이론에 대한 반박

definition_en: completely different; of a contrary kind; situated on the other or further side when facing
example_sentence: Newton's third law states that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.
academic_context: 물리 — 반작용력, 논증 — 상반되는 결과/입장

definition_en: resistance or dissent, expressed in action or argument
example_sentence: The proposed tax increase faced fierce opposition from local business owners.
academic_context: 정치/역사 — 정부에 반대하는 야당(The opposition) 및 저항 세력

definition_en: relating to sight, especially in relation to the physical action of light
example_sentence: A microscope uses a series of optical lenses to magnify tiny specimens.
academic_context: 물리 — 광학(Optics), 빛의 굴절 및 반사 장치

definition_en: best or most favorable
example_sentence: Enzymes function best when they are kept at an optimal temperature and pH level.
academic_context: 생물/경제 — 반응을 극대화하는 최적 조건(Optimal condition)

definition_en: to make the best or most effective use of (a situation, opportunity, or resource)
example_sentence: Engineers continually test the wing design to optimize the airplane's fuel efficiency.
academic_context: 수학/컴퓨터 — 알고리즘 효율성 증가, 시스템 최적화

definition_en: by word of mouth; spoken rather than written; relating to the mouth
example_sentence: Before written languages were developed, history was passed down through oral traditions.
academic_context: 역사/문학 — 구비 문학(Oral tradition), 구술 평가

definition_en: the curved path of a celestial object or spacecraft around a star, planet, or moon
example_sentence: The Earth takes roughly 365 days to complete one full orbit around the Sun.
academic_context: 지구과학/물리 — 위성과 행성의 공전, 전자 궤도

definition_en: a part of an organism that is typically self-contained and has a specific vital function
example_sentence: The skin is actually the largest organ in the human body.
academic_context: 생물 — 인체 해부학, 장기(심장, 간 등) 구조

definition_en: relating to or derived from living matter; produced without the use of chemical fertilizers or pesticides
example_sentence: Organic chemistry focuses primarily on the study of carbon-containing compounds.
academic_context: 화학/생물 — 유기 화학(Organic chemistry), 생태학적 물질

definition_en: an individual animal, plant, or single-celled life form
example_sentence: An amoeba is a microscopic, single-celled organism found in damp environments.
academic_context: 생물 — 생명체의 기본 단위부터 개체 전체 지칭

definition_en: the determination of the relative position of something or someone; a person's basic attitude, beliefs, or feelings
example_sentence: The magnetic orientation of ancient rocks provides evidence for plate tectonics.
academic_context: 지구과학/기하학 — 공간적 방향, 사회 — 성적 지향

definition_en: the point or place where something begins, arises, or is derived
example_sentence: Charles Darwin's book, 'On the Origin of Species', revolutionized biology.
academic_context: 수학 — 원점(Origin), 생물/역사 — 기원과 진화

definition_en: to have a specified beginning; to create or initiate (something)
example_sentence: Many English words originate from ancient Latin or Greek roots.
academic_context: 언어학/역사 — 사상이나 발명품, 언어의 출발점

definition_en: a colorless, odorless reactive gas, the chemical element of atomic number 8 and the life-supporting component of the air
example_sentence: During cellular respiration, animals take in oxygen and release carbon dioxide.
academic_context: 화학/생물 — 원소(O), 광합성 및 호흡 작용

definition_en: a single step taken when walking or running; the speed or rate at which something happens, changes, or develops
example_sentence: The rapid pace of technological advancement has drastically changed how we communicate.
academic_context: 역사/문학 — 플롯 전개 속도, 시대 변화의 척도

definition_en: an organism that lives in or on an organism of another species (its host) and benefits by deriving nutrients at the other's expense
example_sentence: A tick is a common parasite that feeds on the blood of mammals.
academic_context: 생태학 — 숙주와 기생(Parasitism) 관계 분석

definition_en: existing only in part; incomplete; favoring one side in a dispute above the other
example_sentence: A lunar eclipse is partial when only a portion of the moon enters the Earth's shadow.
academic_context: 과학/수학 — 부분 분수(Partial fraction), 사회 — 편향성(Partiality)

definition_en: a minute portion of matter; the least possible amount
example_sentence: An electron is a negatively charged subatomic particle found in all atoms.
academic_context: 물리/화학 — 입자 물리학(Particle physics), 소립자

definition_en: used to single out an individual member of a specified group or class; especially great or intense
example_sentence: The researchers focused on one particular species of frog that survives in freezing temperatures.
academic_context: 에세이 — 대상을 좁혀 구체적으로 명시할 때 사용

definition_en: a track that constitutes or serves as a path; a route to or way of achieving something
example_sentence: The optic nerve serves as the sensory pathway from the eye to the brain.
academic_context: 생물/화학 — 대사 경로(Metabolic pathway), 신경 회로

definition_en: the pointed top of a mountain; the point of highest activity, quality, or achievement
example_sentence: The Roman Empire reached the peak of its territorial expansion under Emperor Trajan.
academic_context: 수학/그래프 — 함수의 정점, 역사 — 전성기

definition_en: a person of the same age, status, or ability as another specified person; to look keenly or with difficulty
example_sentence: Before a scientific paper is published, it undergoes a rigorous process of peer review.
academic_context: 학술/연구 — 동료 평가(Peer review), 심리 — 또래 압력(Peer pressure)

definition_en: a rate, number, or amount in each hundred
example_sentence: A high percentage of the Earth's fresh water is locked up in glaciers and ice caps.
academic_context: 수학/통계 — 데이터와 확률을 비율로 설명

definition_en: the ability to see, hear, or become aware of something through the senses; the way in which something is regarded or understood
example_sentence: The media's portrayal of the event heavily influenced public perception of the crisis.
academic_context: 심리 — 감각의 지각(Sensory perception), 문학 — 인식의 차이

definition_en: to carry out, accomplish, or fulfill (an action, task, or function)
example_sentence: Students must perform the chemistry experiment exactly as outlined in the manual.
academic_context: 과학/수학 — 실험 및 연산의 수행

definition_en: the action or process of carrying out or accomplishing an action, task, or function; a display of artistic talent
example_sentence: Lack of sleep can severely negatively impact a student's academic performance.
academic_context: 교육/평가 — 학업 성취도(Academic performance), 기계의 성능

definition_en: lasting or intended to last or remain unchanged indefinitely
example_sentence: Using a permanent marker on the whiteboard will ruin it.
academic_context: 과학 — 비가역적 변화, 역사 — 영구적 조약 체결

definition_en: continuing firmly or obstinately in a course of action in spite of difficulty or opposition; continuing to exist over a prolonged period
example_sentence: The patient complained of a persistent cough that lasted for several weeks.
academic_context: 의학/환경 — 만성적 증상, 자연 분해되지 않는 잔류 물질

definition_en: to cause (someone) to do something through reasoning or argument
example_sentence: The goal of an argumentative essay is to persuade the reader to agree with your thesis.
academic_context: 작문/토론 — 설득력 있는 논리 전개

definition_en: the action or fact of persuading someone or of being persuaded to do or believe something; a belief or set of beliefs
example_sentence: Aristotle outlined three modes of persuasion: ethos, pathos, and logos.
academic_context: 수사학/작문 — 수사학의 기초, 화자의 설득 전략

definition_en: the study of the fundamental nature of knowledge, reality, and existence, especially when considered as an academic discipline
example_sentence: The concept of democracy is deeply rooted in ancient Greek philosophy.
academic_context: 인문/역사 — 사상사, 윤리학 등 학문 분과

definition_en: a person qualified to practice medicine; a medical doctor
example_sentence: The attending physician diagnosed the patient with a severe bacterial infection.
academic_context: 보건/역사 — 의료계 종사자

definition_en: the branch of science concerned with the nature and properties of matter and energy
example_sentence: Newton's laws of motion form the foundation of classical physics.
academic_context: 과학 — 과학 분과(역학, 전자기학 등 다룸)

definition_en: a person who is among the first to explore or settle a new country or area; a person who is among the first to research and develop a new area of knowledge or activity
example_sentence: Marie Curie was a pioneer in the early study of radioactivity.
academic_context: 역사/연구 — 역사적 신대륙 개척, 학술적 신분야 개척

definition_en: the main events of a play, novel, movie, or similar work, devised and presented by the writer as an interrelated sequence; a secret plan
example_sentence: The plot of the novel is driven by the protagonist's desire for revenge.
academic_context: 문학 — 서사 구조(발단-전개-위기-절정-결말)

definition_en: connected with the regions of the earth near the North and South Poles; having electrical or magnetic polarity
example_sentence: Water is a polar molecule, meaning it has a slight positive charge on one end and a negative charge on the other.
academic_context: 화학 — 극성 공유 결합(Polar covalent bond), 지리 — 극지방

definition_en: a painting, drawing, photograph, or engraving of a person, especially one depicting only the face or head and shoulders
example_sentence: The author paints a vivid psychological portrait of a teenager struggling with identity.
academic_context: 예술/문학 — 인물화, 텍스트를 통한 인물의 입체적 묘사

definition_en: to depict (someone or something) in a work of art or literature; to describe in a particular way
example_sentence: The documentary attempts to portray the historical figures as flawed, complex human beings.
academic_context: 문학/예술 — 텍스트나 예술 매체를 통한 현실의 재현

definition_en: to have as belonging to one; own; to have as an ability, quality, or characteristic
example_sentence: Unlike mammals, reptiles do not possess the ability to internally regulate their body temperature.
academic_context: 과학/문학 — 유전적, 물리적 형질/특성의 보유

definition_en: the state of having, owning, or controlling something; an item of property
example_sentence: The treaty demanded that the defeated nation give up all of its overseas colonial possessions.
academic_context: 역사/법 — 영토 점유, 사유 재산권

definition_en: the state of being extremely poor
example_sentence: The economic depression forced millions of families into deep poverty.
academic_context: 사회/경제 — 빈곤선(Poverty line), 불평등

definition_en: of or concerned with the actual doing or use of something rather than with theory and ideas
example_sentence: While the theory sounds good, engineers must consider its practical application in the real world.
academic_context: 과학/공학 — 이론(Theoretical)의 반대 개념, 실제 적용

definition_en: the actual application or use of an idea, belief, or method as opposed to theories about such application; repeated exercise
example_sentence: Slavery was an accepted economic practice in the Southern colonies prior to the Civil War.
academic_context: 역사/사회 — 사회적 관습(Custom) 및 제도적 관행

definition_en: the quality, condition, or fact of being exact and accurate
example_sentence: Scientific instruments must be calibrated regularly to ensure mathematical precision.
academic_context: 과학/수학 — 측정의 일관성 및 정밀도(Precision vs. Accuracy)

definition_en: an animal that naturally preys on others
example_sentence: The lion is an apex predator that plays a crucial role in maintaining the food web.
academic_context: 생태학 — 포식자와 피식자(Predator and prey) 관계

definition_en: a thing predicted; a forecast
example_sentence: The scientist's hypothesis makes a specific, testable prediction about the outcome of the experiment.
academic_context: 과학적 방법론 — 가설에 기반한 결과 예측

definition_en: a greater liking for one alternative over another or others
example_sentence: Consumers are showing a growing preference for eco-friendly products.
academic_context: 경제/사회 — 소비자 선호도(Consumer preference), 선택의 경향

definition_en: preconceived opinion that is not based on reason or actual experience
example_sentence: The author explores how social prejudice can lead to systemic discrimination.
academic_context: 문학/사회 — 인종/성별 등 사회적 편견과 고정관념

definition_en: an amount to be paid for an insurance policy; a sum added to an ordinary price or charge; of exceptional quality
example_sentence: Consumers are often willing to pay a premium for organically grown vegetables.
academic_context: 경제 — 웃돈, 가치의 추가 비용

definition_en: the state or fact of existing, occurring, or being present in a place or thing
example_sentence: The chemical indicator turns blue to confirm the presence of starch in the solution.
academic_context: 과학 — 물질의 존재 여부(Presence vs. Absence)

definition_en: a speech or talk in which a new product, idea, or piece of work is shown and explained to an audience
example_sentence: Your final grade will be based on both the written report and the oral presentation.
academic_context: 교육/평가 — 구두 발표, 시각적 정보 제시

definition_en: to maintain (something) in its original or existing state; keep safe from harm or injury
example_sentence: Historians use special climate-controlled rooms to preserve delicate ancient texts.
academic_context: 역사/환경 — 유물 보존, 생태계 및 자원 보호

definition_en: continuous physical force exerted on or against an object; the use of persuasion, influence, or intimidation to make someone do something
example_sentence: As altitude increases, the atmospheric pressure decreases significantly.
academic_context: 물리/지구과학 — 기압, 수압, 심리/사회 — 또래 압력(Peer pressure)

definition_en: widespread respect and admiration felt for someone or something on the basis of a perception of their achievements or quality
example_sentence: Winning the Nobel Prize brings immense international prestige to a scientist.
academic_context: 역사/사회 — 사회적 지위와 명망

definition_en: to speak and act so as to make it appear that something is the case when in fact it is not
example_sentence: In literature, the unreliable narrator may pretend to be innocent to deceive the reader.
academic_context: 문학 — 등장인물의 기만, 위장

definition_en: widespread in a particular area or at a particular time
example_sentence: Malaria is highly prevalent in tropical regions with large mosquito populations.
academic_context: 보건/사회 — 질병 발병률(Prevalence), 현상의 광범위한 확산

definition_en: to keep (something) from happening or arising
example_sentence: Vaccines are administered to prevent the spread of infectious diseases.
academic_context: 보건/과학 — 질병 통제 및 사고 예방 조치

definition_en: a special right, advantage, or immunity granted or available only to a particular person or group of people
example_sentence: In pre-revolutionary France, the nobility enjoyed the privilege of being exempt from most taxes.
academic_context: 역사/사회 — 신분 제도와 권리 불평등

definition_en: likely to be the case or to happen
example_sentence: The most probable cause of the mass extinction was a massive asteroid strike.
academic_context: 통계/과학 — 확률(Probability), 유력한 가설

definition_en: an established or official way of doing something
example_sentence: Students must follow the correct laboratory procedure to avoid accidents.
academic_context: 과학/규정 — 실험의 단계적 순서 및 매뉴얼

definition_en: important; famous; projecting from something
example_sentence: Alexander Hamilton was a prominent figure in the drafting of the US Constitution.
academic_context: 역사/사회 — 주요 인물(Prominent figure), 눈에 띄는 특징

definition_en: done without delay; immediate; to cause or bring about (an action or feeling)
example_sentence: Read the essay prompt carefully before you begin writing your introduction.
academic_context: 평가/작문 — 글쓰기나 토론의 지시문(Writing prompt)

definition_en: corresponding in size or amount to something else
example_sentence: According to Newton's second law, acceleration is directly proportional to net force.
academic_context: 수학/물리 — 정비례/반비례 관계(Directly/inversely proportional)

definition_en: to put forward (an idea or plan) for consideration or discussion by others
example_sentence: The scientist will propose a new theory to explain the unexpected data.
academic_context: 논술/토론 — 가설, 정책, 해결책의 제시

definition_en: a statement or assertion that expresses a judgment or opinion
example_sentence: The debate centered on the proposition that college education should be free.
academic_context: 논리학/토론 — 찬반 토론의 주제나 명제

definition_en: to keep safe from harm or injury
example_sentence: The Bill of Rights was added to the Constitution to protect individual liberties.
academic_context: 역사/환경 — 권리의 보장, 자연 환경의 보존

definition_en: any of a class of nitrogenous organic compounds that consist of large molecules composed of one or more long chains of amino acids
example_sentence: DNA contains the genetic instructions needed to synthesize proteins within the cell.
academic_context: 생물/화학 — 생명체의 기본 구성 성분

definition_en: a statement or action expressing disapproval of or objection to something
example_sentence: Citizens organized a peaceful protest against the government's unjust taxation policies.
academic_context: 역사/사회 — 민권 운동, 정치적 저항(Boycott, Strike 등)

definition_en: to stimulate or give rise to (a reaction or emotion, typically a strong or unwelcome one) in someone
example_sentence: The controversial new law is likely to provoke outrage among environmentalists.
academic_context: 정치/문학 — 반발이나 특정 감정의 촉발

definition_en: the action of following or pursuing someone or something; an activity of a specified kind, especially a recreational or academic one
example_sentence: The Declaration of Independence lists life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness as unalienable rights.
academic_context: 역사/철학 — 이상이나 목표를 향한 노력

definition_en: to express or measure the quantity of
example_sentence: It is difficult to quantify the exact economic impact of the natural disaster.
academic_context: 수학/연구 — 데이터를 수치로 변환하여 분석

definition_en: relating to, measuring, or measured by the quantity of something rather than its quality
example_sentence: The researchers gathered quantitative data by recording the exact temperatures over a 30-day period.
academic_context: 과학/통계 — 수치화된 데이터 연구 (Quantitative vs. Qualitative)

definition_en: a question, especially one addressed to an official or organization; to ask a question about something, especially in order to express one's doubts about it
example_sentence: The student submitted a query to the librarian regarding how to access academic journals online.
academic_context: 정보 검색 — 데이터베이스(SQL) 및 문헌 검색 질문

definition_en: on the grounds of or connected with the difference in race
example_sentence: The Civil Rights Act of 1964 outlawed racial segregation in schools and public places.
academic_context: 역사/사회 — 인종 차별, 민권 운동, 사회적 불평등

definition_en: prejudice, discrimination, or antagonism directed against someone of a different race based on the belief that one's own race is superior
example_sentence: The novel To Kill a Mockingbird explores the destructive effects of racism in a small Southern town.
academic_context: 사회/역사 — 노예제도, 제국주의의 사회적 결과

definition_en: happening in a short time or at a fast pace
example_sentence: The late 19th century saw rapid industrialization in many parts of the world.
academic_context: 역사/과학 — 급격한 발전(Rapid growth/change)

definition_en: (of an event, situation, or condition) not occurring very often; (of a thing) not found in large numbers and consequently of interest or value
example_sentence: A total solar eclipse is a relatively rare astronomical event.
academic_context: 통계/과학 — 발생 빈도가 낮은 현상, 희귀 원소

definition_en: a measure, quantity, or frequency, typically one measured against some other quantity or measure
example_sentence: The unemployment rate increased during the prolonged economic recession.
academic_context: 수학/경제 — 변화율, 이자율, 단위 시간당 속도

definition_en: an action performed or a feeling experienced in response to a situation or event; a chemical process
example_sentence: Mixing the two chemicals produced an explosive exothermic reaction.
academic_context: 화학/물리 — 화학 반응(Chemical reaction), 반작용(Action and reaction)

definition_en: the world or the state of things as they actually exist, as opposed to an idealistic or notional idea of them
example_sentence: The author contrasts the romanticized myth of the West with the harsh reality of frontier life.
academic_context: 철학/문학 — 이상(Ideal)과 현실(Reality)의 대조

definition_en: a kingdom; a field or domain of activity or interest
example_sentence: The discovery opened up entirely new possibilities in the realm of quantum physics.
academic_context: 문학/학술 — 특정 학문 분야나 주제의 범위

definition_en: an act of violent or open resistance to an established government or ruler
example_sentence: Shays' Rebellion highlighted the weaknesses of the national government under the Articles of Confederation.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 대중의 무장 봉기 및 저항 운동

definition_en: the action of receiving something or the fact of its being received; a written or printed statement acknowledging that something has been paid for
example_sentence: Please keep this email as a receipt confirming the submission of your university application.
academic_context: 경제/행정 — 공식적인 수령의 증거

definition_en: to enlist (someone) in the armed forces; to find new people to join an organization or support a cause
example_sentence: During the war, the military launched a massive campaign to recruit more soldiers.
academic_context: 역사/사회 — 군대 징집, 인력 선발 과정

definition_en: a person who has been forced to leave their country in order to escape war, persecution, or natural disaster
example_sentence: The ongoing civil war has created millions of refugees seeking safety in neighboring countries.
academic_context: 사회/역사 — 전쟁 및 재난으로 인한 대규모 이주민 문제

definition_en: to consider or think of (someone or something) in a specified way; attention to or concern for something
example_sentence: George Washington is widely regarded as one of the most important Founding Fathers.
academic_context: 문학/에세이 — 특정 대상에 대한 평가(be regarded as)

definition_en: a rule or directive made and maintained by an authority; the action or process of regulating or being regulated
example_sentence: The government passed strict environmental regulations to control factory emissions.
academic_context: 법학/경제 — 정부의 시장 개입, 생물 — 유전자 조절(Gene regulation)

definition_en: the way in which two or more concepts, objects, or people are connected; a thing's effect on or relevance to another
example_sentence: The scatter plot displays a positive linear relation between a student's study time and their test scores.
academic_context: 통계/수학 — 변인 간의 상관관계(Correlation/Relation)

definition_en: considered in relation or in proportion to something else; existing or possessing a specified characteristic only in comparison to something else
example_sentence: Concepts like 'hot' and 'cold' are relative terms that depend on a person's baseline perspective.
academic_context: 물리 — 상대성 이론(Theory of Relativity), 비교/대조 분석

definition_en: dependence on or trust in someone or something
example_sentence: The country's heavy reliance on imported oil makes its economy vulnerable to price shocks.
academic_context: 경제/지리 — 특정 자원이나 국가에 대한 종속성

definition_en: a feeling of reassurance and relaxation following release from anxiety or distress; financial or practical assistance given to those in special need or difficulty
example_sentence: The Red Cross sent immediate relief supplies to the region devastated by the earthquake.
academic_context: 지리 — 지형의 고저/기복(Relief map), 사회 — 재난 구호조치

definition_en: relating to or believing in a religion
example_sentence: The First Amendment guarantees the freedom to practice any religious belief without government interference.
academic_context: 역사/사회 — 신념의 자유, 세계의 여러 종교들

definition_en: a part, number, or quantity that is left over
example_sentence: When you divide 10 by 3, the quotient is 3 with a remainder of 1.
academic_context: 수학 — 나눗셈의 나머지(Remainder), 잔여 부분

definition_en: to make an exact copy of; reproduce
example_sentence: Other scientists must be able to replicate your experiment to verify that the results are accurate.
academic_context: 과학/연구 — 바이러스 복제(DNA replication), 실험의 재현성

definition_en: a state in which supreme power is held by the people and their elected representatives, and which has an elected or nominated president rather than a monarch
example_sentence: After overthrowing the king, the nation established a republic governed by elected officials.
academic_context: 정치/역사 — 민주주의 정부 체제(Republic vs. Monarchy)

definition_en: the beliefs or opinions that are generally held about someone or something
example_sentence: The university has a strong reputation for its rigorous engineering program.
academic_context: 사회/역사 — 단체나 개인에 대한 대중적 평가

definition_en: an act of asking politely or formally for something; to ask for
example_sentence: Students must formally request an extension from the professor before the deadline.
academic_context: 학교 생활 — 서류/기간 연장 등의 공식적 절차

definition_en: a thing that is needed or wanted; a thing that is compulsory; a necessary condition
example_sentence: Passing the advanced algebra course is a strict requirement for high school graduation.
academic_context: 교육/평가 — 졸업/입학 조건(Graduation requirement)

definition_en: to retain for future use; to arrange for (a room, seat, etc.) to be kept for the use of a particular person
example_sentence: The federal government established the national park to reserve the land for wildlife conservation.
academic_context: 환경/경제 — 생태계 보호 구역(Nature reserve), 지급 준비금

definition_en: a person who lives somewhere permanently or on a long-term basis
example_sentence: Every resident of the city was asked to participate in the local census.
academic_context: 사회/지리 — 지역 사회 인구 집단

definition_en: to voluntarily leave a job or other position; to accept that something undesirable cannot be avoided
example_sentence: Following the public scandal, the corrupt official was forced to resign from office.
academic_context: 정치/역사 — 관직에서의 사퇴 및 권력 이양

definition_en: to withstand the action or effect of; to try to prevent by action or argument
example_sentence: Some strains of bacteria have evolved to resist the effects of common antibiotics.
academic_context: 생물 — 내성/저항성(Antibiotic resistance), 정치적 저항

definition_en: the refusal to accept or comply with something; the impending effect exerted by one material thing on another
example_sentence: Air resistance is the force that acts in the opposite direction to an object moving through the air.
academic_context: 물리 — 전기/공기 저항, 역사 — 레지스탕스 운동

definition_en: a firm decision to do or not to do something; the action of solving a problem or dispute; the clarity of an image
example_sentence: The conflict reached its resolution when both sides finally signed the peace treaty.
academic_context: 문학 — 플롯의 결말(Resolution), 정치 — UN 결의안

definition_en: to turn to and adopt (a strategy or course of action, especially a disagreeable one) so as to resolve a difficult situation
example_sentence: When diplomatic negotiations failed, the nation decided to resort to military action.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 최후의 수단으로 극단적 방법을 채택(resort to)

definition_en: belonging or relating separately to each of two or more people or things
example_sentence: After the joint meeting concluded, the diplomats returned to their respective countries.
academic_context: 작문/수학 — 짝지어진 항목들의 개별적 매칭 설명

definition_en: separately or individually and in the order already mentioned
example_sentence: The melting and boiling points of water are 0°C and 100°C, respectively.
academic_context: 도표/수치 분석 — 두 개 이상의 값을 순서대로 나열할 때 필수

definition_en: a verbal or written answer; a reaction to something
example_sentence: The plant's growth toward the sunlight is a biological response to its environment.
academic_context: 과학/심리 — 자극과 반응(Stimulus and response)

definition_en: the state or fact of having a duty to deal with something or of having control over someone; moral obligation
example_sentence: It is the government's primary responsibility to ensure the safety and welfare of its citizens.
academic_context: 사회/윤리 — 시민의 역할과 사회적 책무

definition_en: a limiting condition or measure, especially a legal one
example_sentence: The city placed a severe restriction on water usage during the prolonged drought.
academic_context: 사회/생물 — 제도적 통제, 제한 효소(Restriction enzyme)

definition_en: to move back or withdraw, especially as a military force in the face of enemy attack
example_sentence: Outnumbered and outgunned, the general ordered his troops to safely retreat.
academic_context: 역사/군사 — 전투에서의 병력 철수

definition_en: a change to an opposite direction, position, or course of action
example_sentence: The sudden economic boom was a complete reversal of the previous decade's depression.
academic_context: 역사/과학 — 자기장 역전, 정책이나 상황의 뒤바뀜

definition_en: a formal assessment or examination of something with the possibility or intention of instituting change if necessary
example_sentence: Before the final exam, the teacher will hold a comprehensive review of the semester's material.
academic_context: 교육/연구 — 문헌 연구(Literature review), 시험 전 복습

definition_en: the action of revising; a revised edition or form of something
example_sentence: The author made several major revisions to the manuscript before sending it to the publisher.
academic_context: 작문/법 — 에세이 초안 수정 과정, 조약/법의 개정

definition_en: a thing given in recognition of one's service, effort, or achievement
example_sentence: In psychology, positive reinforcement involves giving a reward to encourage a specific behavior.
academic_context: 심리 — 행동주의 학습 이론(Reward and punishment)

definition_en: the quality of being extremely thorough, exhaustive, or accurate; severity or strictness
example_sentence: The scientific community requires mathematical and methodological rigor before accepting a new theory.
academic_context: 학문/연구 — 학문적 엄밀성(Academic rigor)

definition_en: a situation involving exposure to danger; the possibility that something unpleasant or unwelcome will happen
example_sentence: Smoking cigarettes significantly increases the risk of developing lung cancer.
academic_context: 통계/보건 — 발병 확률, 금융 투자에서의 위험 부담

definition_en: a person or thing competing with another for the same objective or for superiority in the same field of activity
example_sentence: During the Cold War, the United States and the Soviet Union were the primary global rivals.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 이권 다툼을 하는 적대국 및 경쟁 세력

definition_en: competition for the same objective or for superiority in the same field
example_sentence: The bitter political rivalry between Hamilton and Jefferson shaped the early American government.
academic_context: 역사 — 정치적 패권 다툼, 제국 간의 갈등

definition_en: the part of a plant that attaches it to the ground; the basic cause, source, or origin of something
example_sentence: To solve the economic crisis, the government must address the root cause of the inflation.
academic_context: 수학 — 제곱근(Square root), 인문 — 문제의 근본 원인

definition_en: having an uneven or irregular surface; not exact or precise; approximate
example_sentence: Before writing the final essay, the student created a rough outline of her main arguments.
academic_context: 작문 — 초안(Rough draft), 통계 — 대략적인 추산치

definition_en: a sequence of actions regularly followed; a fixed program
example_sentence: The laboratory equipment requires routine maintenance to function accurately.
academic_context: 과학/공학 — 정기 점검, 심리 — 반복되는 일상 습관

definition_en: the physical destruction or disintegration of something or the state of disintegrating or being destroyed
example_sentence: Archaeologists spent years excavating the ancient Roman ruins buried under the volcanic ash.
academic_context: 역사 — 고대 문명의 멸망과 유적지(Ruins)

definition_en: connected with God (or the gods) or dedicated to a religious purpose and so deserving veneration
example_sentence: The river Ganges is considered a highly sacred site in the Hindu religion.
academic_context: 역사/인류학 — 종교적 성역 및 불가침의 권리

definition_en: an act of giving up something valued for the sake of something else regarded as more important or worthy
example_sentence: During the war, citizens had to make enormous economic sacrifices to support the military.
academic_context: 역사/문학 — 영웅의 대의를 위한 개인적 포기 및 제물

definition_en: the condition of being protected from or unlikely to cause danger, risk, or injury
example_sentence: You must wear goggles at all times to ensure eye safety in the chemistry lab.
academic_context: 실험실 수칙 — 안전 장비 및 규정(Safety protocols)

definition_en: a small part or quantity intended to show what the whole is like
example_sentence: The researchers collected a random sample of 500 voters to conduct their political poll.
academic_context: 통계/과학 — 표본 추출(Sampling), 실험용 시료

definition_en: a threatened penalty for disobeying a law or rule; official permission or approval for an action
example_sentence: The United Nations imposed strict economic sanctions on the country for violating the peace treaty.
academic_context: 정치/국제 관계 — 국가 간의 무역/외교적 제재

definition_en: an artificial body placed in orbit around the earth or moon or another planet in order to collect information or for communication
example_sentence: The moon is a natural satellite, while the ISS is an artificial one orbiting the Earth.
academic_context: 지구과학/천문학 — 자연/인공 위성, 우주 탐사

definition_en: fulfillment of one's wishes, expectations, or needs, or the pleasure derived from this
example_sentence: The sociological survey measured the overall job satisfaction among factory workers.
academic_context: 심리/경제 — 욕구와 기대치의 충족 지표

definition_en: an action or event regarded as morally or legally wrong and causing general public outrage
example_sentence: The Watergate scandal ultimately led to the resignation of President Richard Nixon.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 공직자의 비리 및 사회적 파장

definition_en: (especially of food, money, or some other resource) insufficient for the demand
example_sentence: Water becomes incredibly scarce in the desert during the long, dry summer months.
academic_context: 경제/지리 — 자원의 희소성(Scarcity), 수요와 공급

definition_en: to throw in various random directions; to separate and move off quickly in different directions
example_sentence: A prism will scatter a single beam of white light into a spectrum of different colors.
academic_context: 물리 — 빛의 산란, 통계 — 산점도(Scatter plot)

definition_en: to test or examine for the presence of something (like a disease); a flat panel or area on an electronic device for viewing
example_sentence: The medical clinic will screen all incoming patients for symptoms of the contagious virus.
academic_context: 보건/유전학 — 유전병 및 바이러스 사전 검사(Screening)

definition_en: critical observation or examination
example_sentence: The scientific paper was subjected to intense scrutiny by a panel of peer reviewers.
academic_context: 학술/법 — 증거나 논문에 대한 엄격하고 비판적인 검토

definition_en: the art of making two- or three-dimensional representative or abstract forms, especially by carving stone or wood or by casting metal or plaster
example_sentence: Michelangelo's 'David' is widely considered a masterpiece of Renaissance marble sculpture.
academic_context: 예술/역사 — 미술사, 3차원 예술 작품

definition_en: to try to find something by looking or otherwise seeking carefully and thoroughly
example_sentence: The algorithm can quickly search through a massive database to find the relevant information.
academic_context: 컴퓨터/정보 — 데이터 검색, 문헌 찾기

definition_en: coming after, less important than, or resulting from someone or something else that is primary
example_sentence: A textbook is a secondary source because it analyzes and summarizes primary historical documents.
academic_context: 역사/연구 — 2차 사료(Secondary source), 부작용

definition_en: the state of being free from danger or threat; the safety of a state or organization against criminal activity such as terrorism, theft, or espionage
example_sentence: Following the cyberattack, the bank heavily invested in upgrading its digital security systems.
academic_context: 정치/기술 — 국가 안보(National security), 사이버 보안

definition_en: each of the parts into which something is or may be divided
example_sentence: The marketing team divided the population into different demographic segments to target their ads.
academic_context: 기하학/통계 — 선분(Line segment), 데이터 그룹의 분할

definition_en: to take hold of suddenly and forcibly; to take (an opportunity or initiative) eagerly and decisively
example_sentence: During the revolution, the rebel forces managed to seize control of the capital city.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 권력 장악(Seize power), 불법 물품 압수

definition_en: not often; rarely
example_sentence: Because the planet's orbit is extremely tilted, eclipses occur very seldom.
academic_context: 통계/분석 — 빈도가 매우 낮음을 강조하는 부정 부사

definition_en: the action or fact of carefully choosing someone or something as being the best or most suitable
example_sentence: Natural selection favors organisms that possess traits best suited to their environments.
academic_context: 생물/문학 — 자연 선택(Natural selection), 지문(Reading selection)

definition_en: relating to or involving the selection of the most suitable or best qualified; choosing carefully
example_sentence: The cell membrane is selectively permeable, allowing only certain molecules to pass through.
academic_context: 생물 — 선택적 투과성, 농업 — 선택적 교배

definition_en: a conference or other meeting for discussion or training; a small group of advanced students in a college studying a subject with a professor
example_sentence: The graduate students attended a weekly seminar to discuss recent advancements in physics.
academic_context: 대학/연구 — 토론 중심의 소규모 학술 수업

definition_en: of a more advanced age; high or higher in rank or status; a student in the final year of high school or college
example_sentence: As a senior researcher, she was responsible for directing the entire laboratory team.
academic_context: 학교/직장 — 12학년(Senior), 서열상 상위 그룹

definition_en: a physical feeling or perception resulting from something that happens to or comes into contact with the body
example_sentence: The nervous system is responsible for transmitting the sensation of pain to the brain.
academic_context: 생물/심리학 — 신체적 자극의 지각 작용

definition_en: a faculty by which the body perceives an external stimulus; a feeling that something is the case; a sane and realistic attitude
example_sentence: Dogs have a highly developed sense of smell that allows them to track scents over long distances.
academic_context: 생물/문학 — 오감(Five senses), 단어의 문맥적 의미

definition_en: quick to detect or respond to slight changes, signals, or influences; delicate
example_sentence: Photographic film is coated with chemicals that are highly sensitive to light exposure.
academic_context: 과학/기기 — 정밀 기기의 높은 감도, 환경/변화에 대한 취약성

definition_en: the quality or condition of being sensitive
example_sentence: The telescope's extreme sensitivity allowed astronomers to detect faint, distant galaxies.
academic_context: 물리/의학 — 관측 기기의 정밀한 측정 한계, 인체의 감도

definition_en: a set of words that is complete in itself; the punishment assigned to a defendant found guilty by a court
example_sentence: The judge handed down a harsh sentence of twenty years in prison for the crime.
academic_context: 문법/법학 — 구문론적 문장, 사법적 유죄 판결

definition_en: forming or viewed as a unit apart or by itself; to cause to move or be apart
example_sentence: The centrifuge is used to separate the different components of the blood based on density.
academic_context: 화학/역사 — 혼합물의 분리, 권력의 분립(Separation of powers)

definition_en: a period of time devoted to a particular activity; a meeting of a deliberative body
example_sentence: Congress held a special session to discuss the national emergency.
academic_context: 정치/역사 — 국회/의회의 회기, 학교 — 학기(Session)

definition_en: (of something bad or undesirable) very great; intense; demanding strictness
example_sentence: The hurricane caused severe damage to the coastal towns.
academic_context: 지구과학/의학 — 기상 이변의 강도, 질병의 심각성

definition_en: of little depth; not exhibiting, requiring, or capable of serious thought
example_sentence: Coral reefs are typically found in warm, shallow waters.
academic_context: 지리 — 수심, 문학/심리 — 피상적인(Shallow) 분석이나 성격

definition_en: a broad piece of metal or another suitable material used for protection; to protect from a danger
example_sentence: The Earth's magnetic field shields the planet from harmful solar radiation.
academic_context: 과학 — 방사선/입자 차폐(Shielding), 역사 — 전투 장비

definition_en: a state or situation in which something needed cannot be obtained in sufficient amounts
example_sentence: The prolonged drought led to a severe water shortage in the region.
academic_context: 경제/지리 — 자원의 희소성, 공급 부족

definition_en: to become or make smaller in size or amount
example_sentence: As the lake evaporates during the summer, its surface area begins to shrink.
academic_context: 과학 — 열수축, 경제 — 시장/자산의 축소

definition_en: occurring, operating, or done at the same time
example_sentence: The pianist is known for her ability to play two different melodies in simultaneous harmony.
academic_context: 수학 — 연립 방정식(Simultaneous equations), 역사 — 동시다발적 사건

definition_en: a surface of which one end or side is at a higher level than another; a rising or falling surface
example_sentence: In a linear equation, the slope represents the rate of change between two variables.
academic_context: 수학 — 일차함수의 기울기(Slope), 지리 — 지형의 경사

definition_en: relating to or determined by the sun
example_sentence: The solar system consists of the sun and the planets that orbit around it.
academic_context: 지구과학/환경 — 태양계(Solar system), 태양 에너지

definition_en: firm and stable in shape; not liquid or fluid; reliable and strong
example_sentence: Ice is the solid state of water, formed when temperatures drop below freezing.
academic_context: 화학/물리 — 고체(Solid state), 논술 — 확실한(Solid) 증거

definition_en: relating to space and the relationship of objects within it
example_sentence: Geometry helps students develop strong spatial reasoning skills.
academic_context: 기하학/물리 — 공간 지각 능력, 지리 — 공간 분포

definition_en: to concentrate on and become expert in a particular subject or skill
example_sentence: In college, she plans to specialize in marine biology.
academic_context: 진로/학문 — 전공 분야, 생물 — 세포의 특화(Specialization)

definition_en: a group of living organisms consisting of similar individuals capable of exchanging genes or interbreeding
example_sentence: Charles Darwin's research focused heavily on how different species adapt to their environments.
academic_context: 생물학 — 생물 분류(종), 진화론

definition_en: an individual animal, plant, piece of a mineral, etc., used as an example of its species or type for scientific study or display
example_sentence: The biologist examined the blood specimen under a high-powered microscope.
academic_context: 과학/의학 — 실험 및 관찰용 시료, 암석 표본

definition_en: a band of colors, as seen in a rainbow, produced by separation of the components of light; a wide range
example_sentence: A prism separates white light into the visible color spectrum.
academic_context: 물리 — 전자기 스펙트럼(Electromagnetic spectrum), 사회 — 의견의 범위

definition_en: to form a theory or conjecture about a subject without firm evidence
example_sentence: Scientists can only speculate about the existence of life on other planets until they find concrete proof.
academic_context: 연구/역사 — 가설 설정, 경제 — 주식/부동산 투기

definition_en: a person or organization that provides funds for a project or activity carried out by another
example_sentence: The university agreed to sponsor the student's research expedition to the Amazon.
academic_context: 연구/사회 — 프로젝트 자금 지원 및 주최

definition_en: performed or occurring as a result of a sudden inner impulse or inclination and without premeditation or external stimulus
example_sentence: A spontaneous chemical reaction occurs without the addition of outside energy.
academic_context: 화학/물리 — 자발적 반응, 생물 — 자연 발생설

definition_en: to open out (something) so as to extend its surface area, width, or length; to extend over a large or increasing area
example_sentence: The invention of the printing press facilitated the rapid spread of knowledge across Europe.
academic_context: 역사/보건 — 문화나 질병의 확산

definition_en: a level of quality or attainment; an idea or thing used as a measure, norm, or model in comparative evaluations
example_sentence: The metric system is the international standard for scientific measurement.
academic_context: 과학/수학 — 표준 측정 단위, 통계 — 표준 편차(Standard deviation)

definition_en: firmly placed or fixed; continuing or developing uniformly or continuously
example_sentence: The graph shows a steady increase in global population over the last century.
academic_context: 통계/과학 — 그래프의 일관된 변화 추이 묘사

definition_en: (of a slope, flight of stairs, angle, ascent, etc.) rising or falling sharply; nearly perpendicular
example_sentence: The demand for oil caused a steep rise in gasoline prices.
academic_context: 수학/지리 — 급격한 경사, 그래프의 급격한 변동

definition_en: the main body or stalk of a plant; to originate in or be caused by
example_sentence: Many modern political conflicts stem from historical border disputes.
academic_context: 생물 — 줄기세포(Stem cell), 논술 — 원인의 도출(Stem from)

definition_en: a widely held but fixed and oversimplified image or idea of a particular type of person or thing
example_sentence: The novel aims to break down the common cultural stereotype associated with immigrants.
academic_context: 사회/문학 — 편견과 고정관념 비판

definition_en: not easily bent or changed in shape; rigid
example_sentence: The plant's cell walls are stiff to provide structural support for the organism.
academic_context: 생물/물리 — 물질의 경직성, 물리적 성질

definition_en: to raise levels of physiological or nervous activity in (the body or any biological system); to encourage interest or activity
example_sentence: Light acts as a sensory stimulus to stimulate the optic nerve.
academic_context: 생물/심리 — 자극과 반응(Stimulus), 경제 — 경기 부양

definition_en: the action or method of storing something for future use
example_sentence: The liver is responsible for the storage of glycogen, which the body uses for energy.
academic_context: 생물/컴퓨터 — 에너지 저장, 데이터 보관소

definition_en: a force tending to pull or stretch something to an extreme or damaging degree; a distinct variety of an organism
example_sentence: Researchers discovered a new strain of the virus that was resistant to the vaccine.
academic_context: 생물 — 바이러스/세균의 변종(Strain), 물리 — 응력과 변형률(Stress and strain)

definition_en: a single thin length of something such as thread, fiber, or wire, especially as twisted together with others
example_sentence: A DNA molecule consists of two complementary strands twisted into a double helix.
academic_context: 생물 — DNA 가닥(Strand), 문학 — 이야기의 갈래

definition_en: a small, narrow river; a continuous flow of liquid, air, or gas
example_sentence: The jet stream is a fast-flowing, narrow air current found in the Earth's atmosphere.
academic_context: 지구과학/지리 — 하천 지형, 기류(Jet stream)

definition_en: the quality or state of being physically strong; a good or beneficial quality or attribute of a person or thing
example_sentence: One major strength of this historical source is its eyewitness perspective.
academic_context: 물리 — 인장 강도(Tensile strength), 논술 — 논거의 강점

definition_en: to make or become stronger
example_sentence: The president sought to strengthen diplomatic ties with neighboring nations.
academic_context: 논증 에세이 — 주장/논리 강화, 과학 — 구조물 보강

definition_en: to be made or be capable of being made longer or wider without tearing or breaking
example_sentence: The Andes mountain range stretches along the entire western coast of South America.
academic_context: 지리 — 산맥이나 영토의 길게 뻗은 형태 묘사

definition_en: demanding that rules concerning behavior are obeyed and observed
example_sentence: The laboratory maintains strict safety protocols when handling hazardous chemicals.
academic_context: 규정/실험 — 통제 및 규정의 엄격함
