definition_en: to hit forcibly and deliberately with one's hand or a weapon or other implement; a refusal to work organized by a body of employees as a form of protest
example_sentence: During the Industrial Revolution, workers often went on strike to protest poor working conditions.
academic_context: 역사/사회 — 노동조합 파업, 과학 — 충돌(운석 등)

definition_en: to remove all coverings from; a long, narrow piece of cloth, paper, plastic, or some other material
example_sentence: The dictator attempted to strip the citizens of their basic human rights.
academic_context: 정치/법 — 권리의 박탈, 지리 — 가늘고 긴 영토(Strip of land)

definition_en: to make great efforts to achieve or obtain something
example_sentence: Scientists constantly strive to find more efficient sources of renewable energy.
academic_context: 연구/논술 — 목표 달성을 위한 학술적/사회적 노력

definition_en: a sudden disabling attack or loss of consciousness caused by an interruption in the flow of blood to the brain; an act of hitting or striking
example_sentence: High blood pressure is a leading cause of heart disease and stroke.
academic_context: 의학/보건 — 심혈관계 질환, 미술 — 붓놀림(Brush stroke)

definition_en: to make forceful or violent efforts to get free of restraint or constriction; to have difficulty handling or coping with
example_sentence: The book details the long struggle for women's suffrage in the United States.
academic_context: 역사/사회 — 권리 쟁취를 위한 사회적 투쟁

definition_en: to take away (a number or amount) from another to calculate the difference
example_sentence: To find the net weight, you must subtract the weight of the container from the total weight.
academic_context: 수학/물리 — 뺄셈 연산, 벡터 차감

definition_en: an outlying district of a city, especially a residential one
example_sentence: Following World War II, many American families moved from urban centers to the suburbs.
academic_context: 지리/역사 — 교외화 현상(Suburbanization)

definition_en: a number of people or things sharing a specified characteristic and following one after the other; the process of inheriting a title, office, or property
example_sentence: Ecological succession explains how a barren landscape gradually becomes a mature forest.
academic_context: 역사 — 왕위 계승, 생태학 — 생태 천이(Ecological succession)

definition_en: to experience or be subjected to (something bad or unpleasant)
example_sentence: The local economy began to suffer heavily as a result of the trade embargo.
academic_context: 역사/경제 — 위기나 손실 경험, 의학 — 질병의 고통

definition_en: the highest point of a hill or mountain; a meeting between heads of government
example_sentence: The leaders of the two nations met at a peace summit to negotiate a ceasefire.
academic_context: 지리 — 산의 정상, 정치/국제 — 양국 정상 회담

definition_en: higher in rank, status, or quality
example_sentence: The army possessed technologically superior weaponry compared to their opponents.
academic_context: 역사/과학 — 우위성 비교, 해부학 — 상반신의(Superior)

definition_en: to observe and direct the execution of (a task, project, or activity)
example_sentence: A licensed scientist must supervise all student experiments in the chemistry lab.
academic_context: 규정/안전 — 실험 및 시험 감독 절차

definition_en: to make (something needed or wanted) available to someone; a stock of a resource from which a person or place can be provided
example_sentence: The law of supply and demand dictates the prices of goods in a free market economy.
academic_context: 경제 — 공급과 수요(Supply and demand), 물류

definition_en: to bear all or part of the weight of; to hold up; to give assistance to, especially financially; to provide evidence for
example_sentence: You must use multiple quotes from the text to support your primary thesis.
academic_context: 작문/논증 — 주장을 뒷받침하는 근거 제시

definition_en: to assume that something is the case on the basis of evidence or probability but without proof or certain knowledge
example_sentence: Let us suppose, for the sake of the mathematical proof, that x equals zero.
academic_context: 수학/논리 — 증명을 위한 가설 설정(Suppose that...)

definition_en: to forcibly put an end to; to prevent the development, action, or expression of
example_sentence: The military was deployed to violently suppress the democratic uprising.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 반란 진압, 생물 — 면역 억제(Immunosuppression)

definition_en: highest in rank or authority; very great or the greatest
example_sentence: The Supreme Court has the final authority to interpret the United States Constitution.
academic_context: 정치/법 — 대법원(Supreme Court), 최고 권력

definition_en: a sudden powerful forward or upward movement, especially by a crowd or by a natural force such as the waves or tide
example_sentence: The invention of the smartphone caused a massive surge in global internet usage.
academic_context: 통계/경제 — 데이터 수치나 수요의 급격한 상승

definition_en: a medical practitioner qualified to practice surgery
example_sentence: The cardiac surgeon successfully performed a highly complex heart bypass operation.
academic_context: 의학/보건 — 의료 전문 직업군

definition_en: the medical treatment of injuries or diseases that involves cutting open a person's body
example_sentence: Advances in robotic technology have made minimally invasive surgery much more common.
academic_context: 의학/과학 — 외과적 수술 및 의료 기술 발전

definition_en: to exceed; be greater than
example_sentence: The company's quarterly profits managed to significantly surpass analysts' predictions.
academic_context: 경제/통계 — 예측치나 한계점 초과 돌파

definition_en: an amount of something left over when requirements have been met; an excess of production or supply over demand
example_sentence: Ancient farming communities traded their crop surplus for other necessary goods.
academic_context: 경제/역사 — 잉여 농산물, 무역 흑자(Trade surplus)

definition_en: to cease resistance to an enemy or opponent and submit to their authority
example_sentence: After a long and brutal siege, the defending army was finally forced to surrender.
academic_context: 역사/정치 — 전쟁 종료, 무조건 항복

definition_en: to be all around (someone or something)
example_sentence: The nucleus is surrounded by cytoplasm, which contains the other cellular organelles.
academic_context: 과학/지리 — 세포 구조 및 지리적 환경 묘사

definition_en: close observation, especially of a suspected spy or criminal
example_sentence: The totalitarian government used mass surveillance to monitor the activities of its citizens.
academic_context: 정치/사회 — 정보 통제 및 사생활 침해 문제

definition_en: to have an idea or impression of the existence, presence, or truth of (something) without certain proof; a person thought to be guilty of a crime or offense
example_sentence: Scientists suspect that the sudden temperature drop was caused by a massive volcanic eruption.
academic_context: 과학 — 확실치 않은 가설 의심, 법 — 범죄 용의자

definition_en: to clean (an area) by brushing away dirt or litter; to move swiftly and smoothly
example_sentence: A deadly plague began to sweep across the continent, decimating the population.
academic_context: 역사/환경 — 질병이나 재난의 광범위한 확산

definition_en: happening quickly or promptly; moving or capable of moving at high speed
example_sentence: The government promised a swift response to the escalating humanitarian crisis.
academic_context: 정치/역사 — 정책이나 결정의 빠른 속도

definition_en: to move or cause to move back and forth or from side to side while suspended or on an axis
example_sentence: The political pendulum often swings between conservative and liberal ideologies over time.
academic_context: 정치 — 여론의 변동(Swing states), 물리 — 진자 운동

definition_en: to change the position, direction, or focus of; a device for making and breaking the connection in an electric circuit
example_sentence: Many consumers have decided to switch from gas-powered vehicles to electric cars.
academic_context: 환경/기술 — 에너지/기기 전환, 물리 — 전기 회로

definition_en: natural aptitude or skill
example_sentence: The Renaissance period produced many individuals with extraordinary artistic talent.
academic_context: 역사/예술 — 예술가 및 위인의 천부적 역량

definition_en: relating to or using technology
example_sentence: The industrial age was defined by massive and rapid technological advancements.
academic_context: 역사/과학 — 기술 발전 및 혁신(Technological advancement)

definition_en: for a limited period of time; not permanently
example_sentence: The library will be temporarily closed for renovations during the winter break.
academic_context: 공지/안내 — 시설 및 서비스의 임시 중단

definition_en: concerned with or involving the theory of a subject or area of study rather than its practical application
example_sentence: Theoretical physics relies heavily on complex mathematics to predict how the universe behaves.
academic_context: 과학/철학 — 이론적 배경(Theoretical framework), 실증 전 단계

definition_en: relating to heat
example_sentence: Geothermal energy harnesses the natural thermal heat generated beneath the Earth's surface.
academic_context: 물리/지구과학 — 열에너지(Thermal energy), 지열 발전

definition_en: complete with regard to every detail; not superficial or partial
example_sentence: Before publishing the paper, the scientists conducted a thorough review of the data.
academic_context: 연구/평가 — 철저한 검토 및 심층적 조사 과정

definition_en: a strip of wood, metal, or stone forming the bottom of a doorway; the magnitude or intensity that must be exceeded for a certain reaction to occur
example_sentence: A neuron will only fire an electrical impulse if the stimulus reaches a certain threshold.
academic_context: 생물/심리 — 자극 한계치(Action potential threshold), 임계점

definition_en: the ability or willingness to tolerate something, in particular the existence of opinions or behavior that one does not necessarily agree with; capacity to endure hardship
example_sentence: Frequent use of painkillers can cause a patient to build up a high tolerance to the drug.
academic_context: 의학 — 약물 내성, 사회 — 문화적 다양성에 대한 관용

definition_en: to allow the existence, occurrence, or practice of (something that one does not necessarily like or agree with) without interference; to endure
example_sentence: Certain species of plants can tolerate extremely high levels of salt in their soil.
academic_context: 생물 — 악조건(추위, 염분 등)을 견디는 생존력

definition_en: a major passage in the body, large bundle of nerve fibers, or other continuous elongated anatomical structure or region; an area of indefinite extent, typically a large one
example_sentence: The digestive tract is a long, continuous tube where food is broken down and absorbed.
academic_context: 생물/해부학 — 소화관(Digestive tract), 호흡기 계통

definition_en: an event causing great suffering, destruction, and distress; a play dealing with tragic events and having an unhappy ending
example_sentence: Shakespeare's 'Hamlet' is a classic example of Renaissance tragedy.
academic_context: 문학 — 비극 장르(Tragedy), 영웅의 몰락 구조

definition_en: causing or characterized by extreme distress or sorrow
example_sentence: The tragic hero typically possesses a fatal flaw that ultimately leads to his downfall.
academic_context: 문학 — 비극적 결함(Tragic flaw), 비극적 영웅

definition_en: (of a material or article) allowing light to pass through so that objects behind can be distinctly seen; easy to perceive or detect
example_sentence: Glass is a transparent material, meaning that light rays can easily pass completely through it.
academic_context: 물리 — 빛의 투과(Transparent, translucent, opaque), 정치 — 투명성

definition_en: the action of transporting someone or something or the process of being transported
example_sentence: The invention of the steam engine revolutionized land and water transportation.
academic_context: 지리/역사 — 대중교통 인프라, 산업화의 핵심 수단

definition_en: a device or enclosure designed to catch and retain animals; to catch or keep in a place from which escape is difficult
example_sentence: Greenhouse gases trap heat from the sun within the Earth's atmosphere, warming the planet.
academic_context: 지구과학/환경 — 온실 효과(Trapping heat)

definition_en: a deeply distressing or disturbing experience; physical injury
example_sentence: The soldiers returned from the war suffering from severe psychological trauma.
academic_context: 심리/의학 — PTSD, 심리적 및 신체적 심각한 손상

definition_en: very great in amount, scale, or intensity
example_sentence: The internet has had a tremendous impact on how information is shared globally.
academic_context: 논증/강조 — 거대한 스케일이나 영향력 표현

definition_en: a great victory or achievement; to achieve a victory or be successful
example_sentence: The successful moon landing was considered a monumental triumph of human engineering.
academic_context: 역사/문학 — 역사적 대승리, 어려움 극복의 성공

definition_en: of, typical of, or peculiar to the tropics (the region near the equator)
example_sentence: Tropical rainforests are known for their hot, humid climates and incredible biodiversity.
academic_context: 지리/기후 — 기후대(Tropical climate), 열대 우림

definition_en: a sum of money charged for teaching or instruction by a school, college, or university
example_sentence: Many students must take out federal loans in order to afford high college tuition costs.
academic_context: 학교 생활/경제 — 대학 학비 및 교육 관련 지출

definition_en: (of two or more people) fully in agreement; (of an opinion, decision, or vote) held or carried by everyone involved
example_sentence: The jury reached a unanimous verdict of 'guilty' after only an hour of deliberation.
academic_context: 법/정치 — 배심원 판결, 의회의 전원 찬성 표결

definition_en: significant as a cause or basis of something but not necessarily manifest or obvious
example_sentence: To fix the economy, the government must address the underlying issue of severe inflation.
academic_context: 분석/연구 — 표면적인 것 외의 근본 원인(Underlying cause/theme)

definition_en: to make (something) more modern or up to date; an act of bringing something or someone up to date
definition_ko: 갱신하다, 최신 정보를 알려주다; 최신 정보
example_sentence: The textbook was recently updated to include the latest discoveries in genetics.
academic_context: 문헌/데이터 — 정보의 최신화 및 교정판 발간

definition_en: to raise (something) to a higher standard, in particular improve (equipment or machinery)
example_sentence: The school received a grant to upgrade all the computers in the science laboratory.
academic_context: 기술/설비 — 기기 성능 및 시스템의 상향 조치

definition_en: to confirm or support (something which has been questioned); to maintain (a custom or practice)
example_sentence: The Supreme Court voted to uphold the constitutionality of the new healthcare law.
academic_context: 법/정치 — 법률적 판결 유지, 헌법 및 도덕률 수호

definition_en: to disappear suddenly and completely
example_sentence: Many species of amphibians are beginning to vanish as their natural habitats are destroyed.
academic_context: 생물/문학 — 종의 멸종, 갑작스러운 소멸 묘사

definition_en: any of the tubes forming part of the blood circulation system of the body, carrying in most cases oxygen-depleted blood toward the heart; a fracture in rock containing a deposit of minerals
example_sentence: Veins carry deoxygenated blood back to the heart, while arteries carry oxygenated blood away.
academic_context: 생물학 — 혈관계(Veins vs. Arteries), 지구과학 — 광맥

definition_en: a risky or daring journey or undertaking; to dare to do something or go somewhere that may be dangerous
example_sentence: The expedition was a dangerous venture into the uncharted territories of the Arctic.
academic_context: 역사/경제 — 탐험, 벤처 기업(위험을 감수하는 사업)

definition_en: at right angles to a horizontal plane; in a direction, or having an alignment, such that the top is directly above the bottom
example_sentence: On a standard coordinate graph, the y-axis represents the vertical line.
academic_context: 수학/기하학 — 수직선(y축), 종적인 방향성

definition_en: an oscillation of the parts of a fluid or an elastic solid whose equilibrium has been disturbed
example_sentence: Sound is produced by the mechanical vibration of an object traveling through a medium like air.
academic_context: 물리 — 음파, 파동의 발생 원인

definition_en: a person harmed, injured, or killed as a result of a crime, accident, or other event or action
example_sentence: The Red Cross provided food and shelter for the victims of the massive earthquake.
academic_context: 사회/역사 — 재난 및 전쟁의 사상자, 범죄 피해자
